question: Where can I find my IRD number?
answer:
An IRD number is an eight-or-nine digit number unique to you. You can find your IRD number on your payslip, on letters or statements from the
Inland Revenue
or on your KiwiSaver statement.
If you're an ASB customer you can find a record of your Tax Details, including your IRD number, current Resident Withholding Tax rate (RWT), and Prescribed Investor Rate (PIR) in the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic internet banking.
To find them in the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Personal profile'.
Scroll down and tap 'Tax details' to see your IRD number.
To find them in FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Personal profile’.
Scroll down and click 'Tax details' to see your IRD number.
You can alter your RWT rate and PIR yourself in FastNet Classic. You can also add your IRD number if ASB does not already have it.
If you need to update your IRD number, the best way to get in touch with us is by sending a message in FastNet Classic. Here's how:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Help and contact' in the bottom right hand corner of the page.
Under ‘Messages’, click 'Send a message'.
If you've forgotten your ASB login password, you can
reset your password
using the ASB Mobile app. Alternatively, you can visit any
ASB branch
.
Find out more information on RWT and what rate you should use.
Find out more information on PIR and what rate you should use.
For further information please contact the
Inland Revenue
.
If you’d like to 'store' your IRD number and other important information, such as your passport details and drivers licence, you can save them together in FastNet Classic in your own online vault which can be found under Documents in the menu.
For more ways to get help please
see our contact us page
.

question: How do I rename my accounts so they are personal to me?
answer:
You can personalise your bank account names via
FastNet Classic internet banking
and the
ASB Mobile Banking app
. For example, you could call your FastSaver 'Car Savings', or set up a dedicated 'Travel Fund'.
Here’s how:
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, find ‘Personalise’ and tap ‘Rename accounts’.
Tap the account you wish to rename and tap ‘Done’.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Personalise' on the top of the accounts.
Select 'Personalise account' to change an account image or name.
Select an account and make the changes.
Click 'Save'.
There are some accounts that can't be renamed.
Please note, your updated account names will not be displayed on your printed statements.

question: How can I update my contact details?
answer:
If your preferred name, email, contact number or address has changed, you can update your contact details either through the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic internet banking. Here’s how:
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the
ASB Mobile app
.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Personal profile’.
Select which details to update.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your
FastNet Classic
account
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Personal profile’.
Select which details to update.
Note:
If you want to change your name/identity, you'll need to come into a branch with relevant documentation to verify this.
To find out more, check out our
how-to guide on updating your contact details
.

question: How do I make an International Money Transfer (IMT)?
answer:
To make an International Money Transfer, log in to
FastNet Classic internet banking
and select Make a payment.  You will also need a
Netcode
.
Select International Transfers.
Follow the on-screen instructions to obtain a quote for the transaction and select New International Transfers Quote.
If you wish to continue, select Next.
Enter the SWIFT code for your recipient/beneficiary bank (the bank the funds are being sent to), or search for the recipient/beneficiary bank's
full details. You will need the following information about the overseas bank account you are transferring funds to: -
Recipient/beneficiary Name: Recipient’s full account name.
Recipient/beneficiary Address: Recipients physical address including city and country.
Recipient/beneficiary Account: Full account number or IBAN (International Bank Account Number). If an IBAN is used, it should be presented as a continuous string of alphanumeric characters with no spaces.
Recipient/beneficiary Bank: The recipient bank’s name, address and SWIFT Code (also known as a Bank Identifier Code/BIC).
Complete the transfer.
If the recipient's name is long and exceeds the character limit, continue typing into the first line of the address field, then continue typing the recipient's address after the recipient's name. This will ensure ASB can send all the required details with your payment to the recipient bank.
Please note
that
limits apply to International Money Transfers made via FastNet Classic
. When sending money overseas different countries may need different information from you. Find out more information on
country specific details
.
You can also visit our helpful
how to make an International Money Transfer guide
that will step you through the process.

question: What is an IBAN?
answer: IBAN stands for International Bank Account Number. IBAN's are commonly used across Europe and parts of the Middle East. If an IBAN is used, it should be presented as a continuous string of alphanumeric characters with no spaces. If someone is sending you money, and they request an IBAN for ASB, please provide your full ASB account number, eg 12 1234 1234567 12.

question: Can I easily transfer money between my accounts with FastNet Classic?
answer:
Yes, to transfer money between accounts in Fastnet Classic internet banking:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Under Accounts, select Transfers.
Select the account you wish to make a transfer from and the account it is going to. The order of your accounts will match your order on the Balances page.
Enter the amount you would like to transfer and a reference (optional).
Click the ‘Make Transfer’ button. The transfer will be processed immediately*.
If you are transferring out of an ASB Credit Card or Savings Plus Monthly account or into a KiwiSaver account or ASB Investment Fund, you will need to confirm you are aware of the applicable fees/ reward interest impact and/ or reversal limitations, before you can continue with the transfer.
If you wish to make more than one transfer, click the ‘Make another Transfer’ button on the success page.
* Note, it can take 3-5 working days for the transaction to be visible in your ASB Investment Fund or ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account. Once the transfer is accepted, the unit price that applies, will be the price for the date that the transaction is accepted and processed.

question: How do I adjust my credit card limit?
answer:
To do this, you’ll need to make an appointment by calling us on 0800 803 804 or +64 9 306 3000 if overseas. We’re available Monday to Friday, 8:00am to 8:00pm, and on weekends or public holidays from 8:30am to 5:00pm. Or by visiting any
ASB branch
.
There are a few things to consider before applying to adjust your credit card limit.
Approval is subject to ASB lending criteria.
If you wish to reduce your current limit, you can reduce it to the minimum limit for your type of credit card. However you are unable to have a limit lower than your balance.
If you decrease your limit, you may not be able to increase it again.

question: What do I do if I can't send an International Money Transfer (IMT) via FastNet Classic because it exceeds the daily transaction limit?
answer:
If you exceed the
daily transaction limit
for an International Money Transfer via FastNet Classic please visit an ASB branch for assistance. You can also complete an International Money Transfer by submitting this
form
via email to an ASB branch. We’ll contact you during New Zealand business hours to complete your request so please ensure your
contact details held with us are up to date
.

question: I've noticed the expression Recipient to Pay, in relation to International Money Transfers - what does this mean?
answer:
The Recipient to Pay option refers to Other Bank Fees. If you select the Recipient to Pay option, you will not be charged an Other Bank Fee, but there will likely be fees deducted from the amount that your recipient receives.
Alternatively, you can choose the “Sender to Pay” option. An Other Bank Fee is charged when you choose the “Sender to Pay” option. This fee covers charges from other banks (also known as correspondent, intermediary and beneficiary banks) involved in the payment. This fee will be charged to your ASB account the business day after the payment is sent.
Please note
, while ASB will send your payment with instructions for the sender to cover all fees and charges, due to the steps involved in the international payment ecosystem, it is possible that the recipient may still have fees deducted from the amount that they receive.
If the debit account is a Foreign Currency Account, the fee will be converted to the currency of the debit account at the applicable foreign exchange rate at the time the fee is processed.
Our current fees are published in our
Guide to fees
, and our
Guide to business banking fees
.
You can also find exchange rates, fees, and other IMT informaton on our
International money transfers page
.

question: I've noticed the expression Sender to Pay, in relation to International Money Transfers - what does this mean?
answer:
The “Sender to Pay” option refers to Other Bank Fees. An Other Bank Fee is charged when you choose the “Sender to Pay” option. This fee covers charges from other banks (also known as correspondent, intermediary and beneficiary banks) involved in the payment. This fee will be charged to your ASB account the business day after the payment is sent. Please note, while ASB will send your payment with instructions for the sender to cover all fees and charges, due to the steps involved in the international payment ecosystem, it is possible that the recipient may still have fees deducted from the amount that they receive.
If the debit account is a Foreign Currency Account, the fee will be converted to the currency of the debit account at the applicable foreign exchange rate at the time the fee is processed.
Our current fees are published in our
Guide to fees
, and our
Guide to business banking fees
.
You can also find exchange rates, fees, and other IMT informaton on our
International money transfers page
.
Alternatively, you can choose the “Recipient to Pay” option. In this case, you will not be charged an Other Bank Fee, but there will likely be fees deducted from the amount that your recipient receives.

question: How do I register for Netcode?
answer:
Registering for Netcode for free* by:
Calling us on 0800 FASTNET (
0800 327 863
) or + 64 9 306 3000 (from overseas) and select
option 2
. We’ll ask you some security questions to establish it’s really you, but we’ll never ask you for your
FastNet Classic internet banking
password or your PIN number.
Alternatively visit any
ASB branch
and bring valid photo ID.
Choose how you want to receive your codes:
By text*
: We’ll send your Netcode as a text message to your mobile. Once your registration is complete you’ll receive them straight away.
On a token:
We’ll post your token out to you. When it arrives, call 0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863) to activate it.
What is Netcode?
*Netcode via SMS is free to use if you have a New Zealand mobile phone number, but please check with your provider in case there are charges to receive international text messages. Since we rely on international phone providers to send our Netcode SMS to international phone numbers, we cannot guarantee you’ll be able to receive Netcode SMS to your international phone number.

question: What is a SWIFT Code? Why do I need it?
answer:
A SWIFT code is a unique international ID code used to identify a particular bank.  You might also see it referred to as a Bank Identifier Code (BIC).
SWIFT stands for 'Society of Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunications'.
To ensure correct details are used it can be helpful to know the SWIFT code of the beneficiary bank, (ie the bank you are sending money to), when completing your International Money Transfer in
FastNet Classic
.
The ASB SWIFT Code/BIC is ASBBNZ2A.

question: Where can I spend my True Rewards dollars?
answer:
You can spend your True Rewards dollars with our partner stores or at the True Rewards online store. You can also contribute them to your ASB KiwiSaver Fund, or donate them to a partner charity.
Find out more at the
ASB True Rewards online store
.

question: Are there any daily limits to an International Money Transfer (IMT), using FastNet Classic?
answer:
Yes, the following limits apply:
When transferring from a New Zealand Dollar (NZD) account.
During business hours
(Monday – Friday 8am – 5pm New Zealand time) -
$100,000
(including $5 fee).
After business hours
(Monday – Friday before 8am and after 5pm and all day Saturday & Sunday New Zealand time) -
$60,000
(including $5 fee).
When transferring from a Foreign Currency Account (FCA).
During business hours
(Monday – Friday 8am – 5pm New Zealand time) – equivalent to NZD
$100,000
(including $5 fee) in a 24 hour period.
After business hours
(Monday – Friday before 8am and after 5pm and all day Saturday & Sunday New Zealand time) - equivalent to NZD
$60,000
(including $5 fee), in a 24 hour period, this limit includes any transfer you make between your foreign currency account and your other accounts of any type.

question: How do I set up Xero account feeds in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Our account feeds can send your ASB transactions to Xero every day, automatically. If you use
FastNet Classic internet banking
, here's how to set them up online. (If you use FastNet Business,
follow these instructions
instead. We also provide
transaction feeds for MYOB
.)
Before you start
The first step is to set up your ASB accounts in Xero. Then you'll be able to choose them as the destination for your daily transactions.
It's easy to do. For details, see
Xero's help article on setting up new accounts
.
Activating your feeds
Once you've logged in to FastNet Classic:
1. Activate the drop down menu under your name at the top right corner.
2. Select 'Accounting Set Up' from the drop down menu.
3. Choose Xero.
FastNet Classic shows a list of all your ASB accounts and cards that support transaction feeds.
4. Tick the ones you want to feed to Xero. There's no cost for adding accounts, and you can turn off your feeds at any time.
5. You can back date your first account feed. The default is 1 April, which for most businesses is the start of the financial year, and means you can get the full financial year of details.
6. When you've made your selections, confirm you're happy for us to send your information to Xero, and click OK.
You'll then be taken to a Xero authentication screen. This lets Xero confirm your identity before adding account feeds, and lets you link your ASB accounts to the right Xero accounts.
Log in with your Xero details.
Allocate your new ASB transaction feeds to your ASB accounts in Xero.
Click OK, and you're done.
Your first transaction feed will appear in Xero the next morning. Depending on how far back you backdated your feeds, your first report may include a lot of transactions.
Problems?
If you've run into any issues on the ASB side, just give our FastNet Classic help desk a call on 0800 327 863. If you have any Xero issues, it's best to contact them directly via
their support page
.

question: What do I do if I’ve sent a payment to a closed or incorrect New Zealand bank account?
answer:
We've introduced
Confirmation of Payee
to check the account owners name and number match up, giving you greater confidence that you're paying the right person.
If you do make a payment to a closed New Zealand bank account it will be returned by the receiving bank within a few business days.
If you make a payment to an incorrect account, you will need to contact the payee and request the money is returned.
If you don't know who the payee is, get in touch with us and we will advise you on what to do.
The best way to get in touch with us is by
sending a message in FastNet Classic internet banking
and one of our team will try to reply within the same business day. You'll need to let us know what date you made the payment, the amount, and the account number the payment was sent to. If you've forgotten your ASB login password, you can
reset your password
using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Visit any
ASB branch
.
We can send a request to recover the funds to the bank you paid. This is dependent on the recipient agreeing to return the funds and there is no guarantee that the recipient will agree to returning the funds.
Please note
, there is a $40 fee for this service (Direct Credit Recovery). Please take care when making a One–Off payment, and make sure when you set up a self-selected Bill Payee, that you have the correct bank details for them.
For more ways to get help please
see our contact us page
.

question: What are ASB's password security requirements?
answer:
To help guide your choices when selecting a new password for internet banking and Online Share Trading we’ve included a strength meter on the Change Password page in FastNet Classic internet banking.
You can choose a case sensitive password up to 100 characters long, with the usual special characters (such as $,*,@ and ! and %). You can also put spaces in if you wanted to use a phrase as your password.
Your password must:
contain at least 2 letters
contain at least 2 numbers or special characters, e.g. 2$, 79, or @#
be at least 8 characters
not repeat the same character more than 3 times consecutively
not be the word “Password” including versions with special characters, e.g. p@$$w0rd
not be your username
not be your previous 5 passwords
not contain non-ASCII characters
To guide your choice, valid passwords will be rated as Basic, Better or Best. Try different combinations to increase the strength rating.
Remember, never give your password to anyone.  ASB will never ask you by email for your secure banking information or ask you to log in to your account from an email.

question: How much does an International Money Transfer cost?
answer:
The cost of an International Money Transfer (IMT) is made up in part through the exchange rate, and in part through fees.
View our current exchange rates
.
Our current fees are published in our
Guide to fees
, and our
Guide to business banking fees
.
You can also find exchange rates, fees, and other IMT informaton on our
International money transfers page
.

question: Can I open new accounts or apply for new products online in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Within FastNet Classic internet banking you can open and apply for many ASB products and services, including:
Savings, transaction, business, and foreign currency accounts.
Term Deposit or Term Fund accounts
New Credit cards
Visa Debit cards
True Rewards
Personal Loans
Debt Consolidation Loans
A Share trading account with ASB Securities
Insurance
The ASB KiwiSaver Scheme
An ASB Cash Fund Account
To start an application, choose your selection from the Apply for menu.
You can also register for digital services like alerts via email and/or notifications.
How much does it cost to apply online?
Nothing. This service is free.
How long before I get an answer to my application?
Our response time to you will depend on the type of service you apply for. Some, like opening a savings account, are available straight away.

question: I'm an existing ASB customer, how do I apply for a new account?
answer:
If you're an existing ASB customer, you can use either the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic internet banking to apply for a new account by:
ASB Mobile app:
Logging in to ASB Mobile app.
Tapping ‘More’ on bottom right of screen.
Selectig ‘Open and apply’.
Choosing the type of account you want to open e.g. Transaction accounts, Savings and term deposits or Business accounts and follow the instructions to apply.
FastNet Classic:
Logging in to FastNet Classic.
Opening ‘Menu’ in the top left of the screen, then 'Open and apply'.
Clicking 'Open an account' in the 'Bank accounts' section.
Selecting the account you wish to open and follow the instructions to apply.

question: Am I eligible for an ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
If you are 18 or over and a NZ citizen, permanent resident or have a work visa, you can apply to borrow any amount over $2,000.
ASB’s lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: Does ASB support Account2Account?
answer:
We take the security and protection of our customer’s internet banking experience and information very seriously.
Using Account2Account’s payment system requires users to input their username and password to a third party which breaches
ASB FastNet Classic’s Terms and Conditions
.
We have never endorsed the Account2Account service, as we are unable to audit their security.
To protect our customers from fraudulent transactions, we may ask users to complete two-step verification if we notice something unusual about their Account2Account login, or they have not used these third-party payment products in a period of time.
Two step-verification can be completed using one of the three available channels: ASB Mobile Banking app, text message, or physical token.
Learn more about two-step verification
.
Alternatively, merchants may offer other methods of payment for you to use such as credit card,
Visa Debit
or
Online EFTPOS
.

question: How do I request a tax certificate for my tax return?
answer:
We'll generate an annual tax certificate at the end of the tax year (i.e. 31 March) where you have earned more than NZ$20 in interest, and Resident Withholding Tax has been deducted. This document will be available from around mid April. We’ll also send you a final certificate if you close an account which has earned interest.
If you need a tax certificate at any other time you can obtain it through the
IRD website
or their app 'MyIR'.
Otherwise, call us on
0800 803 804
. Or if you are calling from overseas please call
+ 649 306 3000
.

question: Can I get a personal loan if I’m not an ASB customer?
answer:
If you're not an existing customer you can learn how to
join ASB here
.
Once you have set up a transaction account with us, you can
apply online
. Our team will be in contact within the next week to talk about your application.
To complete your loan, you will need to provide photo ID, verification of your address (such as a utility or rates bill) and your IRD number.

question: Can I set up an Automatic Payment via FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app to pay my bills or credit card?
answer:
Yes you can. If you wish to make on-going payments for the same amount and at regular time, doing this using an automatic payment will mean you can 'set and forget'. Just make sure you've got enough money in your account to cover the payment when they're due.
You can create, view and edit Automatic Payments via
FastNet Classic internet banking
and the
ASB Mobile Banking app
.
To set up an Automatic Payment using FastNet Classic:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Select 'Make a payment'.
Select 'Automatic payment'.
Select the account you’d like payments to come from and select 'New Automatic Payment'. If you want to alter an existing payment, select ‘View/Alter/Delete’.
Payments can be made to any bank account in New Zealand. All you need is the payee's account number and name.
If you’re setting up an automatic payment to pay towards your ASB Visa credit card, please use the following information for payment details:
Recipient (Payee) Name: ASB Credit Card
Recipient (Payee) Account Number: 12-3116-0987656-20
Particulars: The first 12 digits of the credit card number
Code: The last 4 digits of the credit card number
Reference: Surname + Initials
To set up an Automatic Payment using the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
From the menu, tap 'Payments'.
Tap 'Make a Payment'.
Tap 'Choose who to pay', then 'Pay someone new'.
Enter the payee details, then select 'Check payee details'.
We'll perform a
Confirmation of Payee
check, show a response and then you can choose whether to go ahead with the payment or to edit the payee details.
Select 'Save this payee' so you have the account details if you need to pay them again in the future.
Enter the amount, the frequency of the payments and any reference details if required then tap 'Pay'.
If you're setting up an automatic payment to pay towards your ASB Visa credit card, please use the following information for payment details:
Account Number - 12-3116-0987656-20
New payee – ASB Credit Card
Their reference details:
Particulars: The first 12 digits of the credit card number
Code: The last 4 digits of the credit card number
Reference: Surname + Initials
Please note, if you’re setting up an Automatic Payment to pay for your ASB Visa credit card, payments can take up to two working days to appear. To avoid getting charged a late payment fee, set up your Automatic Payment to commence several days before the normal payment due date, and make sure it covers at least the minimum payment due each month too.

question: What proof of address do I need to open a new account?
answer:
If you are a new customer to ASB, you’ll need to provide proof of your physical address, which is current and original. This is a legal requirement to protect you and ASB. If you are opening a personal account for yourself, select the appropriate guide below:
Identification Requirements – Adult
Identification Requirements – Kids
If you are an entity, we need proof of the physical and registered address of the entity as well as the individuals associated with it. Select the appropriate guide below for further information on entity identification requirements.
Identification Requirements – Limited Company
Identification Requirements – Trust
Identification Requirements – Incorporated Society
Identification Requirements – Unincorporated Society
Identification Requirements – Partnership
Identification Requirements – Limited Partnership
If you are already an ASB customer and are registered for internet banking, you can open an account in FastNet Classic internet banking without providing further proof of address. If you’re not already registered, and would like to be,
follow our handy guide
.

question: Can I send an International Money Transfer from my ASB account if I'm overseas?
answer:
Yes. The easiest way to send an International Money Transfer from your ASB account is with
FastNet Classic internet banking.
You'll need
Netcode
to complete the transaction which you can obtain by using either:
A Netcode token (this is the most reliable method as it avoids any mobile coverage issues. There is a charge of $1 per month to use it.)
A mobile phone with an NZ registered mobile number. Just note that this is dependent on your mobile phone coverage; if it’s poor you may not receive the Netcode TXT you need to complete your International Money Transfer transaction.
A mobile phone with an internationally registered mobile number. Please note that Netcode via SMS is free to use if you have a New Zealand mobile phone number, but please check with your provider in case there are charges to receive international text messages. Since we rely on international phone providers to send our Netcode SMS to international phone numbers, we cannot guarantee you’ll be able to receive Netcode SMS to your international phone number.
If you are not yet registered for FastNet Classic or Netcode, please call us on
0800 803 804,
or if you are calling from overseas
+ 64 9 306 3000.
You can also complete an International Money Transfer by
submitting a form
via fax or email to an ASB branch. We will contact you during New Zealand business hours to complete your request so please ensure your
contact details held with us are up to date
.

question: Can I set up one-off payments with FastNet Classic internet banking to pay my bills?
answer:
Yes, you can. All you need is the account number of the person you want to pay.
Here's how to do this:
Log In to FastNet Classic.
Click on 'Make a payment'.
Select One-off payments.
Choose the account to make the payment from, then click the 'To' box and select 'Pay someone new'.
Enter the payee details, then select 'Check payee details'.
We'll perform a
Confirmation of Payee
check, show a response and then you can choose whether to go ahead with the payment or to edit the payee details.
Select 'Save this payee' so you have the account details if you need to pay them again in the future.
If you continue, enter the details to appear on your payee’s statement and on your statement.
Select 'Pay'.
We'll ask you if you are sure you want to make the payment. If you wish to continue with the payment, please check the details as they appear and select 'Yes, make payment'.
If you are sending a one-off payment to an ASB account, the payment will be made straight away - the recipient doesn't have to physically deposit the money into their account, so there's no waiting around while funds clear. Payments to other banks will be made on the following day at the latest.*
* Payments made after 10pm to non-ASB accounts will be deposited on the following calendar day.
Will I be charged for one-off payments?
You’ll only be charged the standard self-service transaction fee for each one-off payment you make. The self-service transaction fee varies for each account type. Find out more information on
account fees
.
Can I post-date one–off payments?
No, sorry. One–Off payments can’t be scheduled for the future, they are designed to go immediately.  However, you can post date Bill Payments for up to 12 months in advance.

question: How will I know if I've made a mistake while using FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
We've designed FastNet Classic to be user-friendly. If you make a mistake while using FastNet Classic, a message will appear telling you exactly what it is that you're doing wrong. FastNet Classic also lets you check any payment you're making for a final approval.
We've introduced
Confirmation of Payee
to check the account owners name and number match up, giving you greater confidence that you're paying the right person.

question: Can I print out my statements from FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Bank statements are one of the most requested documents and can be used as proof of address or when you need to show your incomings and outgoings. With many third parties accepting these digitally, there isn't always a need to print and certify these at your local branch. However if needed, you can print out statements on FastNet Classic internet banking using your print function.
Open up the account you want the statement for.
Click 'Print' towards the top right of your transaction list.
One the print summary screen, click 'Print' in the top right.
The Document Centre in FastNet Classic also stores PDF files of your statement, just like the paper statement you may receive in your letterbox.
Why do my statements appear over more than one page?
Statements are viewed in pages. You can alter the number of transactions you see on a page by using the 'Transactions per Page' drop down box in the Statement Account Selection Area. You can display anything from 10 to 250 transactions per page.
If your statement includes more transactions than the amount you have selected for the time period you have selected, then a 'Next' button will appear; simply click this button to view the next page or click the 'Previous' button to move back one page.

question: I don't want my bank statements in the mail. Can I choose to stop receiving them?
answer:
Yes you can. You can choose to stop receiving statements by mail for any ASB transaction or savings account. You will instead receive these statements online. To stop receiving statements in the mail:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
On the right-hand side, select 'Documents'.
When the Document Centre page opens, select 'Statement Stopper'.
Select the Online Statement box for any statements you’d like to receive online and not in the mail.
If you decide you’d like to start receiving your statements in the mail again, you can simply deselect the online statement box.
Document Centre is a central place to view and download your paper statements in PDF. You can even choose to receive an email or TXT message when a new statement arrives.
What if I need statements for tax purposes?
If you have investments or own a small business and require statements for tax purposes, you have three choices:
Stop statements and download them in PDF format on a regular basis from FastNet Classic Document Centre.
Stop statements and order duplicates when you need them.
Continue to receive mailed paper statements.

question: How do I join True Rewards?
answer:
When you apply for an ASB Visa Rewards, ASB Visa Platinum Rewards credit card, you'll automatically be enrolled for True Rewards.
Transferring from ASB Visa Light.
If you have an ASB Visa Light credit card and wish to join True Rewards, you can do this by transferring to an ASB Visa Rewards or ASB Visa Platinum Rewards credit card.
You can change your credit card type in FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app.
To apply to change your credit card via FastNet Classic:
Log into FastNet Classic.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Under ‘Cards’ select ‘Change card type’.
Fill in the form.
To apply to change your credit card via the ASB Mobile app:
Log into the ASB Mobile app.
Tap 'More' at the bottom right of the accounts screen.
Tap ‘Open & Apply'.
Find ‘Change Card Type’ then tap 'Apply online'.
Fill in the Change Card Type form.
Once you have received and successfully added a PIN on your new ASB Visa credit card, any balance on your previous card will be transferred automatically to your new credit card, and your old credit card account will be closed overnight. If you have a joint or additional card on this account, the first cardholder that successfully completes the PIN activation process will close your old credit card account.
If the new card is not pinned within 25 days, ASB will automatically transfer any balance and close the old credit card account. Remember you will need to update any providers that you pay regularly (like bill payments) with your new card details. To view transactions on your old credit card account, use the 'Search and Export' feature in FastNet Classic.
Alternatively you can call our contact centre on 0800 255 382.
ASB Visa Business Rewards.
When you apply for an ASB Visa Business Rewards card, you'll automatically be enrolled for True Rewards, so you can start earning True Rewards Dollars right away.

question: What proof of ID do I need to open a new account?
answer:
If you are a new customer to ASB, you’ll need to provide proof of your identity and residential address. This is a legal requirement to protect you and ASB.
The information you supply should be current and original. You can often provide proof of ID and address online using the ASB ID app. We can also accept copies certified by a trusted referee if required.
Documentation in a foreign language must be accompanied by an independent and certified English translation provided to you by a professional translator.
For any additional queries, call our Contact Centre on
0800 803 804
, vist your nearest
ASB branch
or contact your ASB Relationship Manager.
If you are opening a personal account, we have different guides depending on who is opening and operating the account.  Select the appropriate guide below:
If you are opening a personal account for yourself and are 13 or older:
Identification Requirements – Individual
If you are a parent/guardian opening a personal account for someone under 18 years of age:
Identification Requirements – Parent and child
If you are opening a business account, depending on the entity type that requires the account, particular information is required. Select the appropriate guide below:
Identification Requirements – Limited Company
Identification Requirements – Trust
Identification Requirements – Incorporated Society
Identification Requirements – Unincorporated Society
Identification Requirements – Partnership
Identification Requirements – Limited Partnership
If you are already an ASB customer and are registered for internet banking, you can
open an account in FastNet Classic
without providing further ID. If you’re not already registered, and would like to be, see our guide on
joining Fastnet Classic
.

question: How far back can I go to see my statements in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
You can see your credit card and bank statements back to January 2015.
To view your statements:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
On the right-hand side, select 'Documents'.
When the document centre opens, choose the time period you need.
Then select 'Statements' in Document types.
Your statements are in .pdf format, so are easy to download if you need to.

question: How do I know if my International Money Transfer (IMT) has been received by the beneficiary bank?
answer:
We will transfer the funds the same day if the instruction is received by ASB before 3.00pm on a business day (Monday to Friday excluding public holidays), otherwise it will be sent the next business day.
However, how quickly the payment reaches the beneficiary is determined by how quickly the beneficiary’s bank (and any intermediary banks) process the payment instruction and unfortunately this is beyond ASB’s control.
If you are concerned that the payment has been unduly delayed, you can request for the funds to be traced by ASB (note, this will usually involve a cost). If you wish to do this, please call the ASB contact centre on
0800 803 804
or visit your nearest branch.

question: Can I transfer an amount from my account which is greater than my FastNet Classic or ASB Mobile app daily limit?
answer:
For payments made through FastNet Classic internet banking and the ASB Mobile app, the daily limit excludes transfers between your own ASB accounts, to most Registered payees (e.g. Spark / One NZ) or to bill payees loaded for you by an ASB staff member.  For the payments excluded from the limit you can make them for any amount you wish.
If you are making a transfer involving a Foreign Currency account, the daily limit does not apply during processing hours (9am to 4pm NZT, Mon-Fri). However, if you make a transfer involving a Foreign Currency account outside of processing hours, or on a currency holiday, an after-hours daily limit of $100k will apply.
Please note
, the daily limit applies to all International Money Transfers, both during and outside processing hours.
A full list of
currency holidays
is available.
In order for you to make a transfer that exceeds your daily limit, please contact us on
0800 803 804
, or if you are calling from overseas
+ 64 9 306 3000
, and we can assist you. If you’re an organisation needing to make payments greater than $100,000,
find out more about FastNet Business
.

question: How do I redeem my True Rewards dollars online?
answer:
Here’s how to redeem your True Rewards dollars for rewards online:
Log in to FastNet Classic online banking and select True Rewards under the Accounts menu.
To redeem your True Rewards for merchandise, vouchers or charity donations, select True Rewards online store and select your choice from there.
Some of our True Rewards partners accept payment online too. Explore more on the
True Rewards website
.

question: How do I change my FastNet Classic password online from within FastNet Classic?
answer:
To change your FastNet Classic internet banking password online you’ll need to know your current password and username, as this is done from within your FastNet Classic account.
To change your password from within FastNet Classic:
Log in to FastNet Classic using your existing password.
Select your Profile at the top right of the page.
Select Change Password from the drop down menu.
Follow the on screen instructions and select Change Password.
You can choose a case sensitive password up to 100 characters long, with the usual special characters such as $,*,@ and ! and %. To help guide your choices when selecting a new password we’ve included a strength meter on the Change Password screen.
How do I reset or change my ASB login password using ASB Banking mobile app?

question: How do I view, change or delete a bill payee in FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
You can change the particulars, code or reference for a bill payee in FastNet Classic. To change the account you pay from, the payee name or payee account number, you'll need to delete the bill payee via FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app and create a new payee with the updated details.
To view or change the statements details (particulars, code or reference) via FastNet Classic:
Bill payments
Log into FastNet Classic.
Select the 'Make a payment' button.
Select 'Bill payment'.
Choose the bank account which your payee is associated with.
Then select the 'View/Edit/Delete' button.
Choose the payee name you wish to view or alter.
Update the details of your payee and then select ‘Edit'.
Complete the change by selecting the 'Yes' button when asked 'Are you sure you want to do this?'.
One-off payments
Log into FastNet Classic.
Select the 'Make a payment' button.
Select 'One-off payments'.
Search and select the payee you wish to view or alter in the 'To' field.
Edit the details of your payee, following the instructions provided.
Choose whether to save the details for future payments.
Confirm details in the 'One-off payments' and select 'Pay' to continue.
Complete the change by selecting 'Yes, confirm' when you are sure you have provided the right details.
You can also change the details of a self-selected payee as you are making a one-off payment. The payment details you provide will be used for the payment you are making at the time.
To delete a bill payee via FastNet Classic:
Log into FastNet Classic.
Select the 'Make a payment' button.
Select 'Bill payment'.
Choose the bank account you wish to delete the bill payment from.
Then select the 'View/Edit/Delete' button.
Select the Payee Name you wish to delete.
Then select the 'Delete' button.
Delete the payee by selecting the 'Yes' button when asked 'Are you sure you want to do this?'.
To delete a bill payee via the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap 'Payments' at the bottom of the screen.
Tap 'Make a payment'.
Tap the 'Payee' box, then find the payee you wish to delete.
On iOS, swipe left on the payee and select delete or on Android, long-press the payee then tap the bin icon in the top right of the screen.

question: How do I set up my own payees in FastNet Classic?
answer:
We call these self-selected payees. Here's how to set them up for payment:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Open 'Payments' in the menu.
Select ‘Bill payment’.
Select an account to work with from your account list.
Select ‘New Bill Payment Payee’.
Select ‘Add Self Selected Payee’.
Enter the payee’s name and account number, the details to appear on your bill payee's statement and on your statement.
Select ‘Submit’.
Once the bill payee has been set up, you can choose which of your accounts you wish to pay from, the amount you wish to pay and when you want to pay, up to 12 months in advance (excluding non-working days).
You can also set up self-selected payees when you are making a one-off payment. Enter the payee account number and then select 'Add a new payee'. You will then have this new payee available the next time you make a payment.
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Select 'Make a payment'.
Select 'One-off payment'.
Enter your payee’s account number in the ‘To’ field and select 'Add a new payee’.
Enter the payee’s name.
Select ‘Add payee’.
Enter the particulars, code and reference details to appear on your payee's statement and on your statement.
Select ‘Pay’.
Complete the payee set up and payment by selecting 'Yes, confirm' when you are sure you have provided the right details.
Your payee will be saved and your payment will be made at the same time.

question: Why can't I change my ASB credit card or overdraft limits online anymore?
answer:
From the 22nd November 2021, the ability to adjust your credit card or arranged overdraft limits up or down online will be removed completely. You will no longer be able to increase your limit online without a full application.
To add, remove or change a limit you will need to get in contact with us. If you want a lower limit, just let us know. We’ll reduce it to a limit that suits your needs. Or, if you want a higher limit, we’ll start an application process to ensure you receive the right limit for your financial situation.
There are a few things you should know about overdrafts:
Approval is subject to ASB lending criteria.
An overdraft works as a line of credit, meaning it's available for you to use, as and when you need it, up to a set limit.
The current
unsecured interest rate
applies to any overdrawn balance up to your approved overdraft limit.
If your approved limit is exceeded, then default interest at our
unarranged overdraft interest rate
will be charged on that amount.
If there is no arranged overdraft in place, then interest at our
unarranged overdraft interest rate
will be charged on any overdrawn balance on your account. If your account is in excess of $20.00, you will also be charged our
monthly unarranged overdraft facility fee
of $3.00.
There are a few things you should know about Credit cards:
Approval is subject to ASB lending criteria.
A credit card works as a line of credit, meaning it's available for you to use, as and when you need it, up to a set limit.
If you wish to reduce your current limit, you can reduce it to the minimum limit for your type of credit card however you are unable to have a limit lower than your balance.

question: Can I set up Bill Payments with FastNet Classic to pay my regular bills?
answer:
Yes, a FastNet Classic internet banking Bill Payment is ideal if you are regularly paying a person or company, at different times and for varying amounts. There are two types of Bill Payees you can set up:
Self-selected Bill Payments
- Self-selected Bill Payments
are for people such as your lawn mower contractor or your Doctor. These are payees that you set up yourself in FastNet Classic; you’ll need their account details and their name handy when you set them up.
Registered Payees
- FastNet Classic also offers you a directory of companies that have registered their payment details with us. We call these Registered payees. They are companies such as Vodafone, Mercury Energy, Local Authorities (for rates, water, etc.), Insurance Companies, and many others.
Details for these payees are pre-loaded into FastNet Classic Bill Payments and you can easily search for them. Registered payees have already provided us with their account details and rules for completing information fields. These payees are responsible for keeping the account details up to date; you do not need to check them.
Find out more about
how to add a bill payee
.

question: Am I eligible for an ASB Personal Loan if I am buying a car privately?
answer:
Yes, provided we approve your loan. The minimum amount is $2000 for an ASB Personal Loan.
ASB’s lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: Could someone else access information in my Online Vault?
answer:
The Online Vault is very secure as it can only be accessed by logging in to your
FastNet Classic internet banking.
To find your Online Vault:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
On the right hand side of the screen, select 'Documents'.
Scroll to the bottom of the page and select 'Online Vault'.
What you can store:
Personal Information (Passport details, drivers licence and much more)
Bank Details (Accounts, credit cards etc.)
Important Contacts (Next of kin, accountant, solicitor or any other contacts you like)
Medical Details (Medical conditions, allergies, vaccination history and doctor details)
Insurance Details (Insurance contacts and insurance policies)
And more…
This information is only visible to you, and will not be used by ASB for any purpose without your consent. Remember, it is important that you never disclose passwords to anyone.
We recommend that you do not store your passwords in the Online Vault. If someone gains access to your FastNet Classic without you knowing, then there is a risk that all other accounts with passwords stored in Online Vault could be compromised.
We recommend that you activate
Netcode at login
as an extra layer of protection for your personal information, in case your password is compromised.
Learn more about Netcode.
How do I register for Netcode?

question: How do I set up a Bill Payment to a Registered payee?
answer:
What is a Registered Payee?
Registered payees have already provided us with their account details and rules for completing information fields. These payees are responsible for keeping the account details up to date; you do not need to check them.
To set up a payment to a bill payee who has already registered their details with ASB:
Log in to FastNet Classic Internet Banking.
Select 'Make a payment'.
Select 'Bill payments'.
From the drop down menu, select the account you wish the bill payment to come from.
Select New Bill Payment Payee and enter the name of the company you wish to pay in the search field on the New Payee screen.
Select Search.
A list of companies that match your search will be displayed.
If your payee company is listed, click on the name.
Their details will be displayed on the New Payee screen.
What information do I need to put in the Particulars, Code and Reference Fields?
For Registered payees, you will probably need to enter specific information in the Particulars, Code and Reference fields.
Hover your mouse over the field, and a box will appear explaining what you need to enter (e.g. ‘your account number, found in the top right hand corner of your statement’).
You may not always have to enter information into all three fields, this depends on the Registered payees' requirements.
Once complete, enter the details to appear on your statement, so you know what the payment is for.
Enter your FastNet Classic Password and Select Submit.

question: How do I make additional payments on my ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
For FastNet Classic online banking users, follow these steps:
Log In to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Select the loan that you want to make an extra payment to.
If the loan is eligible the 'Make an Extra Payment' button will display in the page.
Click on the ‘Make an Extra Payment’ button and follow the instructions.
You can also make extra payments from your ASB Mobile Banking App, here’s how.
Log In to the ASB Mobile Banking App.
Select your loan.
Select 'Make extra payment' then follow the instructions.
If you are not a FastNet Classic or ASB Mobile App user you can also make additional payments by calling us on 0800 803 804, or by visiting your
nearest ASB branch
.

question: How do I pay for my new car?
answer:
If your ASB car loan is unsecured, you can make your own payment arrangement directly with the seller, typically via an electronic transfer to their account.
If your ASB car loan is secured you will need to provide us evidence of the seller's account details prior to advance, for example a copy of their bank statement.
ASB's lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: Can I borrow money for a car that has not been registered?
answer:
Yes you can, provided we approve your loan application. However, if your loan is secured the car will need to be registered before we are able to transfer the funds to the seller, for the purchase price.
ASB's lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: What is Netcode and why do I need it?
answer:
Netcode is a
two-step verification
method that's an extra layer of security to help us make sure it's really you when completing certain banking activities on your ASB account, and it's free*.
We text the Netcode to your mobile phone or send it to you on a portable Netcode token. You'll need it to:
Register to use the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Make a payment that will take you over the $1,000 daily payment threshold (or a lower amount as chosen by you).
Complete an online transaction with your Visa credit or debit card, depending on the risk of the transaction.
Change your card PIN.
Add Google Pay or Apple Pay to your cards via the ASB Mobile Banking app or from the Google or Apple digital wallets.
Make International Money Transfers (FastNet Classic internet banking only).
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking (if you have
Netcode at login
turned on).
To register for Netcode, call us on 0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863) or +64 9 306 3185.
Find out more about
registering for Netcode
.
More about Netcode:
I haven't received my Netcode. What now?
Will I need to enter a Netcode for all my payments?
Can I use Netcode whilst I'm overseas?
*Netcode via SMS is free to use if you have a New Zealand mobile phone number, but please check with your provider in case there are charges to receive international text messages. Since we rely on international phone providers to send our Netcode SMS to international phone numbers, we cannot guarantee you’ll be able to receive Netcode SMS to your international phone number.

question: Does it cost to have a Netcode token?
answer:
If you are using FastNet Classic Internet Banking,
two-step verification
using the ASB Mobile Banking app, a Netcode token or Netcodes sent by text message is free.
If you are using FastNet Business, you will be provided with two free Netcode tokens per FastNet Business site.  Additional tokens are at a cost of $20 each.
A replacement Netcode token fee of $20.00 is charged if you lose or damage your token and require a new one.
Please note: Netcode via SMS is free to use if you have a New Zealand mobile phone number, but please check with your provider in case there are charges to receive international text messages. Since we rely on international phone providers to send our Netcode SMS to international phone numbers, we cannot guarantee you’ll be able to receive Netcode SMS to your international phone number.

question: Can I use Netcode whilst I'm overseas?
answer:
You can continue receiving your
Netcode
via the token while you're overseas.
If you usually receive Netcode by text, you should be able to receive your Netcode if you have roaming turned on, however we cannot guarantee the delivery of your Netcode text overseas.
Alternatively, you can use the
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your Netcode-verified device to complete payments without Netcode (except International Money Transfers).
You can also use the ASB Mobile Banking app to complete two-step verification when logging in to FastNet Classic.
Find out more about
two-step verification using the ASB Mobile Banking app
.
I haven't received my Netcode. What now?

question: I haven’t received my Netcode. What now?
answer:
There are a few reasons why you may not be receiving your
Netcode
text:
Your mobile coverage could be poor where you are, so please check your signal. Unfortunately, your mobile provider's signal coverage is out of our control.
The phone number you're using is not set up to receive Netcode. Please check you're using the phone number (sim card) you provided to ASB for Netcode in your
Personal Profile
(your name at the top right-hand side of your screen) on
FastNet Classic internet banking
.
You are overseas and may need to set up roaming on your phone with your mobile service provider. Please note, we can’t guarantee Netcode delivery by text when you are overseas.
If none of the above reasons apply, you can call our contact centre on 0800 FASTNET (
0800 327 863
) from within New Zealand, or on +64 9 306 3000 if you are overseas, and we'll be happy to help resolve the issue.
If necessary, we can release your online payment with your authorisation. Please note, however, for security reasons, we can’t release International Money Transfers. They always require Netcode.
Alternatively, you can use the
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your Netcode-verified device to complete payments without Netcode (except International Money Transfers).
See also:
I haven't received my notification for two-step verification using the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: How do I know my FastNet Classic internet banking is safe?
answer:
ASB's online services all come with security features aligned with good practice.
FastNet Classic internet banking
has several layers of security to keep your information and accounts safe.
1. Secure login details
To log in to FastNet Classic internet banking, you'll need to enter your unique username and password every time. Five incorrect attempts to log in will result in a lock out.
2. reCATPCHA
You may be verified with the
reCAPTCHA
security feature when you log in to your FastNet Classic internet banking. This is in place to prevent other people guessing or compromising your account.
3. Time and date stamp
You'll be able to see the time and date of your last login. This way you'll know that no one else has accessed your account.
4. We have security systems in place
FastNet Classic security monitoring and software uses strong encryption, which scrambles information passed between you and ASB before it travels over the internet.
5. Look for the padlock
FastNet Classic internet banking also holds an Extended Validation (EV) SSL Certificate. It's a badge of authentication and means that if you're using a modern browser, your address bar will show a padlock next to its URL when you visit asb.co.nz. This makes it easy for you to be sure you're on the genuine ASB website.
6. Netcode
FastNet Classic internet banking also has a second level of security called
two-step verification
which is a free service.
Two-step verification helps ensure it’s really you, by using the
ASB Mobile Banking app
, texting a code to your mobile phone, or using a portable Netcode token.
See also:
Learn how to set up
Netcode at Login
, our two-factor authentication.
Learn more about
two-step verification.
What is Netcode and why do I need it?
How do I register for Netcode?

question: What is the Dashboard in the ASB Mobile Banking App?
answer:
The Dashboard in the ASB Mobile Banking app allows you to stay up to date with your financial position across key ASB products.  When you click on the Dashboard icon in the ASB Mobile Banking app you’ll find an overview of ASB products such as your home loan, ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account, investment products and insurance policies.  By clicking on each tile, you’ll be taken to ASB product hubs where you can manage your products and get deeper insights.
With the Dashboard you can:
See an overview of your combined home lending with ASB and access to Home Central with one click
See an overview of your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account and investment products and access Wealth Central with one click
See an overview of ASB Insurance products and access to Insurance Central with one click
Manage your home loan repayments
Review your investment history via Wealth Central
View your next upcoming insurance payment

question: How do I log in to Online Share Trading?
answer:
There are two ways to log in to
Online Share Trading
:
1. Typing the URL in directly
To log in to ASB Securities Online Share Trading, type
www.asb.co.nz/asb-securities
into your browser, and click the yellow Log In button at the top right corner. On the next page, enter your username and password, and then click Log In.
2. Selecting 'Online Share Trading' from the log in menu
Head to
www.asb.co.nz
and select Online Share Trading from the dropdown menu next to the yellow Log In button at the top right corner of the page.
More about
Online Share Trading
.
Remember, ASB will never ask you by email or text for your secure banking information or ask you to log in to your account from a link in an email or text.

question: How do I log in to FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
To log in to ASB's FastNet Classic internet banking, type
www.asb.co.nz
into your browser and click on the yellow Log In button at the top right corner of the page. Make sure ‘FastNet Classic’ is selected in the dropdown menu next to the yellow button.
On the next page, enter your username and password, and then click Log In.
You may also be verified by the security feature,
reCAPTCHA
, which provides an additional level of protection.
Remember, ASB will never ask you by email or text for your secure banking information or ask you to log in to your account from a link in an email or text.

question: The log in page looks different- is it genuine?
answer:
To ensure you’ve arrived on our genuine website, we ask you to log in through the ASB website, and do so by typing asb.co.nz directly into your browser.
You may also see the logo for the security feature,
reCAPTCHA
, which provides an additional level of protection.
Remember, ASB will never contact you and ask for your Internet Banking password, Netcode details, credit card details or PIN numbers.
We will also never TXT or email you with a link to log in to your Internet Banking. Both methods are often used by fraudsters to scam customers.
If you have clicked on a link, purporting to be from ASB and entered your credentials, please contact us directly on
0800 ASB FRAUD
(0800 272 372) and forward the message to
phishing@asb.co.nz
.
For any general queries, please feel free to contact us on
0800 803 804
.

question: What security precautions can I take to keep my transactions secure?
answer:
Taking your own security measures when you're using
FastNet Classic internet banking
, FastNet Business or the
ASB Mobile Banking app
is important.
Head to
asb.co.nz/security
for tips on how to stay safe online, and protect yourself from fraud and scams.

question: Will I need to enter a Netcode for all my payments?
answer:
No, you won’t need to enter a Netcode if:
The total amount of payments in a day is below $1,000 (or a lower amount as chosen by you)
You have already entered a Netcode during your current session in
FastNet Classic internet banking
They are automatic payments or bill payments loaded for you by an ASB staff member
You're paying someone you have already saved as a Netcode trusted payee
You're paying a company on our pre-registered or trusted list (such as Spark, Vodafone or IRD)
You're making payments via the
ASB Mobile Banking app
, if you have Netcode-verified the app
What is Netcode?
I haven't received my Netcode. What now?

question: Do I need to be registered for Netcode to make an International Money Transfer?
answer:
Yes, you must be registered for Netcode to make an International Money Transfer.
Learn more about Netcode.
How do I register for Netcode?

question: How do I open an account with ASB?
answer:
Welcome, we’re glad you want to join ASB!
Moving your banking to ASB is easy and you can start the process online. Simply
choose the account
and services you would like and complete your details on our online application form, it only takes a few minutes.
Once you have completed the form we will automatically open your account and send you out a Visa Debit card if you have selected one. Your account will already accept deposits so you can give your account number to your employer or anyone else who needs it.
To activate your account, you’ll need to confirm your identity. You can do this anytime, anywhere on the ASB ID app (note: you’ll need to have a NZ driver licence, NZ passport or Australian passport). Alternatively you can visit any ASB branch with your
proof of identification, and a proof of address.
Once your account is activated you can then make withdrawals, use it for online bill payments, and access our internet banking and mobile services.
If you have any further questions, please visit our online help pages or call us on
0800 803 804
(+64 9 306 3000).
We’re looking forward to helping you with your banking.

question: Why isn’t my online payment going through in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
There could be a few reasons an online payment you are trying to make won’t go through.
One-off payments
If you are making a one-off payment online, ensure you have entered the account number of your payee correctly. It pays to double check they’ve given you the right details e.g. 12-1234-1234567-00. If the suffix at the end contains 3 digits, enter the last two.
Check you have selected the correct account from which to pay. You will not be able to make an online payment if it would result in a negative available balance in the account you’ve selected.
Bill payments
If you are making a bill payment, select a date for the payment and a valid amount. Check the details carefully.
Automatic payments
For automatic payments, if there are insufficient funds in your account to cover the outgoing payment, ASB will attempt to debit your selected account several times over a 24 hour period. This gives you time to deposit or transfer funds into the account to cover the outgoing automatic payment.
After this period, the payment will fail to go through if there are insufficient funds in your account to cover it and this will incur a fee. To avoid this, you can set up alerts via email and/or notifications, so you know when an automatic payment or bill payment may fail due to insufficient funds.

question: I keep getting security notices when I log in to my internet banking. How do I stop these messages?
answer:
As part of our efforts to increase account security, we will contact you every time there is an unrecognised login to your account. You can't opt out of receiving these messages.
We take online security very seriously and so we introduced these messages to help you detect if your internet banking has been accessed by someone other than yourself. This may help to eliminate fraudulent activity.
Remember, we will never ask you to log in to your account from a link in an email or text message. This email will always be sent from custserv@asb.co.nz. If you receive a suspicious email, forward it to phishing@asb.co.nz and delete the email.

question: Why are you sending me a security notice when I log in to my internet banking?
answer:
You will receive an alert if we detect a login to your internet banking that we believe you need to be aware of.
One of our key focuses is the safety of your accounts and your online banking. So we put this in place to help you detect if your account has been accessed by someone other than yourself, which may help protect against online fraud.
If it was you who logged in, then you can ignore the message. However if you don’t recognise the login then call us on 0800 272 204 (or +64 9 306 3000 if you're overseas) to secure your account as soon as possible.
Remember, we will never ask you to log in to your account from a link in an email or text message. This email will always be sent from custserv@asb.co.nz. If you receive a suspicious email, forward it to phishing@asb.co.nz and delete the email.
The email looks like this:

question: How do I stop my browser from saving my FastNet Classic internet banking password?
answer:
Some recent browser updates mean that you may now be prompted to save your password when logging in to
FastNet Classic internet banking
.
For security reasons, we recommend you
never
use this feature to store your FastNet password. This is of particular importance when using a public or shared computer as someone else could access your personal information without your knowledge.
If you would like your browser to stop doing this, please go to your relevant browser web page below and follow the instructions.
Internet Explorer
Firefox
Chrome
Safari

question: What does FastNet Classic internet banking cost?
answer:
It costs nothing to join and there is no fortnightly or monthly fee. All you will pay are the normal
ASB account and transaction fees
.
Managing your money through
FastNet Classic internet banking
is often more cost effective than through manual or branch transactions. It can also be quicker and more convenient.

question: How can I manage my True Rewards dollars in FastNet Classic?
answer:
Use FastNet Classic to see your True Rewards dollars balance and your statement of activity.
To view your True Rewards statement:
Log In to FastNet Classic.
Locate your True Rewards Card number on the Balances page.
Select your True Rewards Card number.

question: How do I make repayments on my ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
Repayments on your personal loan are made by fortnightly or monthly payments, which are automatically  deducted from your ASB transaction account. You can choose to have  fortnightly or monthly payments. If you do not have an ASB transaction account at the time we arrange your personal loan, no problem, we’ll open one for you.
If you’d like to apply for a personal loan, you can do so by visiting the application page
here
.
Watch our video to find out more tips on how you can reduce your debt:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: How do I increase my ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
As an existing ASB customer, you can apply to
increase your personal loan online
using FastNet Classic internet banking. You’ll need to complete a new loan application for the increased amount.
If you don’t use internet banking you can also visit your nearest
ASB branch
, or call us on 0800 272 662.
Watch our video to find out more tips on how you can reduce your debt:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: How do I set up an MYOB bank feed from FastNet Classic?
answer:
Our account feeds can send your ASB transactions to MYOB every day, automatically. If you use FastNet Classic internet banking, here's how to set them up online. (We also provide
transaction feeds for Xero
.)
Before you start
The first step is to set up your ASB accounts in MYOB. Then you'll be able to choose them as the destination for your daily transactions.
It's easy to do. For details, see
MYOB's help article on setting up new accounts
. In their process, MYOB gives you an eight digit number. Take a note of it, as you'll need it to complete your set up in FastNet Classic.
Activating your feeds
Once you've logged in to FastNet Classic:
1. Select your name in the top right corner.
2. Then select 'Accounting Set Up' from the drop down menu.
3. Choose 'MYOB' from the pull down menu.
4. Enter your eight digit MYOB code.
A list of all your ASB accounts will appear.
5. Tick the ones you want to update in MYOB. There's no cost for adding accounts, and you can turn off your feeds at any time.
6. You can back date your account feeds. The default is 1 April, which for most businesses is the start of the financial year.
7. When you've made your selections, confirm you're happy for us to send your information to MYOB, and click OK.
MYOB will then complete the set up at their end, and email you when your bank feeds are ready. Then you can link the bank feeds in AccountRight, and you're done.
Problems?
If you've run into any issues on the ASB side, just give our FastNet Business help desk a call on 0800 22 55 27. If you have any MYOB issues, it's best to contact them directly via
their support page
.

question: How can I accept my loan documentation through FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Accepting your loan digitally is easy through FastNet Classic and if there's a document waiting to be accepted, we'll send a you notification.
1. Log into FastNet Classic, select the notification bell in the page header and click
'View Agreement'
.
2. Review your loan details and then select
'As soon as possible'
or another date you’d like the loan funds to be made available.
3. Open and review the Facility Agreement and Terms and Conditions pdfs and click the
'I accept & agree'
buttons for each document.
4. If you feel that you need more time, select the
'I'll do this later'
option. You still have until the expiry date to accept them and if there's anything you'd like to discuss, please contact your lending specialist or account manager.
5. If you agree to all the terms and conditions, click the tick box and then select the
'Next'
button.
6. On the next screen enter the Netcode that was sent to your mobile phone or use your Netcode token if you have one. Then select
'Finish'
and you're all done.
To print or save your documents:
Open the loan document in Adobe Reader; if you don't have this, download free from https://get2.adobe.com/reader/
Once the document is opened, right click at the bottom of the page and a menu bar will appear to the option to save or print.

question: Which money management package is best if I want to export my statement?
answer: There are many applications available that support the CSV and TDV formats, including Microsoft Excel, plus some that support OFX and QIF.  If you wish to use these file formats for Statement Exports, check your application's documentation to see if these formats are supported.

question: What is Statement Export and how do I use it?
answer:
Statement Export in FastNet Classic allows you to export transactional information from your ASB accounts and import it into your chosen financial management package. Statement Export supports the OFX (Open Financial Exchange) file standard. For more information on this standard, please go to the
Open Financial Exchange site
. We also support QIF (earlier versions of Quicken), CSV (Comma Separated Values) and TDV (Tab Delimited Values) file formats.
How many months of statement history can I export at once?
You can export up to 10,000 transactions on your bank and Credit Card accounts.

question: Why have you changed the phrase Access Code to Username?
answer: We have renamed the FastNet Classic and Online Share Trading 'access code' to ‘username’ which is a standard term used across most websites. This doesn't change your username, only how we refer to it on our websites.

question: How does the Password Strength meter work?
answer:
To help guide your choices when selecting a new password for FastNet Classic internet banking and Online Share Trading we’ve included a strength meter on the Change Password page. The strength meter will guide you to choosing a stronger valid password by rating it as Basic, Better or Best. Try different combinations to increase the strength rating.
We recommend you update your password from time to time.
Remember, never give your password to anyone. ASB will never ask you by email for your secure banking information or ask you to log in to your account from an email.

question: How is the foreign exchange rate calculated on my purchase?
answer:
Your purchase will be converted at ASB's
latest foreign currency rates.

question: Why did I get sent to the ASB homepage when I tried to visit the FastNet Classic log in page?
answer:
We have recently made some changes to the way you log in to FastNet Classic as part of some new features introduced based on customer feedback.
If you have tried visiting FastNet Classic from an old bookmark or favourite you might notice we have redirected you to asb.co.nz.  This is because FastNet Classic has a new website address – https://online.asb.co.nz
We recommend you access our online services by first typing asb.co.nz or asb.co.nz/asb-securities directly into your browser address bar.
One option to make the login process faster now is Remember My Name. Simply tick this option when you're about to log in from your computer.
Watch this video to see the new ASB Login experience for FastNet Classic:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Why did my internet banking or Online Share Trading not log out when I closed my browser tab?
answer:
If you forget to click log off and then navigate to another website or close your browser tab, your internet banking or Online Share Trading will remain logged in for a short period.
To protect your account, you’ll be automatically logged off after 10 minutes of inactivity. If you return to the log in page within this time, we will automatically ask you to log in again.
It’s still more secure to click the Log Off button at the top right corner of internet banking or Online Share Trading when you’ve finished your banking or share trading.

question: Has my username or password changed?
answer:
No, there has been no changes to your FastNet Classic internet banking or Online Share Trading username or password unless you choose to change these yourself. As always we do recommend you change your password from time to time.
Remember, ASB will never ask you by email for your secure banking information or ask you to log in to your account from an email.

question: What should I do if I receive an email asking me to log into my account?
answer:
We will never ask you by email for your secure banking information (account or credit card details, password, PIN number or NetCode details) or ask you to log in to your account from an email. We'll always ask you to log in through our website and you should do this by typing www.asb.co.nz directly into your browser address bar.
If you have been asked then this is someone trying to gain access to your accounts, this type of attack is commonly known as phishing and you should forward the email to phishing@asb.co.nz and then delete the email immediately.
Phishing is the process of trying to get information by pretending to be a trustworthy organisation. In most cases fraudsters will send a fake company email asking for things like usernames, passwords or credit card details. These emails can look very real. Our
Email Policy
will tell you more about Phishing and what to do if you’ve been targeted.

question: How do I check the monthly fees on my transaction account?
answer:
If you have chosen to receive your statement electronically, and monthly fees apply to your account, you can see your fee summary online in FastNet Classic. Otherwise please check your paper statement to check your monthly fees.
To view your monthly fee summary online:
- Log in to FastNet Classic online banking.
- Under the balances main screen, select the account for which you want to see fees.
- Scroll to the bottom of the page below all the transactions
and you’ll see "Click
here
to view the monthly account fees for this account". Click on the link.
- You’ll be taken to the ‘Fee Summary’ page, simply select the date range you require and select View.
- Your monthly fees will be listed.
For an overview of bank fees and charges see the
ASB Guide to fees
.

question: I have a new phone number, how do I update Netcode so I get it on my phone?
answer:
If you change your phone number you’ll need to update those details with us so you can continue to receive Netcode.  For your security and protection, you are not able do this online.
To update your details for Netcode, please call on 0800 FASTNET (
0800 327 863
), or if you are overseas call us on
+64 9 306 3000
. We’ll ask you some security questions to establish you are you, and update your Netcode details so you can continue to receive Netcode on your new number.
Alternatively, you can visit any
ASB branch
, the staff will be pleased to help you.

question: Are One-Off and Bill Payments secure?
answer:
