Yes.  Only you can authorise and action One-Off and Bill Payments, using your FastNet Classic Password.  As an added protection for you there is also a
daily limit
to the amount you can pay away from your account using FastNet Classic.

question: How do I change my FastNet Classic access code?
answer:
To change your FastNet Classic Username, simply call us on 0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863) or if you are overseas, call us on + 64 9 306 3185. Alternatively,  visit any
ASB branch
and we’ll be happy to help.

question: I can't Log In to FastNet Classic – what am I doing wrong?
answer:
If this is the first time you’ve tried to Log In to FastNet Classic, the first thing to check is your Username and Password.
Check that the zeros, O's, ones or I's in the Username you selected and the Password we supplied to you are correct. It’s easy to miss-key these when entering them. If your password was changed after 23 May2015, it will be case sensitive.
If you’ve selected your own Password, check it’s correct.  If it is correct and you still can’t Log In, call the FastNet Help Desk on
0800 FASTNET
(0800 327 863).  If you are overseas, call us on
+ 64 9 306 3185.

question: What internet browsers can I use to view ASB websites?
answer:
To make sure sure our web pages display properly, and that you have the latest security updates, we recommend you use the latest version of one of the supported browsers below. If you use an older browser, or a browser that isn’t on our supported list, you may notice that some functions and features may not work or display perfectly.
Operating system
Recommended browsers (latest versions of)
Windows
Chrome, Firefox or Internet Explorer
iOS
Chrome, Safari or Firefox
Android
Chrome or Firefox
To get the latest versions of our supported browsers:
Download Chrome
Download Firefox
Download Safari
Download Internet Explorer
If you're having difficulty viewing the website and still wish to proceed to log in, please follow one of the links below:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking
Log in to FastNet Classic mobile banking
Log in to Online Sharetrading
Log in to FastNet Business internet banking

question: How do I know which ASB internet banking option to use for my business?
answer:
ASB has two internet banking platforms –
FastNet Classic
and
FastNet Business
.
Most customers manage their personal banking with FastNet Classic, which also often meets the needs of many smaller businesses.
If your organisation’s finances are too complex for one person to manage using FastNet Classic, FastNet Business provides advanced user tools, the ability to delegate banking tasks to your finance team and accounting integration.

question: Does Netcode work on a foreign phone?
answer:
Yes. Netcode can be sent to New Zealand or International mobile phone numbers. The alternative option for you is to use a Netcode token, which we can post out to you. If you’d like a Netcode token, call us on + 64 9 306 3000 from overseas.  Once you receive your Netcode token, call us again to activate it.
Please note:
Netcode via SMS is free to use if you have a New Zealand mobile phone number, but please check with your provider in case there are charges to receive international text messages. Since we rely on international phone providers to send our Netcode SMS to international phone numbers, we cannot guarantee you’ll be able to receive Netcode SMS to your international phone number.

question: Can I stop Netcode if I want to?
answer:
Yes. Simply reply STOP to any Netcode TXT message to deregister from Netcode. Your mobile provider may charge you for the TXT as normal mobile charges will apply.
Remember, if you choose to not to use Netcode, you’ll need to call
0800 FASTNET
(0800 327 863) to identify yourself and release payments whenever you make transactions which take you over your daily Netcode limit of $1000.

question: Will I have to pay charges or fees if I pay off my ASB Personal Loan early?
answer: No you won’t. You can pay off your personal loan at any time, with no fees and no charges. In fact, the faster you pay it off, the more you will save in interest payments.

question: Why does the new Log In panel flash or not stay open?
answer:
Some customers using older computers have reported problems with the Log In panel at the top right of asb.co.nz.  This article provides options to address this problem.
In some cases the Log In panel shows FastNet Business details (three boxes), flashes, or cannot be changed to FastNet Classic internet banking. This problem has been reported with some older computers running Windows Vista and Internet Explorer 9.
Our technical team is working on this problem with urgency. We apologise for any inconvenience this problem may cause.
What are my options?
If you’re trying to access FastNet Classic internet banking, try the following options.
If three boxes are displayed asking to enter your Client ID, User ID and Password, change the log in panel from FastNet Business by clicking the arrow and choosing FastNet Classic from the list. Once you choose FastNet Classic, you will be taken to the new ASB Login page to enter your username (formally access code) and password.  You can then do your internet banking as normal.
If this doesn’t work or the panel does not stay open, try the following:
Access our new ASB Login page by typing
https://online.asb.co.nz/auth
directly into your browser address bar. Alternatively, click the Internet Banking link at the foot of our home page, asb.co.nz
Try a different internet browser, like
Google Chrome
.
We recommend you always use the current version of your preferred browser to ensure our web pages display quickly, and that you have the latest security updates. Our websites are designed to work with the production version of most modern popular browsers –
see our list here
.
If you’ve tried these options and you still can’t log in, please call the FastNet Help Desk on 0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863).  If you are overseas, call us on + 64 9 306 318.

question: Can I increase the payments on my ASB Car Loan?
answer:
Yes. The more you pay with each instalment, the faster you will pay off your loan and the less interest you will pay.  If you are able to comfortably increase your repayments without putting yourself under financial pressure, it’s an option and it’s free to do.
Call us on
0800 272 662
to increase your payments.

question: Can I delete requests made in the FastNet Business Card Manager module?
answer:
Yes you can, but only if the request hasn’t been fully authorised. Such requests can be found under the ‘Unsent requests’ section of Card Manager.
Once deleted, the request will remain visible under ‘Unsent requests’ until the end of the day. Overnight it will be removed from the list of ‘Unsent requests’.
A ‘Sent request’ cannot be deleted.

question: How do I cancel and replace a Visa Business card via the FastNet Business Card Manager module?
answer: Once you’ve identified the card you wish to cancel and replace, use the drop down box to the right and select ‘Cancel and Replace’. Only one authorisation is required for cancelling and replacing a card.

question: How do I increase or decrease the credit limit on a Visa Business card via the FastNet Business Card Manager module?
answer:
Once you’ve identified the card you wish to increase the limit on, use the drop down box to the right and select ‘Change Credit Limit’.
Enter the new limit (in multiples of $100) as required and submit the request. Authorisation, as per your preferred number of signatories, is required for the requested limit increase. Only one authorisation is required for decreasing a limit.
Note: the limit can’t be increased to a value beyond your organisation’s facility credit limit. An error message will appear if this occurs.
Also note: the limit cannot be decreased below the existing balance on the card.

question: How do I provide FastNet Business Card Manager access to another user in my organisation?
answer:
Once your organisation has the Card Manager module, your FastNet Business Administrator can provide access to other FastNet Business users as required.
They can assign three  types of permissions:
View
Create, Modify and Delete
Authorise

question: Can I manage my personal card using the FastNet Business Card Manager module?
answer:
No, the Card Manager module is for managing VISA Business cards only. Personal cards and VISA Business Rewards cards can’t be managed via the Card Manager module. To manage your personal cards you can use
ASB Card Control
via ASB’s personal mobile banking app.

question: Why are you greeting me by my first name – can I change this to my preferred name?
answer:
At this stage the ASB Login will use your first name if you have ticked Remember My Name. By unticking the Remember My Name box you will no longer see this.
While we do not currently show preferred names, this is being considered as a future improvement.

question: How do I release a payment after getting a Netcode?
answer:
Once you receive a Netcode on your phone for a particular payment you are making, here’s how to release the payment:
Enter the eight digit Netcode you have received on your phone into the Netcode field on the pop up box within FastNet Classic. This authorises the payment.
You’ll then be asked to confirm you are sure you want to make the payment.  Check the details of the payment carefully, if they are correct, select Yes.
Your payment will then be added to your scheduled payments.

question: I'm in FastNet Classic, how do I know if an online payment has reached my payee?
answer:
With Bill or One-Off Payments, we'll prompt you on screen to check you intend to make the payment you have set up.  With One-Off Payments, check the account details of your payee carefully to ensure you are paying the right person.
Then check your list of transactions in the Statement feature after you've made your online payment (or on your selected payment date). Your payment should appear in your list of transactions, as a debit. This shows the payment has left your account.
Your payee's receiving bank controls when your payee receives the payment in their account, generally this is the next working day.  Normally, payees who also bank with ASB will receive the payment straight away.
If you have any concerns, please either contact the payee directly, or contact us on
0800 FASTNET
(0800 327 863) or
+ 64 9 306 3185
if you are calling from overseas and we will do all we can to help.

question: If I'm making an International Money Transfer, how do I identify the beneficiary bank I wish to transfer funds to?
answer:
There are two ways to identify the beneficiary bank to which you wish to transfer funds.
The first is to contact the beneficiary and obtain their bank’s SWIFT Code. Enter this code on the transfer where indicated. The second is by using the search function available on the International Transfers page in FastNet Classic.
You’ll see you can select a country, city, bank and address in order. Make your selections from the drop down lists you see there.  If using this method we recommend you have the beneficiary bank branch address available to ensure you are sending funds to the correct location.

question: What information do I need to put in the "Particulars", "Code" and "Reference" fields when I am making an IRD Payment?
answer: The fields for "Particulars", "Code" and "Reference" allow you to enter information that you want to appear on your statement, this is your reference for the transaction. You can enter any information you like (up to 12 characters, except in the reference field which is limited to 8 characters).

question: How do I reset or change my ASB login password?
answer:
You can reset or change your password using the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic internet banking.
To change your Fastnet Classic password using the ASB Mobile Banking app you must be registered for Netcode.
What is Netcode and why do I need it?
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Passwords and security’.
Select ‘Change password’.
Tap 'Send Netcode’.
You’ll be sent a Netcode to your Netcode registered mobile number, enter this and select ‘Continue’.
Enter a new, unique password and confirm by re-entering.
Select ‘Submit’.
FastNet Classic:
If you don’t know your password:
Click 'Log In'.
Click 'Forgot your password?'
Follow the onscreen instructions.
If you know your password and want to change it:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Select ‘Change password’.
Type in your current password.
Then type in your new password and confirm by re-entering.
Click ‘Change password’.
Find out more information on
how to create a strong password
and
where to find your username
.
If you’re still having trouble resetting your password, call us on 0800 327 863, or +64 9 306 3185 from overseas.

question: How do I create, change or stop my alerts?
answer:
You can set up and manage alerts on the
ASB Mobile Banking app
or
FastNet Classic internet banking
.
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, and under ‘Notifications’ select ‘Manage alerts’.
Choose how you receive your alerts or set an account alert.
Use the toggle button to move to 'ON'.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Select ‘Manage alerts’.
Choose how you receive your alerts or set an account alert.
When setting up email alerts, check that we’ve got your correct email address in FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Personal profile’.
Open the email section and check your email address is set as your primary email address.
If you have any questions, feel free to call us on
0800 803 804
.

question: Can I personalise my ASB account images?
answer:
Yes, you can personalise your ASB account images, here’s how:
ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’.
Under ‘Personalise’ tap 'Account images'.
Select an account and make the changes.
Click 'Done'.
FastNet Classic internet banking:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Personalise' on the top of the accounts.
Select 'Personalise account' then select an account.
Click 'Change account image', choose an image and click 'Select'.
Click 'Save'.
Please note, you cannot upload your own images to the gallery via Fastnet Classic, however any images uploaded into the mobile app will reflect on FastNet Classic.

question: How do I activate my new ASB Visa credit card or debit card?
answer:
To activate your credit card you need to create a PIN.
You can create a PIN for your card and activate it from within our ASB Mobile Banking app or
FastNet Classic internet banking.
(You must be registered for
Netcode
).
To activate your card through your ASB Mobile app:
Log into your ASB Mobile app.
Open the ‘More’ menu in the bottom bar.
Select ‘Cards & devices’.
If you have more than one card, select the card you wish to activate, then click 'Update card PIN'.
Enter the 3-digit CVV number on the back of your physical card.
Enter your chosen PIN on the keypad.
Confirm your chosen PIN on the keypad.
To activate your card in FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Card PIN’.
Select the card you wish to change the PIN for.
Enter the 3-digit security code number (CVV) on the back of your physical card.
Enter chosen PIN and confirm by re-entering.
Your card is activated and is ready to use.
Alternatively you can call us on 0800 803 804 or go into any
ASB branch
to create a PIN.
For more information on how to stay safe online visit our
Security
section.

question: How do I reduce my daily payment limits?
answer:
Reducing your daily payment limits can help protect you if you fall victim to fraud and scams. By setting a lower daily limit, you can make it more difficult for someone to steal a large amount of money from your account if they gain access to your account.
To reduce your daily limits on the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Passwords and security' then ‘Security Checkup’.
Tap on ‘Reduce daily limits’.
Move the sliders to the value that you want to select as your daily limit.
Click on ‘Save daily payment limit'.
To reduce your daily limits in FastNet Classic internet banking:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Security Checkup’.
Click on ‘Reduce daily limits’.
Move the sliders to the value that you want to select as your daily limit.
Click on ‘Save daily payment limit'.
Find out more about the
daily payment limits in the ASB Mobile app
.
Find out more about the
daily payment limits in FastNet Classic
.

question: How do I update my five-digit PIN on my ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
To update your five-digit PIN using the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Passwords and security'.
Select ‘Change PIN’.
Tap 'Send Netcode’.
Enter your new PIN and confirm by re-entering.
Enter your FastNet Classic password.
Select 'Confirm'.

question: How do I change my default account for payments and transfers in FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
To save time when making payments or transfers using FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app, you can choose a default account. This account will be automatically selected as your 'from account', but you can change it at any time.
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.

question: Why am I seeing a pop up when I log into FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:

You’ll see this message the first time you log into your FastNet Classic. To continue banking, simply close it and it won’t appear again until other new features or important information is released.

question: What information do I need to provide an overseas bank or to someone else who wants to send an International Money Transfer to my ASB account?
answer:
A deposit into your ASB bank account can be made from anywhere in the world provided the sender has the right information. You will need to provide the following:
Beneficiary name
This should be your full name as shown on your ASB account.
Beneficiary physical address
This should be your full physical address including post code and country. P.O Boxes or other non-physical addresses are not accepted.
Beneficiary account
Your full account number. For example, your ASB NZD account is formatted like this, 12-3nnn-0nnnnnn-nn, while your ASB Foreign Currency Account is in this format, nnnnnnnn-[CCY]-nn.
If an
IBAN (International Bank Account Number)
is requested provide your full ASB account number, as above.
If a
BSB
or
Sort Code
is requested please provide the first six digits of your full bank account number – these represent the bank and branch codes, i.e. 12-3nnn
SWIFT Code
ASB’s SWIFT code is ASBBNZ2A. This is used to identify that ASB is your bank and is sometimes referred to as a Bank Identifier Code (BIC)
Beneficiary Bank Address
ASB Bank Limited, 12 Jellicoe Street, Auckland Central, Auckland 1010, New Zealand
Please note: ASB's fee for receiving an inbound IMT directly to an NZD account is $15* (no charge for a transfer to a Foreign Currency Account and waived for amounts of NZD $100 or less).
*May be subject to fees imposed by other banks.

question: What happens to Save the Change if I close my accounts?
answer:
If you close your accounts Save the Change will switch off. If you have nominated more than one savings account, Save the Change will switch Saving the Change to the remaining account(s), and switch off when the last nominated savings account is closed.
If you change your transaction or savings account, Save the Change can go with you. For example, if you change your Headstart account to the Everyday account, Save the Change will automatically transfer to the new account and continue as normal.

question: What information do I need to make an International Money Transfer?
answer:
It is important to have the correct information when sending an International Money Transfer. Here's what you need:

Recipient Account: full account number or IBAN (International Bank Account Number). If an IBAN is used, it should be presented as a continuous string of alphanumeric characters with no spaces.
Recipient Bank: The recipient bank's name, address and SWIFT Code (also known as a Bank Identifier Code/BIC).
If the recipient’s name is long and exceeds the character limit, continue typing into the first line of the address field, then continue typing the recipient’s address after the recipient’s name. This will ensure ASB can send all the required details with your payment to the recipient bank.

question: How is the KiwiSaver annual government contribution calculated?
answer:
The annual government contribution is calculated based on a year that begins on 1 July and ends on 30 June. To receive the full contribution you need to be an
eligible member
for the full year (from 1 July to 30 June) and pay at least $1,042.86 into your account during that year. This excludes any amounts transferred from an Australian complying superannuation fund and any contributions made by the Government or your employer.
If you have been an eligible member for the full year and contributed less than $1,042.86, then you will receive an annual government contribution equal to quarter the amount you have contributed. For example, if your contributions were $500 over the year, then your annual government contribution would be $125.
What if I joined KiwiSaver part way through the year (1 July - 30 June)?
If you joined KiwiSaver for the first time part-way through a year, your annual government contribution will be based on the portion of the year you were an eligible member, as well as the KiwiSaver contributions you made.
For example, if you joined KiwiSaver on 1 January, for the year to 30 June, you'll receive $130.36 (provided you've contributed at least $521.43). This is half of the maximum annual government contribution because you joined six months into the year.
Please see the
Inland Revenue website
for more details.

question: How can I change my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme contribution amounts?
answer:
If you are an employee, you can change your contribution rate to 3%, 4%, 6%, 8% or 10% at any time by giving notice to your employer. You can do this by letter or email indicating your new rate (3%, 4%, 6%, 8% or 10%), or by completing a KiwiSaver deduction form (KS2) available on the
Inland Revenue website
and giving it to your employer. You cannot change this rate more frequently than every three months unless your employer agrees.
Contributing at least $1,042.86 each year will mean you receive the maximum annual government contribution of $260.72 each year (if you are eligible). If you earn less than $34,762 and contribute 3%, your contributions will be less than $1,042.86 and you will not receive the maximum
annual government contribution
, unless you make extra contributions.
If you are not employed or are self-employed, you can make lump sum voluntary contributions, or change your regular contribution amount at any time.
Important note:
From 1 April 2026, the minimum employee and employer contribution rate will increase from 3% to 3.5%.
You will be able to temporarily opt down to the current 3% rate, if you choose, and still be matched at that rate by your employer.

answer:
You have 5 attempts to enter your correct ASB login password, any more attempts will lock you out. This is a safeguard to prevent any unauthorised access to your account. If you’re locked out, there are a couple of ways you can reset your password.
If you’ve enabled PIN log in and are
Netcode registered

Using the ASB Mobile app:
Open the ASB Mobile app. If you're automatically logged in with Face ID, choose Log Out from the More button at the bottom of the screen.
Tap on 'Login options' in the bottom left corner of the PIN screen.
Tap on 'Reset password' in the Login options panel and follow the easy steps.
We'll reset your ASB password and unlock your access immediately.
Using FastNet Classic:
Select 'FastNet Classic' in the top right corner of the webpage.
Click 'Log In'.
Click the 'Forgot your password?' link.
Enter your Username and Date of Birth and click ‘Continue’.
Enter the Netcode received on your mobile device (SMS) or on your physical token.
Enter your new password as per the criteria and click ‘Change password’.
You have now successfully set the password.
Log into Fastnet Classic Internet banking with the username and newly changed password and you are all set.
If you are unable to reset your password through the ASB Mobile app or FastNet Classic please call the ASB Contact Centre on 0800 327 863 or + 64 9 306 3185 (if you are overseas).

ASB Mobile app
.

question: What are the pending transactions on my credit or debit card?
answer:
What is a pending transaction?
Whenever you make a purchase with your credit or debit card this is deducted from your available balance immediately. This is called a pending transaction until the purchase has been processed by the merchant (i.e. retailer). A pending transaction occurs whenever the following happens:
When you make a purchase to your credit or debit card, this will be pending (waiting for) overnight processing. In most instances if a transaction is made before 8.30pm it will process overnight. Purchases after 8.30pm will be processed the following night. However, sometimes it can take a few days for the merchant (i.e. retailer) to complete the transaction; in this case purchases will appear as pending until the transaction is completed.
Payments to your credit card account will also be pending overnight processing. To prevent your account from becoming overdue you need to pay at least the minimum due before 8.30pm on your due date. After 8:30pm payments will be processed the following night.
Sometimes merchants apply a 'holding' transaction called a 'pre-authorisation' – for example hotels, when you receive the goods or services before the final payment is requested. The pending transaction should disappear once the final transaction is processed.
How do pending transactions affect my credit or debit card account balance?
Pending transactions affect your available balance immediately, and once the processing is complete the transaction will show on your account and your current balance will include this transaction.
For credit cards, you are not charged interest on any purchases until the processing is complete and they are no longer pending.
How long can transactions be pending?
Pending transactions are generally processed overnight. There are some occasions where transactions will take longer to process. These pending transactions can be held for up to 30 days. Some examples of these include accommodation, rental car and cruise transactions.
Examples of Pending transactions:
A hotel applies a 'pre-authorisation' charge of $500 to ensure your credit card can be used to cover any hotel charges.
If there were no additional hotel charges the $500 will drop off.
If you extend your stay at the hotel, they may require an additional hold amount, for example an additional $100 authorisation to cover the additional days you are staying. This means your available balance will be reduced by a total of $600.
Companies can charge a $1 pre-authorisation to verify your credit card details (such as energy retailers, or iTunes etc).
You have made a payment of $200 to your credit card at 9pm at night, this will remain as pending until it is processed after 8.30pm the following night.

question: Why can’t I see my scheduled IRD payment in FastNet Classic?
answer:
There could be a couple of reasons why you cannot see a scheduled IRD Payment in FastNet Classic:
If the IRD payment is scheduled for today, it may have already been paid. Check if the funds have been withdrawn from your account as expected.
If the IRD payment is on a joint account, it will only show in the scheduled payment view for the account holder who loaded the payment, not all account holders.
If the IRD payment was set up using GO Online or NZHL Online and you have since been migrated to ASB FastNet Classic you will not see the payments you had scheduled, however these payments will still go out on the due date.

question: I've made a credit card payment, when will it be processed?
answer:
Your credit card payment will be processed overnight provided you make it before 8.30pm. If your payment is made after 8:30pm, it will be processed the following night.
If you set up a direct debit, (which you can do from any New Zealand bank account) your payments will happen automatically, so you won’t need to remember to make it.
Check here for more information on
how to pay your credit card
.

question: What are the processing times for electronic one-off payments and transfers?
answer:
Transfers and payments to your own ASB account or someone else’s ASB account are real-time and transferred as clear funds 24 hours / 7 days a week. This means most funds will appear immediately*.
For payments between ASB and other New Zealand banks:
Payments are sent every hour to other banks between 9:15am and 11:30pm. Between these times, if you use One-Off Payment or FastNet IRD, we send the money within one hour but it’s up to the receiving bank when it’s posted to the recipient’s account.
Payments are received from other banks between 8:30am and 12:15 am. Money received by ASB will be credited to your account within 1 hour.
* Note, it can take 3-5 working days for the transaction to be visible in your ASB Investment Fund or ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account. Once the transfer is accepted, the unit price that applies, will be the price for the date that the transaction is accepted and processed.

question: Why do I need to change my password?
answer:
We are committed to ensuring your online banking is safe and secure, so we are asking all our customers who haven’t changed their password in several years to update their password. By changing your password, we can help ensure your password meets modern security standards.
Why am I being asked to do this now?
In recent years there has been an increase in cyber attacks and password breaches globally. We want to ensure our customers’ accounts are kept safe and to do this we need our customers to change their passwords regularly.
How do I know this isn't fraud?
We have only sent notifications within your FastNet Classic internet banking or ASB Mobile App. We will never send you a text message or email with a link asking you to change your password. If you do receive a message through text or email with a link asking you to click to log in or to provide details, this should be deleted. If you have shared information after clicking on a link and are concerned you may have been a victim of fraud, contact our fraud team immediately on 0800 ASB FRAUD (0800 272 372).
What if I don't want to change it?
We are not requiring you to change your password at this time, however we anticipate that we will require you to change it in the future so we do encourage you to take action now.
My password is very secure and meets modern security standards, do I still need to change it?
It's great that you understand the importance of having a strong password, however it's still important to change your password regularly to reduce cyber risks. Older passwords are encrypted using an older encryption algorithm (this is the method we use to store your passwords securely) and the modern algorithms we use now are more secure, so even if your password is high quality it's still a good idea to change it.
How can I change my password?
How do I reset or change my ASB login password using ASB Mobile app?
How do I change my FastNet Classic password online from within FastNet Classic?
