question: How do I manage my personal information in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Updating your personal details such as a new phone number or address is simple to do within FastNet Classic internet banking and we’ve made a few improvements to make it even easier.
There's now a feature called Personal Profile in your profile drop down in the top right-hand corner. Your Personal Profile keeps your key personal details in one easy place, making updates much simpler and faster.
Preferred name
Updating your preferred name means we will call you by the name you prefer when we contact you. We will also use your preferred name in some communications where appropriate.
Email and mobile verification
Your email and mobile phone details can now be updated and verified via your Personal Profile. Verification means that you've confirmed with us that we have the correct email and mobile number for you. To do this, click on the verify button by your email or mobile details. We’ll then send you a code through either email or mobile to enter into your profile. If you have a verified email we can send you some documents to that email address, reducing the amount of paper we send out.
Updating your personal details in FastNet Classic is easy:
Log in to FastNet Classic with your username and password.
Once you have logged in, select your Profile on the top right hand corner.
From the drop-down menu, select Personal Profile.
Update the details you want to change. To change your email and mobile number you will also need to verify them, it's easy you just follow the instructions.
Please note, you'll need to call us to change the phone number linked to Netcode, this is for your security.
To do this, call us on
0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863)
, or if you are overseas call us on
+64 9 306 3000
. We'll ask you some security questions to establish that you are you, before we update your Netcode details.
Address and identity verification, and provision of occupation
Your home address and identity can now be verified via your Personal Profile. Verification means that you've confirmed who you are, and you've helped us comply with New Zealand’s
Anti-Money Laundering (AML)
laws.
To verify your home address, click on the 'Verify address' button in the Home address tile to upload a PDF document as a proof of address.
To verify your identity, click on the 'Verify identity' button in the Identity tile to check if your ePassport is compatible with the
ASB ID app
and to find out how you can electronically complete this process. You might need to let us know your occupation and to do this, click on the 'Add an occupation' button in the Occupation tile to select your primary occupation.
If you need any further help, take a look at our
FastNet Classic FAQs
.

question: Why can't I see my account numbers in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
By default Tile view is visible when you open FastNet Classic, but it's easy to switch to see your account numbers, here's how:
On the balance page, click 'Personalise'.
When the Personalise box opens, click on 'Switch account view'.
Then select 'List view'.
Finally, click 'Save'.
You'll now be able to see more account information including your account numbers.
If there are some accounts that you’re not able to see, just click the 'View All' button to expand the view.

question: How do I change the beneficiary for Donate the Change?
answer:
You can choose from one of three ASB Charity partners to donate to (St John, KidsCan or Youthline) and you are able to change your selected charity any time through the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are donating the change from.
On your Account Summary, select 'More options', then 'Save the Change setup'.
Delete Donate the Change by clicking the 'X' icon next to the charity name.
Select 'Add charity to donate (optional)' and choose the new charity you wish to donate to.
Select 'Done'.

question: How do I set up or edit account grouping?
answer:
Group accounts is a feature available in the 'Personalise' menu. This is available only in FastNet Classic internet banking. The ASB Mobile Banking app will follow the order of the accounts but won’t display any groups.
Why can’t I see the 'Group accounts' option in the 'Personalise' menu?
This functionality is available only for customers that use 'List view'. To do this:
Go to the 'Personalise' menu
Select 'Switch account view'
Select 'List view' on the options
Select 'Save'
When you click on 'Personalise' again you will be presented with a menu which 'Group accounts', where it is the second option down.
How do I set up my accounts?
Select 'Group accounts' in the 'Personalise' menu
Select 'Groups on'
Select 'Edit groups'.
In this screen you can:
Reorder your groups:
Select 'Reorder groups' in the current view.
In 'Reorder groups', customers can drag and drop their groups into an order they prefer within FastNet Classic. If using in FastNet Classic mobile device/browser, use the arrows.
Select 'Next'
Then 'Save'.
Rename your groups:
Rename your groups using the 'Edit' button on the top right corner.
In this same page, you can also reorder your accounts. Using drag and drop in FastNet Classic or the arrows in the mobile device/browser version.
Click 'Next'
Then 'Save' to see the changes made in the accounts page.
Note: When you are regrouping, you can only regroup suffixes under individual unique/account numbers.
How do I remove groups I have set up?
Go to 'Personalise'
Select 'Groups accounts'
Select 'Group off'
Click 'Save'.
Note: Your accounts will lose the groups you set and will be displayed as a list. Once you remove your groups, the group configuration will be deleted.
Important information:
If customers change their account view (from 'List view' to 'Tile view') or reorder their accounts in the 'Personalise' menu in FastNet Classic or ASB Mobile app, they will lose their groups configuration. A message will pop up in FastNet Classic to confirm that the groups will be lost and to make sure the customer wants to proceed, but they will not be alerted about losing groups in the ASB Mobile app.

question: Can I use two-step verification via the ASB Mobile Banking app whilst I’m overseas?
answer:
Yes. If you are overseas, have internet access for your mobile device and meet the
eligibility criteria
, you will be able complete two-step verification using the ASB Mobile app for:
Logging in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Making one-off payments in internet banking.
Verifying your identity on the phone.

question: How can I add or update my business or organisation details to the list of registered payees in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
The registered bill payee list allows your customers to easily set up and pay your business or organisation from within FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Instead of having to type out account details when they want to pay a business or organisation, customers can search for the business or organisation by name and be guided through what details to provide.
Once you register with ASB your details will also be registered with other banks in New Zealand.
To add or update your details on the registered payee list:
Download the
ASB bill payee registration form
.
Fill out your details including the information you’ll need to be able to identify and reconcile payments.
Email your completed form to
customeradministrationsupport@asb.co.nz
or return it to your nearest branch or relationship manager.
ASB will register your details to display on other New Zealand bank’s registered payee lists.

question: After my loan is approved, how quickly will I receive the money?
answer:
If your ASB Personal Loan is unsecured, and you accept and agree to the loan documentation (if eligible) through FastNet Classic internet banking, you'll usually receive the funds in your account on the same day. If you are not eligible for FastNet Classic acceptance, then you'll need to physically sign the loan documentation and once we receive those documents it may take up to 48 hours for your funds to be available.
Find out more about our
personal loan options
.
ASB’s lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: I didn't choose a KiwiSaver scheme and I see I've been signed up to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, what happens now?
answer:
If you have been automatically enrolled in KiwiSaver because you recently started a new job or you asked your employer to sign you up to KiwiSaver, you may not have chosen a KiwiSaver scheme yourself.
If you haven’t chosen a scheme, you will have been placed in the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme because it’s your employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme.
In this situation you should have been given an ASB KiwiSaver Scheme Product Disclosure Statement by your employer.
You should receive a welcome pack from ASB. We encourage you to read it carefully and complete and return the Additional Information form. This form collects important information such as your chosen fund and your Prescribed Investor Rate (PIR), the rate at which tax will be paid on your share of the taxable income earned by the fund. If you do not complete this form, you will invest in the Conservative Fund and pay tax at the highest PIR.
For more information about how your investment is taxed see section 6 (What taxes will you pay?) of the
Other Material Information
document.

question: What’s the difference between Savings On Call and an ASB Cash Fund?
answer:
If you’re saving money but still like the flexibility to access your money any time, a Savings On Call or ASB Cash Fund account may be suitable for you.
There are two key differences between Savings On Call and an ASB Cash Fund account:
The tax you pay on your interest/returns, and
The minimum balance required in your account.
At a glance:
Tax rate
Minimum balance
Savings On Call
Your
Resident Withholding Tax rate
(RWT)
None
ASB Cash Fund
Your
Prescribed Investor Rate
(PIR)
$500
So if your income tax rate is more than 28%, you could  get a better after-tax return by putting your money into an ASB Cash Fund, compared to the after-tax return of a Savings On Call account offering the same rate and charging the same fees.
You can find out more information about how to work out your PIR by visiting our
PIR help page
or visiting
Inland Revenue’s website
for more information on PIRs.
The minimum balance required in your account
No minimum balance is needed for a Savings On Call account.
The minimum balance needed for an ASB Cash Fund account is $500.

question: Why have I been charged fees on my ASB account?
answer:
Depending on which ASB accounts you have and how you bank, fees on accounts and services vary. There are different fees for self-service transactions (such as transferring money between your ASB accounts via online and mobile banking, FastPhone, PayHere and ASB ATM's; and deposits via FastDeposit boxes) and staff-assisted transactions (such as deposits, withdrawals and transfers done in a branch or through the contact centre).
You’ll find a comprehensive guide to our fees and charges here.

question: Can I make regular voluntary payments to my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account through FastNet Classic?
answer:
Yes, you can make regular voluntary contributions to your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account in FastNet Classic.
Find more information on how you (or anyone) can make
additional contributions
.
To set up an automatic payment in FastNet Classic internet banking or another bank's online banking service you’ll need to have your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme member number and IRD number handy.
Follow this process to set up your automatic payment in FastNet Classic internet banking:
Click 'Make a payment' on the balances page, then select 'Automatic payment'.
Select the account you want the payment to come from.
Select ‘New Automatic Payment’.
Select the payment amount, the commencement date and the frequency (i.e. weekly, fortnightly etc).
Enter an amount and final payment date (if you wish).
Under ‘Recipient (Payee) Name’, enter ‘ASB KiwiSaver Scheme’.
Under ‘Recipient Account Number’, enter the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme bank account number 12 3113 0125778 00.
In ‘Details to appear on their statement’, enter your IRD number in the ‘Particulars’ field, your last name in the ‘Code’ field and your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme member number in the ‘Reference’ field.
Enter the details you want to appear on your statement.
Enter your FastNet Classic password and select ‘Submit’.
Other online banking services may have different processes for setting up an automatic payment.
If you prefer, complete a
direct debit form
and send it to ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, Freepost Authority ASB, PO Box 35, Shortland Street, Auckland 1140.

question: What if I want to stop Save the Change?
answer:
That's easy. You can switch off Save the Change any time you like through the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Saving the Change from.
On your Account Summary, select 'Save the Change Setup' under 'More Options'.
Disable Save the Change using the sliding button.
Select 'Done'.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Select 'Financial wellbeing' and then 'Save the change'.
On Your Save the Change Accounts screen, select 'Off'.

question: What happens if there's a problem with my Save the Change savings account?
answer:
If for any reason we can’t transfer your savings into one of your nominated savings accounts, the funds will simply be returned to your transaction account. We’ll display an error message in FastNet Classic on your Save the Change accounts page to let you know.
If you only have one nominated savings account, we’ll stop Save the Change until you choose a new savings account.
If you have more than one nominated savings account, we’ll continue distributing to those accounts, excluding the account with an error. For example, if you originally had three nominated savings accounts but there is a problem with one of them, your daily savings will be split over the two remaining accounts.
Can I delete the savings account with an error?
Yes. You can delete the savings account that has the error and add a new one.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Saving the Change from.
On your Account summary, select 'Save the Change Setup' under 'More options'.
Select 'Add another account' and choose the account.
Delete the account with the error.
Select 'Done'.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Select 'Financial wellbeing' and then 'Save the change'.
Select 'View/Edit setup'.
Select 'Add another account' and choose the account.
Delete the account with the error.
Select 'Save and Close'.

question: Do I need to have an ASB savings account to use Save the Change?
answer:
Yes, although it can be either your own ASB savings account or someone else's, whoever you'd like to receive the money.
If you don't have an eligible ASB savings account, you can apply for an account online.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select Open & apply from the main menu.
Select Transaction or Savings account and follow the instructions.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and select ‘Open and apply’ on the accounts page.
When the Open & apply page opens, select 'Open an account' from the Bank accounts section.
Select a savings account and follow the instructions to apply.
How do I send my Save the Change savings to someone else's ASB savings account?
If you’d like to Save the Change for someone else, such as your children or grandchildren, you can.  Their account will need to be an eligible ASB savings account.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Saving the Change from.
On your Account summary, select 'Save the Change Setup' under 'More options'.
Under Change goes into, select Another person's account.
Add their name and account details and the reference to appear on their account.
Select 'Done'.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Select 'Financial wellbeing' and then 'Save the change'.
Select 'View/Edit setup'.
Under You are Saving the Change to, select 'Add another Account'.
Select 'Another person’s ASB savings account'.
Add their name and account details and the reference to appear on their account.
Select 'Add', and then 'Save and close'.

question: Can I change my nominated Save the Change savings account(s)?
answer:
Yes. To change your nominated savings account in FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile Banking app you'll need to add a new savings account and delete the original one.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Saving the Change from.
On your Account summary, select 'Save the Change Setup' under 'More options'.
Select 'Add another account' and choose the account.
Delete the account you no longer wish to Save the Change to.
Select 'Done'.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Select 'Financial wellbeing' and then 'Save the change'.
Select 'View/Edit set up' on Your Save the Change accounts page.
Select 'Add another account' and choose the account.
Delete the account you no longer wish to Save the Change to.
Select 'Save and Close'.

question: How do I set up Save the Change?
answer:
Setting up Save the Change is easy.
In the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you would like to Save the Change from.
On your Account summary, select 'Save the Change Setup' under 'More options'.
Select 'Save the Change setup'.
Nominate the account you would like to Save the Change to.
Select your preferred rounding amount.
Select your preferred payment option.
Review and select 'Done' in the top right of the screen to complete the set up.
In FastNet Classic:
Log in and open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Select 'Financial wellbeing' and then 'Save the change'.
Follow the on-screen instructions to set up your Save the Change.
Watch our video to find out more tips on how you can save money:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Can I see past statement history and transactions on my account?
answer:
Yes, you can view up to seven years of transaction history on ASB bank accounts and ASB Credit Cards.
To do this:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
In the 'Everyday banking' section, select 'Search and export transactions'.
Choose the account you want to see a statement for.
Select the date range you want to view by clicking the calendar icon to the right of the 'From' date range box and choosing a month and day.
Repeat this for the 'To' date.
If you are searching for something specific, e.g., 'rates', enter that term in the search box.
Then select 'View'.
You can export up to 10,000 transactions per file download . This restriction has been put in place to prevent performance issues and maximise successful downloads.
You can also view statements for all your accounts (including credit card statements) in Document Centre in FastNet Classic internet banking. Document Centre stores PDF files of your paper statements from as far back as 1 December 2010, even if you have chosen to stop your paper statement.

question: Can I open a Foreign Currency Account in FastNet Classic?
answer:
Yes, if you’re eligible, you can open a personal Foreign Currency Account in FastNet Classic internet banking, but you'll need to have a standard ASB New Zealand dollar account already open in the same name as you wish to open the Foreign Currency Account in.
Here's how to see if you're eligible and apply:
Log into FastNet Classic.
Select the 'Open and apply' button on the accounts page.
When the Open & apply page opens, if you can see 'Foreign currency account' in the Bank accounts section, you’re eligible and can apply.
Follow the instructions to apply.
Please ensure you click on the ‘Terms and Conditions’ link that will appear for you to view.
Please note that you will have to agree to the ‘Terms and Conditions’ before you can open this a Foreign Currency Account.
Find out more information on
Foreign Currency Accounts
.

question: How do I view, edit or delete an Automatic Payment in FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
You can create, view and edit Automatic Payments via
FastNet Classic internet banking
and in the
ASB Mobile Banking app
.
To view an Automatic Payment in FastNet Classic:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
Under ‘Everyday banking’ Select ‘Upcoming payments’.
To edit or delete an Automatic Payment in FastNet Classic:
Log in to FastNet Classic and select the 'Make a payment' button.
Select 'Automatic payment' at the top of the page.
Select an Account Number from which you wish to view an Automatic Payment.
Select the 'View/Alter/Delete' button. Your current Automatic Payments will appear on screen.
Select the Recipient (payee) Name you wish to view or alter. The details of the current Automatic Payment will be on screen.
You will be able to see the final payment amount, if applicable.
View or alter the details as you wish.
Select Alter.
To view, edit or delete an Automatic Payment via the ASB Mobile app:
Log into the ASB Mobile app.
Select the account the Automatic Payment will be paid out of (deduction account).
Tap on 'More options' and then 'Upcoming payments'.
Tap the payment you wish to view or alter. The details of the current payment will be shown.
To delete an Automatic Payment, tap on 'Delete payment' at the bottom of the screen.
To edit an Automatic Payment, tap on 'Edit' at the top right of the screen.
Notes:
If it is a Bill Payment, you will not be able to edit, only delete. We are working to bring the ability to edit Bill payments to the ASB Mobile app.
In both Fastnet Classic internet banking and the ASB Mobile app, when a regular Automatic Payment is set up, only the next (one) upcoming payment in the series will display in the View Upcoming Payments pages.

question: Can I transfer my True Rewards dollars to an ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account via FastNet Classic?
answer:
With ASB, you can transfer any True Reward dollars you earn into your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account. You can also use True Rewards dollars to top up someone else's ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account. You’ll need your IRD number and your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme member number handy to reference your transfer.
Find your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme member number on the FastNet Classic home screen.
Find your IRD number in FastNet Classic by selecting your Profile – ‘Update Tax Details’.
To transfer your True Rewards dollars:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
On the balance screen, select your True Rewards card.
On the True Rewards card page, select 'Contribute to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme'.
Complete your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme details and select 'add to cart'.
Select 'Go to checkout' to complete your transfer.
True Rewards dollars can take up to ten business days to appear in your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account.

question: Can I make and view IRD payments using FastNet Classic?
answer:
Inland Revenue made changes on April 2020 to the tax types you will use when making an IRD payment. If you are unsure what tax type to use, please contact the Inland Revenue.
You can make all types of payments to Inland Revenue through FastNet Classic internet banking, including PAYE Tax Deductions, GST, Income Tax or Provisional Tax, Child Support and Student Loans, plus others.
To make a tax payment:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Select 'Make a payment'.
Select 'IRD Payment'.
Select a Tax Type from the drop down menu and follow the instructions.
Have your IRD number handy. You’ll need to enter this for whichever tax you are paying and you’ll need to know the end date of the period you are paying. (i.e. March 2020). You can arrange payments to occur up to 12 months in advance.
For your convenience, you can view all upcoming IRD payments by clicking on IRD Payments in the menu.
If you need further help with tax payments, please contact the Inland Revenue, or visit
www.ird.govt.nz
.

question: Can I make a lump sum voluntary contribution into my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account through FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
Yes, you can do this by making a transfer to your KiwiSaver Scheme account:
Log in to FastNet Classic and select the 'Transfer money' button on the accounts page.
Select where you would like the payment to come from under ‘From Account’
Select the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account you would like to make payment to under ‘To Account’
Enter how much you would like to transfer as a lump sum voluntary contribution
Select ‘Continue Transfer’
Confirm you are aware of the early withdrawal and  retirement criteria
Select ‘Make Transfer’*
You can also go into any ASB branch and make a payment over the counter. Find
your local ASB branch here
.
*Note, it can take 3-5 working days for the transaction to be visible in the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account. Once the transfer is accepted, the unit price that applies, will be the price for the date that the transaction is accepted and processed.

question: I've decided to join the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme. Can I do that online?
answer:
Yes, you can.  You’ll need to be an existing ASB customer and be registered for FastNet Classic online banking.   If you are not already a KiwiSaver member, you’ll need to meet the eligibility criteria for joining KiwiSaver (living (or normally living) in New Zealand and entitled to live in New Zealand permanently).
To join the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme online:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Select the 'Open and apply' button on the accounts page.
When the Open & apply page open, select 'ASB KiwiSaver Scheme' from the KiwiSaver & investment section.
Follow the instructions to apply.
If you are an ASB customer but not registered for FastNet Classic, you can register for FastNet Classic by calling 0800 FASTNET (0800 327 863).
If you are not an ASB customer, you’ll need to read the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme
Product Disclosure Statement
, complete an
enrolment form
and drop it into any ASB branch or send it, along with your identification documentation, to FreePost Authority ASB, ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, ASB Group Investments, PO Box 35, Shortland Street, Auckland 1140.

question: How can I withdraw funds from my ASB Foreign Currency account?
answer:
To withdraw funds from your ASB Foreign Currency Account you can either:
Make an
International Money transfer
via FastNet Classic; or
Transfer funds to your linked accounts (NZD and foreign currency) via FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile App; or
Visit your local
ASB branch
Monday to Friday (9am to 4:30pm) to make an International Money Transfer.

question: I have transferred my KiwiSaver savings to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, when will I be able to see my balance in FastNet Classic?
answer:
If you are transferring to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, it usually takes around 10 working days before the transfer is completed and you see your balance appear in FastNet Classic.
This is because we must inform Inland Revenue of your transfer.  We then organise the transfer with your previous KiwiSaver provider.

question: How do I apply for life insurance through ASB?
answer:
You can apply for insurance with ASB, in any of the following ways.
Phone 0800 200 252.
Email
insurances@asb.co.nz
and one of our insurance managers will contact you.
Visit any
ASB branch
.

question: How do I setup a five-digit PIN on my ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
To set up a five-digit PIN on the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in to your ASB Mobile app
Select 'More' in the bottom bar
Select 'Settings'
Select 'Security'
Toggle 'PIN log in for this device'
Select 'Change PIN'
Please enter your new five-digit PIN
Please re-enter your newly selected five-digit PIN
Enter your FastNet Classic password
Select 'Confirm' in the top right hand corner
Success, your five-digit PIN has now been set.

question: If I complete a Foreign Currency Transfer, when will it be processed?
answer:
Transfers are usually processed immediately during processing hours (9am to 4pm NZT, Mon-Fri).
If the transfer is made outside these hours or during a
currency holiday
, it will be processed on the next business day.
You can check the full list of
currency holidays
to find out if your transfer is affected.
If the transfer hasn't processed by 10am on the next business day, please get in touch with Global Markets on +649 302 3055 or
0800 806 280
or ASB Securities on +649 448 8120 or
0800 272 732
(option 2).

question: Why can't I see my business insurance policies on ASB Insurance Central?
answer: ASB Insurance Central doesn't include your business insurance products. For information on your business insurances, please contact the AIA Customer Relationship team on 0800 272 543 or IAG Business Team on 0800 006 177 and they can provide you with the information you need on your cover.

question: I am the Life Assured of a policy, why can't I see it on ASB Insurance Central?
answer: Only policy owners can view their policies on ASB Insurance Central. If you are insured, please contact the AIA Customer Relationship team on 0800 272 543 (Monday – Friday 8am – 5pm) and they can provide you with the information you need on your cover.

question: Why can't I see my other life, loan, income and health policies on ASB Insurance Central?
answer: You may have other ASB policies that are not shown in ASB Insurance Central. If you require information on these insurance policies, please contact us on 0800 200 252 (Monday – Friday 8am – 5pm).

question: Why is ‘Organise’ account view in FastNet Mobile not working?
answer:
Some customers are experiencing issues when trying to use the ‘Organise’ option in the account view of FastNet Mobile (The mobile browser version of FastNet Classic).
To fix this issue, you must clear the browser’s cache.
For instructions on how to clear your browser’s cache, please visit the developers website, as these instructions will vary between browsers.

question: Are there any fees for using Save the Change or Donate the Change?
answer:
No. There are no set up fees, no on-going fees, and no fees for the daily transfer to your savings account or the ASB charity partners.
Watch our video to find out tips on how you can save money:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Can I claim the tax back from my Donate the Change?
answer: You can claim tax back on donations $5 and greater.  You will need to directly contact the charity you donated to in order to obtain a tax receipt.  You will need to provide your full name, amount donated and the date as a reference to the charity so they can identify the donation/s you have made.

question: How do I stop Donate the Change?
answer:
You can disable Donate the Change any time through the ASB Mobile Banking app:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Donating the Change from.
On your Account Summary, select 'Save the Change setup' under 'More Options'.
Disable Donate the Change using the sliding button.
Select 'Done'.
If you have been using both Save and Donate the Change together, both features will be disabled at the same time.
You can delete Donate the Change from the ASB Mobile app and allow just your Save the Change to continue as normal. To do this:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Donating the Change from.
On your Account Summary, select 'Save the Change setup' under 'More Options'.
Delete Donate the Change by clicking the ‘X’ icon next to the Charity name.
Select 'Done'.

question: Can I get summaries of my interest and tax certificates digitally?
answer:
If you've earned more than NZ$20 worth of interest at the end of the tax year (i.e. 31 March) and you’ve been digitally active by using FastNet Classic internet banking and/or the ASB Mobile banking app in the 180 days before 1 April, you’ll receive a notification that you can download a copy of your Resident Withholding Tax (RWT) certificates digitally. No paper copies will be sent in the mail to digitally active customers. ASB KiwiSaver Scheme annual member statements and tax certificates will also be available online for those that are digitally active and again, no paper copies will be sent in the mail for these members.
RWT certificates will be available from around the end of April.
ASB KiwiSaver Scheme annual member statements and tax certificates will be available from the start of June.
How to access these notifications via FastNet Classic:
In the top right of the screen in the header, you’ll see a number on the bell icon, click on this to open up your notifications, or;
Open the menu in the top left of the page and select ‘Notifications’.
How to access these notifications via ASB Mobile Banking app:
In the top right of the screen in the header, you’ll see a number on the bell icon, click on this to open up your notifications.
From here you can view, download and share your documents.
Please note:
ASB KiwiSaver Scheme members who have closed their account during the year, but still hold other ASB products, will only receive a tax certificate. Also, ASB KiwiSaver Scheme members who are under 18 will receive their statements in the mail.
Customers who have Treasury accounts, including Foreign Currency accounts, will receive the RWT certificates for these products as they currently do today, but may receive the certificate for their other accounts digitally.

question: What is Save the Change and how does it help me save?
answer:
Save the Change is a very handy service that helps you save by rounding up what you spend on selected electronic transactions.  A bit like a modern day money jar, the extra amount is then transferred to the ASB savings account(s) you choose.
You can choose to round:
1. EFTPOS transactions (including Visa Debit if you have one)
or
2. EFTPOS + other electronic transactions including:
EFTPOS
Visa Debit
Bill Payments
Automatic Payments
Direct Debits
One-Off Payment
FastPhone Bill Payments
Contactless Payments (Apple Pay and Google Pay)
You can also set your transactions to be rounded to the nearest multiple of $1, $2, $5 or $10.
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Can I check the progress of my home loan application in FastNet Classic?
answer:
The status of your application is not yet available in FastNet Classic.
However, once your application is submitted online, we will contact you within one business day to gather a few more details. Business hours are Mon-Fri 8am - 8pm, Sat and Sun 9am-5pm. They will then update you on the next steps of the process.
You can also contact us for any further queries on
0800 100 600
(Mon-Fri 8am - 8pm, Sat and Sun 9am-5pm).

question: I’ve hidden my account in FastNet Classic, why is it showing on the balances page again?
answer:
When you hide an account, it will be hidden from both the Balances page and Transfers 'from' drop down box in FastNet Classic internet banking and the ASB Mobile Banking app.
There are a few reasons why your account may automatically become visible again:
If you close all your other accounts and your hidden account is the only account with ASB.
If the balance of your hidden account goes below $0.
If any holds are placed on your other accounts that preclude them from showing in FastNet Classic and the ASB Mobile Banking app.
To check which accounts are hidden:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Select your Profile at the top right of the screen.
Choose 'Hide accounts'.
Tick the accounts you would like to hide and click save.
Hidden accounts will no longer display on the Balances page or in the 'from' drop down box on the Transfers page in FastNet Classic internet banking and the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: Can I set up an automatic payment into my ASB Investment Fund?
answer:
Currently automatic payments are not available. However, you can use a direct debit to set up a regular payment either at the time of application, or, once established, by contacting the ASB Wealth Advisory Service on
0800 108 084
.

question: Why can’t I see my money in the ASB Investment Fund straight away?
answer: It can take 3-5 working days for the transaction to be visible in your ASB Investment Fund. The units will be applied on the day of the transfer but they are not visible until we have completed the transaction.

question: Can I reverse an ASB Investment Fund lump sum transfer?
answer:
Transfers into your ASB Investment Funds cannot be reversed. Learn more about
how you can get your money out of ASB Investment funds.

question: I am going overseas, what happens with my KiwiSaver account?
answer:
Contributions from your salary or wages will stop when you stop being paid.  You can still make voluntary contributions. However, if you no longer mainly live in New Zealand you will not be eligible to receive the annual Government contribution (also known as a member tax credit) unless you are a State Services employee or volunteer working for an approved
organisation
.
If you have any other questions please contact us on 0800 ASB RETIRE (0800 272 738) or
retire@asb.co.nz

question: I need to stop my KiwiSaver contributions, what should I do?
answer:
If you're employed, you can apply for a savings suspension of between three months and one year if you have been contributing to KiwiSaver for 12 months or more.  In circumstances of financial hardship, you may be able to apply earlier.
If you're self-employed or not employed, you can stop contributing at any time.

question: I've just bought a house and don't think I can afford home loan repayments and KiwiSaver contributions, what can I do?
answer:
If your money is getting stretched a little too thin, you can apply for a savings suspension if you have been a KiwiSaver member for 12 months or more (or you are suffering or likely to suffer financial hardship in your first 12 months of membership).
You can make voluntary contributions while on a savings suspension, and continue to receive the annual Government contribution (also known as a member tax credit), if you are eligible.
Watch our video to find out more tips on how you can reduce your debt:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Can I take a break from contributing to my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account?
answer:
If you are an employee, you can apply to Inland Revenue to stop making contributions temporarily if one of the following applies:
12 months have passed since Inland Revenue or any KiwiSaver scheme received your first contribution.  In this case your savings suspension can be for a period of between three months and one year.
you are suffering, or are likely to suffer, financial hardship and Inland Revenue has received at least one contribution from you.  In this case your savings suspension can be for a maximum of three months (unless Inland Revenue agrees otherwise).
You can stop or re-start your savings suspension by giving notice to your employer. However, a savings suspension must be at least three months unless your employer agrees otherwise. You can apply for another savings suspension when one expires.
To apply for a savings suspension, please complete the ‘KS6’ application form, available at kiwisaver.govt.nz and send it to Inland Revenue.
Remember, while you are on a savings suspension, your compulsory employer contributions will stop unless your employer chooses to keep paying them. You can still receive annual Government contributions (if you are eligible) by making voluntary contributions direct to your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account.
If you would like to re-start contributions, complete a ‘KS2’ deduction form (also available from
kiwisaver.govt.nz
) and hand it to your employer.
I’m self-employed, can I take a savings suspension?
You may stop and start any voluntary contributions you are making to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme at any time by contacting us. If you are self-employed and pay yourself through PAYE then you will need to apply for a savings suspension.

question: I am going on paid parental leave, will KiwiSaver contributions be deducted?
answer:
Not from Government paid parental leave.  Any parental leave paid by your employer will be subject to KiwiSaver deductions unless you take a savings suspension.
You can make voluntary contributions while on parental leave.

question: What are the rules around contributions to KiwiSaver if I'm self-employed or not employed?
answer:
If you are self-employed (and are not paying yourself via PAYE) or not employed, you have the flexibility to stop and start your contributions to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme at any time and there is currently no minimum contribution level.
If you wish to make contributions to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme, you can choose to make either
lump sum or regular voluntary contributions
.
Remember that if you wish to receive the maximum
annual Government contribution
, you will need to be an eligible member for the full year (1 July to 30 June) and contribute at least $1,042.86.
If you are self-employed and pay yourself a salary or wages (subject to PAYE tax), you may be treated as an employee and employer for the purposes of KiwiSaver contributions.

question: Can I change my account type in FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
To change an account type in
FastNet Classic internet banking
:
Log into FastNet Classic internet banking
Select ‘Balances’ in the ‘Accounts’ menu
Select the account you’d like to change
When the account page opens choose ‘Change’ next to ‘Account Type’
Select the type of account you’d like to change to
Find out more information on
ASB accounts and cards
.

question: How do I join FastNet Classic internet banking?
answer:
To join
FastNet Classic internet banking
, please call
0800 FASTNET
(0800 327 863) or drop into your local ASB branch. If you are calling from overseas, please call
+ 64 9 306 3185,
and we will set up FastNet Classic access for you.
What will I need in order to use FastNet Classic?
Three things:
An ASB Account.
A FastNet Classic Username and FastNet Classic Password. We will talk you through the set up process when you call the number above.
Access to the internet and a computer with an up-to-date web browser. For a computer running Windows this might be the latest versions of Microsoft internet Explorer, Mozilla Firefox or Google Chrome. If you use an Apple Mac, the latest versions of Apple Safari, Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox will work.
To use Netcode security (optional), you’ll need a TXT-capable mobile phone on a NZ network. Alternatively, you can request a Netcode token.
You won’t need any special software to use FastNet Classic; however we strongly recommend you safeguard your computer with up-to-date security software and operating system updates.
Do I need to use a special code or Password to get into FastNet Classic?
Yes, both.  When you first set up FastNet Classic you can choose your own FastNet Classic Username, and we’ll give you a randomly generated FastNet Classic temporary Password over the phone, or at an ASB branch if you come in. When you Log In to FastNet Classic for the first time, we’ll ask you to change your Password to one of your choice. Once you update your Password to something only you know it will be case sensitive. This is the Password you’ll use from then on, to get into FastNet Classic.  If you want to change your FastNet Classic Password at any time, simply change it online in FastNet Classic.

question: How is my ASB credit card interest calculated and charged?
answer:
Interest is calculated on the daily balance of your credit card account on the last day of your statement. Different interest rates apply to different types of transactions.
There are three types of interest, based on different transactions.
1.
The first is
interest on transactions
where you have purchased goods and services. If you purchase goods or services with your credit card, and you don’t pay the full amount of your statement closing balance by the due date, you’ll be charged interest on the outstanding balance. We also charge purchase interest on offshore service margins, ASB retail exchange margins and balances transferred between existing ASB credit card accounts.
For purchases, you can get up to 55 days interest free on personal credit cards or up to 50 days interest free on business credit cards. If you pay the full amount of your statement closing balance by the payment due date, you will not be charged interest on purchases on that statement. You are charged interest on the portion of the closing balance that is unpaid purchases from two or more statements ago.
If you do not pay the full statement closing balance by the due date, you are charged interest for the statement period.
2
. The second is
cash advance interest
which is charged when you get cash from your credit card account (e.g. a cash advance through an ATM withdrawal or a transfer through FastNet Classic). We also charge cash advance interest on dishonoured payments. These are transactions that were declined by us or another bank for example because you had insufficient funds in your account or your bank account was closed.
You are charged interest on the daily balance of the cash advance or dishonoured payment, from the date of the transaction until the date the amount is paid in full.
3.
The third is interest charged on a credit card balance transferred from another non-ASB New Zealand issued card to an ASB credit card (excluding Visa Business). The balance transfer interest rate will be charged on the balance transferred for the term of the balance transfer. Any outstanding balance at the end of this period will be charged at the applicable standard purchase rate.
You are charged interest on the daily balance of each balance transferred from the date of the balance transfer until the date the amount is paid in full.
For more information, please refer to a copy of the
Credit Card Conditions of Use.

question: Which transactions are excluded from Save the Change?
answer:
Save the Change doesn’t apply on transactions involving:
Credit Cards (including refunded purchases).
Cheques.
Transfers.
Transactions processed in an ASB branch or by our Contact Centre.
All account and service fees (including other bank ATM charges).
Any interest charges and any credit paid into accounts.
All overseas transactions.
Watch our video to find out tips on how you can save money:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Why has the website address for FastNet Classic and Online Share Trading changed?
answer:
The website address for FastNet Classic internet banking and Online Share Trading has changed to support some new features introduced based on customer feedback.
If you have tried visiting FastNet Classic or Online Share Trading from an old bookmark or favourite you might notice we have redirected you to asb.co.nz or asb.co.nz/asb-securities. This is because our online services have a new website address – https://online.asb.co.nz.
We recommend you access our online services by first typing asb.co.nz or asb.co.nz/asb-securities directly into your browser address bar.

question: How do I apply for an ASB Debt Consolidation Loan?
answer:
There are a few ways you can apply for a debt consolidation loan:
Existing customers can
log in to FastNet Classic internet banking
.
If you're not an existing customer you can
apply online
.
Call us on 0800 100 600, seven days a week between 8am-8pm, or visit any
ASB branch
.
ASB's lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: How do I check my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account balance?
answer:
You can check your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account balance by:
Logging in to ASB FastNet Classic internet banking.
Checking on the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Calling our team on 0800 272 738.

question: How do I contact ASB?
answer:
There are a few ways you can contact ASB.
If you are already
an ASB customer and you use FastNet Classic
:
Log in to
FastNet Classic
.
Select 'My Messages' from the Main Menu. Then select 'New Message'.
Complete your query and hit 'Send'. We’ll get back to you within 48 hours.
If you are
