, you’ll find all our
contact details here
including if you’re calling from overseas. If you’d like to set up FastNet Classic but aren’t sure how to go about it, please visit
How do I join FastNet Classic?
If you’re
not an ASB customer
, welcome!  Please visit the
contact section
of the ASB website which has a number of ways to contact us.

question: Can I change my tax rate through FastNet Classic?
answer:
Yes, you can update your tax rate by:
Logging into FastNet Classic internet banking.
Selecting your Profile at the top right of the page.
Choosing 'Update Tax Details' from the menu.
Updating your tax rates.
Selecting Save.

question: Why can’t I log in directly from the home page?
answer:
We have recently made some changes to the way you log in to FastNet Classic and Online Sharetrading as part of some new features introduced based on customer feedback.
In the future, more ASB services will use this style of log in, meaning you’ll only need to remember one set of ASB Login details.
One option to make the login process faster now is Remember My Name. Simply tick this option when you're about to log in from your computer.

question: What is a cookie?
answer:
Cookies are short pieces of data, sent to your machine by the ASB's web server. They help us identify you when you are using an ASB service such as FastNet Classic, FastNet Business or Online Share Trading.
How does FastNet Classic use cookies?
When you log on to FastNet Classic the site sends your browser a memory based cookie. This cookie exists only in your computer's memory.  When you close your browser the cookie in the memory is destroyed. By using a cookie, you only have to Log In to FastNet Classic at the start of your session. The data in the cookie is then used to validate you for every web page you request. This ensures that only you can look at your data. We do not use cookies for any other reason than to ensure that once you have logged on, you are the only user who can view your data.

question: How do I query a credit card charge?
answer:
You can find out more about the rates and fees of
personal credit cards
or
business credit cards
. If you’re still unsure of what the charge relates to, please call our contact centre on
0800 803 804
or contact your nearest
ASB branch
. If you’re overseas, call us on +64 9 306 3000 (feel free to call collect).

question: On my credit card statement, why has the balance transfer amount reduced less than the payment I made this month?
answer:
When a payment is made to an ASB credit card account, the payment is allocated to transaction types according to a hierarchy. If you have undertaken a balance transfer – how your payment is allocated to your outstanding balance may be different to what you expected.  Refer to the 'Allocation of payments' sections in the
Credit Card Conditions of Use
for details.

question: Why is the interest charged on my credit card different to what I expected?
answer:
When a payment is made to an ASB credit card account, the payment is allocated to transaction types according to a hierarchy. If you have undertaken a balance transfer, cash advance or have dishonoured payments – how your payment is allocated to your outstanding balance and the interest charged may be different to what you expected. Please refer to the 'Allocation of Payments' and ‘Interest’ sections in the
Credit Card Conditions of Use
for details.

question: I'm changing jobs, how does that affect my KiwiSaver contributions?
answer: When you change jobs, ask your new employer to start making KiwiSaver deductions.  These will continue to be transferred to the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme via Inland Revenue. If you haven't already joined KiwiSaver, then you are likely to be automatically enrolled when you start your new job.

question: What are the costs of using my Visa Debit card overseas?
answer:
Standard Visa Debit rates and fees apply. It also depends on what currency the transaction is in and the applicable exchange rate.
Find out more about the ASB Visa Debit card
interest rates and fees
.

question: What are the fees to use my credit card online?
answer:
Standard credit card rates and fees apply. This will depend on whether you are making a New Zealand or overseas transaction and what currency the transaction is in. Find out more about the rates and fees of
personal credit cards
or
business credit cards
.

question: Why am I now being charged the standard purchase interest rate on my credit card balance transfer, instead of the balance transfer interest rate?
answer:
Your promotional balance transfer interest rate is for a fixed number of months. Any outstanding balance at the end of the promotional period will be charged at the applicable standard interest rate.
Find out
personal credit card interest rates
.
Find out
Visa Business Rewards interest rates
.

question: Is it safe to use FastNet Classic Mobile?
answer:
Yes. It’s the same security as regular FastNet Classic, which you are familiar with using from your computer. Security measures include password access, Netcode and
reCAPTCHA
. To help with security, get in the habit of logging out of FastNet Classic Mobile yourself, and always keep your PIN number safe and known only to you.

question: Can I add more money to my ASB Cash Fund?
answer:
Yes, you can deposit money into your ASB Cash Fund account at any time for free through
FastNet Classic internet banking
. Alternatively, you can visit any
ASB branch
if you’d like help from ASB staff to make your deposit (service fees will apply).

question: Can I access the money in my ASB Cash Fund account at any time?
answer:
Yes, but you’ll need to have a minimum balance of $500 in your account.
You can access your money either by transferring money from your ASB Cash Fund account to another ASB account, or by making a bill payment through
FastNet Classic internet banking
.
Service fees apply.

question: What is compound interest?
answer:
When you have a term investment, you earn interest on the principal you’ve invested. And when that principal and interest combined earns interest as well, that secondary interest is called ‘compound interest’.
Compound interest can help your investment grow faster because you’re earning interest on your interest.
Compound interest is available on a term deposit or an ASB Term Fund for terms of six months or more, with interest/returns paid quarterly, six-monthly, yearly or on maturity. The compound interest option isn't available when interest/returns are paid monthly.

question: When are returns paid on an ASB Cash Fund account?
answer:
Returns are paid to your ASB Cash Fund account as additional units at the end of each month. The amount of returns you receive depends on your closing account balance on that day.
Interest rates may change at any time.

question: If I have a payment to my credit card pending, will I still be charged interest on the entire outstanding balance?
answer:
If interest is being charged on your credit card, it is incurred on the daily balance.
The daily balance reduces when the payment is credited to your account, not while it is showing as pending, so make your payment before 8:30pm in order for it to be taken into account on the daily balance.

question: What interest will I earn with Save the Change?
answer: You'll earn whatever the interest rate is on the ASB savings account(s) that your savings go into. However, if you nominate someone else's ASB savings account, they'll get the interest.

question: What happens if I only have enough money in my account for whatever I'm buying?
answer: Don't worry, the purchase will still go through. Save the Change calculates your savings at the end of each day and transfers the money overnight.  If you don't have enough money in your account to cover it at the end of the day, Save the Change just won't happen that day.

question: How do I check what Direct Debits have come out of my account?
answer: Direct Debits coming out of your account will show on your account statement which you can see online in FastNet Classic, or on your paper statement if you receive one.

question: What is a debt free date?
answer:
With a debt consolidation loan, you’ll be able to put a ring around a date on your calendar on the day you make the last repayment.  That’s your debt free date, the day you’ve paid off all those debts you rolled into one.
Please
apply here
for an ASB Debt Consolidation Loan.
Please
apply here
, if you are an existing FastNet Classic online banking customer.

question: How much is my current mortgage payment?
answer:
You can check your current home loan repayments and get many other details about your loan in FastNet Classic internet banking or on your smartphone with the ASB Mobile app..
To check your current loan payment in FastNet Classic:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
On the Balances screen, select the loan you want to view.
Check the Instalment Amount on screen. Other information shown includes:
Current Loan Balance
Original Loan Amount
Loan Type
Original Term
Interest Type
Interest Rate
Rate Expiry
Repayment Frequency
Next Instalment Due
Deduction Account Number
To check your loan payment in ASB Mobile:
Log in to ASB Mobile.
On the Accounts screen, select the loan account you want to view. You’ll be taken to a Summary page.
Check the Instalment Amount on screen.
You can also see all same information above.

question: Can I use a joint ASB transaction account for Save the Change?
answer:
Yes you can, but only if your joint account allows either of you to authorise transactions on the account. If your joint account requires two signatures to authorise transactions then it can't be used as the transaction account for Save the Change.
Can I use a joint savings account for Save the Change?
Yes, you can use any joint ASB savings account as a savings account for Save the Change.

question: I have Save the Change set up to round to $1 so how come it transferred more than $1?
answer: Save the Change rounds up all your eligible transactions throughout the day, then at the end of the day, adds up each of the individual rounded amounts and makes one transfer to the nominated savings account(s) overnight.

question: Can I integrate my business loan with Xero and MYOB?
answer:
Yes you can. ASB offers an automated Bank feed between our systems and Xero/MYOB . Because your loan repayments are split into interest and principal, when it comes to the end of your tax year it is very easy to look back and calculate how much interest you can claim back as an expense.
Initiate once online, the bank feed takes effect immediately and can also be applied retrospectively to any point in the last 12 months. It provides full view of your accounts 365 days a year, and is updated at 6.30am every morning.

question: How long does it take for an International Money Transfer to arrive at the other bank?
answer:
In most cases funds will arrive one or 2 business days after they are sent. However, these timeframes are a guide only. If funds haven't been received within 10 business days of being sent we recommend a search be requested by the sender.
You can request a search by calling us on 0800 803 804, sending us a secure message request from FastNet Classic 'My Messages', or by visiting any ASB branch.
Include as much detail as you can from the following:
The amount of the IMT and the currency used
Date the IMT was sent
The IMT reference number (available on your receipt)
The beneficiary name and address, beneficiary account and the beneficiary bank for the IMT

question: Can I cancel a pending transaction in my account?
answer: A pending transaction cannot be cancelled.

question: I've made an extra payment to my credit card? Will my direct debit still make a payment to my card on the due date?
answer:
If you make an extra payment to your credit card that is higher than the amount due on your monthly statement, the direct debit will not run that month.
If you make an extra payment to your credit card less than the amount due on your monthly statement, the direct debit will still run, but only for the difference between the amount due on your monthly statement and the extra payment.
For example, if your amount due is $30.00 and you make an extra payment of $10.00, then the direct debit for that month will be $20.00.

question: I need to apply to get some or all of my KiwiSaver contributions back, what should I do?
answer: In circumstances such as significant financial hardship or serious illness, you can apply for an early withdrawal of some or all of your KiwiSaver savings.  There are strict criteria and you will need to provide evidence.  Call 0800 ASB RETIRE (0800 272 738) for further information.

question: If I select more than one savings account to Save the Change, how much does each savings account receive?
answer:
We try to even things up, so the total rounded amount is split evenly amongst the number of savings accounts you nominate. If the amount can't be evenly split between all accounts, the first savings account listed will receive any additional amounts.
For example, if you choose to share your savings between three nominated ASB savings accounts and your total rounded amount for the day is $3.04, the first savings account listed will receive $1.02 and the other two accounts will each receive $1.01.
When you set up or edit your Save the Change preferences, ensure that the savings account at the top of the list is the account you would want to receive the extra.
How many savings accounts can I Save the Change to?
You can choose up to 10 ASB savings accounts to receive your savings. They can be your own ASB savings accounts, someone else's ASB savings accounts or a combination of both.

question: Save the Change rounds up my transactions, what does rounding up mean?
answer: Save the Change works by rounding up the electronic transactions on transactions you’ve selected, to the nearest multiple of $1, $2, $5, or $10. Here’s how it works:

question: How can I avoid unarranged overdraft fees on my account?
answer:
No-one likes paying bank fees such as an unarranged overdraft fee.
If you go into unarranged overdraft, you can avoid the unarranged overdraft fee and interest charges by transferring money to return the account to credit by 11:30pm that day.
And to avoid fees in future, here are some simple suggestions:
Sign up for FastNet Classic internet banking and/or download the ASB Mobile Banking app. That way you can use
FastNet Classic
or the
ASB Mobile app
to keep track of your accounts any time of the day or night and make transfers to cover any shortfalls.
Sign up for alerts via email and/or notifications. We can send you an automatic alert if your account is overdrawn. In most cases if you act on the alert the same day, you'll have time to fix things up and avoid being charged an unarranged overdraft fee. You also have the option of setting an alert when your account gets down to a limit set by you, giving you the chance to manage your money.
See where your money is going with
Spend Tracker
available on the ASB Mobile app. It automatically categorises your transactions and is a great way to see a monthly view and trends in your spending. Helping you manage your money.

question: What is an NTF fee?
answer:
If you are making an International Money Transfer (IMT), an Other Bank Fee is charged when you choose the “Sender to Pay” option. This fee covers charges from other banks (also known as correspondent, intermediary and beneficiary banks) involved in the payment. This fee will be charged to your ASB account the business day after the payment is sent. Please note, while ASB will send your payment with instructions for the sender to cover all fees and charges, due to the steps involved in the international payment ecosystem, it is possible that the recipient may still have fees deducted from the amount that they receive.
This fee is referenced to your statement as an 'NTF' fee.
If the debit account is a Foreign Currency Account, the fee will be converted to the currency of the debit account at the applicable foreign exchange rate at the time the fee is processed.
Alternatively, you can choose the “Recipient to Pay” option. In this case, you will not be charged an Other Bank Fee, but there will likely be fees deducted from the amount that your recipient receives.

question: How are cash advances charged on my credit card?
answer:
You'll be charged cash advance interest when you withdraw cash from your credit card account (e.g. a cash advance through an ATM withdrawal, bank teller or a transfer through FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app). Cash advance interest is charged on the daily balance of the cash advance, from the date of the withdrawal until the date the amount is paid in full.
Please remember, the cash advance interest rate is higher than the usual purchase interest rate of a credit card.
Check out our
current fees and charges
and
understanding credit card interest
.

question: What is withholding tax?
answer:
Withholding tax is tax paid on interest earned from interest-bearing accounts such as Term Deposits and Savings accounts. ASB deducts this tax before the interest is paid to you. We then pay this tax to Inland Revenue (IRD) on your behalf.
Find out more about the different types of withholding tax:
Resident Withholding Tax (RWT)
.
Non-Resident Withholding Tax (NRWT)
.
There’s also
Approved Issuer levy (AIL)
, which may be paid as an alternative to NRWT.
If your IRD number is included on the IRD’s RWT Exemption Register, then withholding tax may not apply to you.
You should ensure your RWT rate is correct for your level of taxable income. Selecting a rate that is too low may mean you have an end of year tax liability. Selecting a rate that is too high may mean you are paying too much tax. If you are paying too much RWT, you may get a refund at the end of the year from the IRD after assessment of your total taxable income for the year.

question: What is the additional card holder fee on my credit card?
answer:
Additional card fees are charged for each additional card linked to your credit card account. These are charged to your account every six months.
Check our current rates and fees
here.

question: What is the True Rewards fee?
answer:
As of 15 May 2017, there is no True Rewards fee charged on eligible ASB Visa credit cards.
Check our current rates and fees
.
ASB Visa Business Rewards
True Rewards is a standard feature of ASB Visa Business Rewards and there is no fee to participate in the True Rewards programme. However, standard Visa Business Rewards rates and fees apply.
Check out current business rates and fees
.
Watch our video to find out some tips on how credit cards work:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: Why have I been charged a $3 unarranged overdraft fee?
answer:
Rather than prevent a payment going out of your account, we will sometimes allow transactions or payments to go through even though there aren’t enough funds in your account to cover them. This means that your account will become overdrawn. We call this an unarranged overdraft.
If your account becomes overdrawn in this way, we will charge an unarranged overdraft fee of $3. This fee is charged at the beginning of the following month after the unarranged overdraft has occurred.
You can avoid this fee by transferring funds to return the account to credit within the following timeframes:
If your account is overdrawn between 11.30pm (prior day) and 4am, you need to transfer sufficient funds by 11.30pm that same day
If your account is overdrawn between 4am and 11.30pm, you have until 11.30pm the following day to transfer sufficient funds
For information on how to avoid this fee, please visit
how to avoid unarranged overdraft fees
.

question: Does it cost me anything to have an ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account?
answer:
Yes, the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme has charges.
For each ASB KiwiSaver Scheme member these are currently annual fund charges ranging from an estimated 0.35% - 1.00% p.a. as an estimated percentage of the net asset value of the fund. These fees and charges cover the management of the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme and for them you get:
Robust governance, including from the ASB Investment Committee, that supports all investment decisions.
World-class investment managers who are highly experienced with investing money.
Access to digital tools such as FastNet Classic, ASB mobile features, our KiwiSaver calculator and our
Help Me Choose tool.
Research and specific advice from leading industry experts about the construction and management of our funds.
Regular communications that keep members updated about their account and how to make the most of KiwiSaver.
Advice from qualified staff.

question: Can I use my EFTPOS card overseas to withdraw money from an ATM?
answer:
Yes you can. You can use your card in any ATM machine that carries the International Plus symbol.
Please be aware that overseas ATM providers can charge a cash withdrawal fee, the fee varies depending on the provider. ASB no longer charges an overseas cash advance fee but an offshore service margin
applies
. Many people minimise the ATM withdrawal fees by making a few, larger withdrawals rather than lots of little ones, if you are able to do so. However, some countries will limit the amount you are able to withdraw per transaction to smaller amounts.

question: What is the Offshore Service Margin on my credit card statement?
answer:
An Offshore Service Margin is a fee charged by us when you make a transaction in a currency other than New Zealand Dollars.
Overseas transactions will either first be converted into US dollars and then into New Zealand dollars or converted directly at an exchange rate selected by Visa or ASB (as the case may be) from available wholesale rates (or, if applicable, the Government mandated rate).
We charge Offshore Service Margins of 2.10% of the converted New Zealand dollar amount for overseas transactions using your ASB Visa card.

question: What does ‘NTRFFEE’ on my bank statement refer to?
answer:
When you make an overseas transfer from your account, you have the option for any fees relating to the transfer to be charged back to you. When you see this reference on a debit on your account this is one of those other banks’ fees being charged back to you.
Please note that ASB's
Foreign Exchange Terms and Conditions
apply.

question: Is there a fee to transfer my Australian superannuation savings to KiwiSaver?
answer:
We don't charge a fee to arrange the transfer of your Australian superannuation savings into the ASB KiwiSaver Scheme.
You should check with your Australian provider to see if any transfer fees apply before you apply for a transfer. Currency conversion fees may apply.

question: What is the Unpaid or Dishonour Charge on my statement?
answer:
Customers have told us that the statement descriptions for some dishonour fees have not been clear or easily understood in the past, so we have changed these to be clearer.
New wording
Old wording
Unpaid DD Charge
ASB D/D NSF
Unpaid AP Charge
A/P NSF Fee
Unpaid BP Charge
B/P NSF Fee
Unpaid IRDpymt Chrge
IRD NSF Payment Fee
If you use ‘Track My Spend’ to record your monthly transactions, you may need to re-code these fee charges to reflect the new descriptions.

question: Why have I been charged fees on my ASB account?
answer:
Depending on which ASB accounts you have and how you bank, fees on accounts and services vary.  There are different fees for self-service transactions (such as transferring money between your ASB accounts via online banking, TXT banking, FastPhone, ASB ATM’s) and staff-assisted transactions (such as deposits, withdrawals and transfers done in a branch or through the contact centre).
You’ll find a comprehensive guide to our fees and charges here.

question: How much does ASB Mobile app cost?
answer:
ASB Mobile app is free to download and install from the Apple App Store or Google Play. Just search for ASB Mobile. Your mobile service provider will charge the usual data costs as per your agreement with them.
You’ll still pay Standard FastNet Classic transaction and service charges when you use the ASB Mobile app, but the one-time Netcode at registration is free.

question: Why was I charged a late payment fee on my credit card payment?
answer:
You’ll incur a late payment fee if you do not make (at least) the minimum payment due by the date shown on your credit card statement.
The best way to avoid incurring unexpected fees on your credit card account is to set up a Direct Debit or Automatic Payment to your credit card.
You’ll find a comprehensive guide to our fees and charges here.

question: What is the interest rate on my credit card?
answer:
Our credit card interest rates vary, as we issue more than one type of card and the rates can be different between cards.  Also, the interest rate on purchases differs from that on cash advances.
Check out our
current rates and fees
here.

question: What is the account fee on my credit card?
answer:
Credit card fees are charged to your account every six months.
Check the current ASB Visa credit card
rates and fees
.

question: Why is there a 'convenience fee' charged on my ASB credit card?
answer: This is a surcharge that is charged by the Merchant when you use your credit card to make a payment. Customers are advised of the fee via the Merchant's website or at the time that you are making a payment.

question: What fees am I charged for FastNet Business?
answer:
FastNet Business fees are charged on the last day of each month.
To view what FastNet Business fees you may have been charged
- Go into Transaction History
- Click on ‘Fees’
- Select the month / year period
- Select the fee deduction account
- Click view
This will produce a fee summary of the FastNet Business fees charged for your chosen selected month.

question: What is an International Money Transfer (IMT)?
answer:
An International Money Transfer is an electronic transfer of funds in a specific currency and amount, to a friend, relative, or organisation overseas.
The person or organisation you are paying is known as a recipient, payee, or beneficiary.
The bank the funds are being sent to is known as the beneficiary bank.

question: What is the difference between ‘You Send’ and ‘You Receive’ exchanges rates?
answer:
A 'You Send' exchange rate is the exchange rate used when you are sending that foreign currency overseas.
A 'You Receive’ exchange rate is the exchange rate used when you are receiving that foreign currency from overseas.

question: What do I need to know if I'm going overseas temporarily e.g. on holiday?
answer:
If you use the ASB Mobile Banking app then you can let us know your travel plans by using Josie, the ASB chatbot by:
Opening the app.
Tapping 'More' at the bottom right of the screen.
Selecting 'Contact us' from the menu.
Tap 'Tell Josie your travel plans'.
Check out our
information page
before you travel for more details.
Other important questions and answers when travelling:
How can I get in touch with ASB from overseas?
Phone us on +64 9 306 3000 or use the mobile app to message us by:
Opening the app.
Tapping 'More' at the bottom right of the screen.
Selecting 'Contact us' from the menu.
Tap 'Message us directly'.
Can I use Netcode overseas?
If you have a Netcode token, it will work just fine overseas. If you usually receive Netcode by TXT, it will depend on your mobile provider's coverage. With roaming set up, it should be fine, but we cannot guarantee delivery of your Netcode TXT overseas.
Can I get my travel insurance through ASB?
If you've got an ASB Visa Platinum Rewards credit card, you might already have access to overseas travel insurance.
Find out more about
ASB credit card travel insurance
.
You can also call a travel insurance specialist on 0800 925 566 to get it set up.
What if I have payments due whilst I’m away?
If you have internet access, you can still manage all your payments via FastNet Classic internet banking. If you have roaming set up, you may be able to access the ASB Mobile Banking app and make payments. If you are unsure if you will have access to either FastNet Classic or the mobile banking app, you can set up either an automatic payment or bill payment to ensure your payments go through.
How to make a payment using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
How to set up an automatic payment using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
How to make a payment using FastNet Classic internet banking.
How to set up an automatic payment using FastNet Classic internet banking.
Or you can set this up at any
ASB branch
, or by calling us on 0800 803 804.
What should I do if I plan to use my card while I’m away?
It’s helpful to let us know when and where you are travelling to. You can do this by calling our travel line on 0800 925 566 or if you have the mobile banking app, let us know you’ll be travelling and also make sure your ASB Visa credit card is unlocked and you’ve got your ‘In-store international transactions’ setting turned on, using
ASB Card Control
.

question: How do I move my money to New Zealand?
answer:
Sending your funds via electronic transfer (an International Inward Payment) is usually the quickest and most convenient way. To do this, you will need to provide the bank you're sending your money from with your ASB account details, including:
1. Your full account name (called your beneficiary name)
2. ASB’s SWIFT Code/BIC: ASBBNZ2A
3. Your full account number
For a standard ASB transaction or savings account in New Zealand dollars (NZD) it will look like this:
12-3nnn-0nnnnnn-nn.
For an ASB Foreign Currency Account (FCA) it will look like this:
nnnnnnnn-[CCY]-nn
. If the bank you're sending money asks for your IBAN (International Bank Account Number), simply provide your full ASB account number above (either you NZD account or FCA).
If the bank asks for a BSB or Sort Code, then for your NZD account give the first six digits of your full bank account number – these represent the bank and branch codes, i.e. 12-3nnn. For your FCA, providing the ASB SWIFT code and your Foreign Currency Account number should be sufficient.
4. Our name and address, as the bank receiving your money (the beneficiary bank):
ASB Bank Limited
12 Jellicoe Street
Auckland Central
Auckland 1010
New Zealand
You need to be careful that you provide the correct information. We’re not able to check if the account details match the account name when we process a money transfer, so it’s important you double check everything is right first.

question: Once the value of a foreign cheque has reached my account, can it still be dishonoured?
answer:
ASB no longer accepts foreign currency cheques. The information below is provided for customers who deposited foreign currency cheques before 01 July 2024.
Foreign cheques processed 'by negotiation' can still be dishonoured even after they're deposited to your account.
If a foreign cheque is dishonoured, its value will be reconverted to the original foreign currency (using the You Send rate at the time we process the dishonour) and that amount will be deducted from your account. Due to changes in exchange rates, the amount deducted from your account may be more or less than what was originally paid into your account.

question: What do I do with a foreign currency cheque?
answer:
ASB stopped accepting any foreign currency cheques from
31 March 2024.
For more information on these changes, please visit our
Foreign currency cheques
page.

question: Where do I exchange any foreign currency I’ve got leftover from my trip?
answer: ASB stopped buying and selling foreign notes on 2nd July 2021. If you need foreign cash services, you can take your foreign notes to a foreign currency exchange business.

question: Where can I buy foreign currency or change my New Zealand dollars?
answer:
ASB stopped buying and selling foreign notes on 2 July 2021. If you need foreign cash services, you can:
Use your ASB Fastcash Card to withdraw cash at overseas ATMs that display the Plus logo or use your ASB Visa Debit card or ASB Visa Credit card at any ATM displaying the Visa logo.
Use your ASB Visa Debit card or ASB Visa Credit card to make purchases at any of the millions of retailers around the world where Visa is accepted. The ASB Visa Debit and ASB Visa Credit card are also Apple Pay, Google Pay, Fitbit Pay and Garmin Pay ready, giving you more ways to pay while you travel.
Use a pre-paid foreign currency travel card.
Purchase foreign notes from a foreign currency services provider.

question: In what ways can I take money overseas?
answer: You have a variety of options, including debit and credit cards. We recommend you take a few different options while travelling to cover all the bases.

question: Where can I see the latest foreign exchange rates and information?
answer:
The ASB foreign exchange page shows you the top 5 most popular currencies (AUD, USD, GBP, EUR, and JPY) displayed at the top, followed by a number of other currencies ASB may buy or sell.
Go to the
ASB foreign exchange page
.
Or see them on the ASB Mobile Banking app by logging in and tapping 'More' on the bottom right of the accounts page.

question: I’m in the Financial Markets Online module,  what is a value tomorrow trade?
answer:
A value tomorrow FX transaction settles one business day after the trade date.
For other related information, find out more about
value today
and
spot rate trade
.

question: I’m using Financial Markets Online, what is a spot rate trade?
answer:
A spot FX transaction typically settles two business days after the trade date (depending on the market convention for the pair of currencies being exchanged).
For other related information, find out more about
value tomorrow trade
and
value today
.

question: How do I manage my ASB Foreign Currency Account?
answer:
The easiest and most convenient way to manage your Foreign Currency Account is online.

question: What does the value today term mean in Financial Markets Online?
answer:
A same day or a value today FX transaction settles on the same day as the trade date.

value tomorrow trade
and
spot rate trade
.

question: How do I find out how much my money is worth in a foreign currency?
answer:
You can find out what your money is worth in a foreign currency by using our
foreign exchange rate calculator
.

question: Which currencies are available from ASB?
answer:
You can see all the currencies we have available and the current price we will buy and sell those currencies on our
foreign exchange rates
page.

question: How do I open an ASB account if I’m overseas?
answer:
To find out how to open an ASB account from overseas, complete the application form and email
internationalbanking@asb.co.nz
. Our relocation specialists will contact you with within 48 hours and can help you with the application process.

question: How can I keep costs down when I’m converting my money to another currency?
answer:
One way you might help keep costs down is opening a Foreign Currency Account. It allows you to hold foreign currency in a New Zealand account, either on-call or as a term deposit.
If you use ASB internet banking, you can then move money between this account and your ASB account in NZD, when you decide the exchange rate suits you best.
This account could be ideal if:
You receive and make payments in a foreign currency often
You’re a new immigrant to New Zealand
You’re a New Zealander living and working overseas

question: How much can I send as an International Money Transfer (IMT) in Fastnet Classic?
answer: During business hours you can send up to $100,000 in total.  After 5pm you may find this is restricted to a smaller amount.

question: How do I create an International Money Transfer via FastNet Business?
answer:
If you do not have the FastNet International Module, you can still create IMT’s from a NZ$ account up to NZ $250,000.
- Click payments on the top menu
- Left-menu, click international money transfer
1. To Obtain a Foreign Exchange Rate Quote
There are three ways to obtain the foreign exchange rate quote:
I. To show the New Zealand Dollar equivalent of a known amount of foreign currency, click the Transfer NZD to radio button then the currency drop box and select the other currency. In the Amount field, type the specific amount of the other currency.
II. To show the foreign currency's equivalent of a known amount of New Zealand Dollars, click the Transfer NZD radio button, type the specific amount of NZD in the Amount field then click the currency dropbox and select the other currency.
III. To show the cost of a transfer to a NZD account without converting to a foreign currency, click the Transfer NZD without conversion to foreign currency radio button, then type the specific amount of New Zealand Dollars in the Amount field.
2. To Enter the Payee's Bank Details
There are three ways to obtain the payee's bank details.
Option 1: If you know the Swift Code of the payee's bank, click the Enter Swift Code radio button, type the code into the field, then click Search and see Step 3.
Option 2: If you know the National Clearing Code or the BSB of the payee's bank, click the 'Enter National Clearing Code' radio button, type the code into the field, then click Search and see Step 3.
Option 3: If you don't know the Swift code, National Clearing Code, or BSB of the payee's bank, click the remaining radio button then:
• Click the Select Country: dropbox and select the country. (If the country is not listed, click More, then click the Country dropbox again to see more countries.)
• In the City field, type the first few letters of the city name, then click Search.
• Click the Please Select dropbox to show the list of city names returned from the search, then select the city.
• Click the Bank dropbox. If more than one bank is listed in the Search Results pane, use the displayed details to help you identify the payee's bank then see Step 3.
Click the payee's bank name hyperlink (scroll down if necessary).
Result: The International Money Transfer page is re-displayed with the payee's bank details shown and the Payee Details pane.
3. To Enter the Payee Details
In the Payee Details pane, type the payee's name, address, and account number, then click the country dropbox and select the country. You must enter information into fields marked with an *asterisk.
4. To Enter Your Payment Details:
I. In the Your Payment Details pane, if the withdrawal account shown is not correct, click the From Account dropbox then select the correct account.
II. In the Particulars field, enter some details that will help to identify this IMT on your statement. You can also enter more details into the Code and Reference fields, if you want.
III. In the Address field, check that a physical address is entered.
a. Note: PO Box or Private Bag addresses cannot be used when processing an International Money Transfer.
IV. Click Save.
a. Result: Your payment details are saved and the International Money Transfer page is re-displayed with a message confirming that the IMT has been successfully saved.
V. At the bottom of the page, click Cancel to return to the Unsent Payments page and authorise the IMT later, or click Authorise to authorise the IMT now.

question: I'm overseas and want to use my Visa credit card. Do I have to use a PIN, or can I still sign for purchases?
answer: You will still be able to sign for purchases made on your NZ issued Visa credit card while you are overseas.  Please make sure you have signed the back of your card and that the person taking your payment checks your signature for verification when completing a transaction.

question: What is a balance transfer?
answer:
A balance transfer is when you transfer the balance owing from another banks credit card or store card e.g. Visa or MasterCard (any bank except ASB), American Express, Gem by Latitude, Q Card, Farmers, Purple Visa Card or Flight Centre Mastercard to your ASB Visa credit card.
It is ideal when you are looking to pay down your credit card debt or avoid paying higher interest elsewhere, making managing your money easier.

question: Which ASB credit cards can I complete a balance transfer to?
answer: A balance from another banks credit card or store card can be transferred to any ASB Visa credit card except for ASB Visa Business or ASB Visa Flex cards.

question: When will I receive the money paid into my ASB account from another bank?
answer:
Banks in NZ typically send funds throughout the day from 9am to midnight and at least every hour. ASB will credit or debit your account within an hour of a payment being received from another bank.
NZ banks exchange payments between 9am and midnight. If the payment has been made after midnight, funds may not arrive from another bank until the morning of the following calendar day.

question: How long does it take to receive funds from another bank into my ASB account?
answer: Payments will generally be received the same day they were sent as banks will exchange payments throughout the day. This will occur at least every hour from 9am to midnight, every day including weekends and public holidays. However, if the payment has been made after midnight, funds may not arrive and be processed until the morning of the following day. The exact time of day a payment transaction is received by customers, or deducted from an account, can vary between banks. Also, the cut-off times for processing payment instructions from customers can vary between banks.

question: Can I use 100% of my ASB credit card limit when completing a balance transfer?
answer: No, we will only transfer an amount equal to or less than 90% of your available credit limit.

question: What do I do if my superannuation savings are being held by the Australian Taxation Office (ATO) as 'unclaimed super'?
answer:
‘Unclaimed super’ is transferred to the ATO to protect superannuation savings. Before your savings can be moved to your KiwiSaver account, you’ll need to arrange for your savings to be transferred from the ATO to an Australian complying superannuation fund regulated by the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA).
You can search for your lost super:
- online using myGov,
- by phoning the ATO’s lost super search line, or
- by completing the ATO’s paper form.
See
here
for further details.

question: What is an off-market transaction?
answer:
Off–market securities transactions take place between an investor and another party, often a share registry or the company that issued the securities, outside of the formal market (ie the stock exchange). These transactions might include (but are not limited to):
Dividend reinvestment:
This is where an investor does not receive dividends in cash, rather the dividend is automatically reinvested in the company and the investor receives additional shares.
New issue:
A new issue is the expression used when securities (shares or bonds) are offered for sale to the public for the first time.
Off-market transfer:
This is a change of ownership from one name to another, done privately through the share registry. Find out more information on
off market transfers
.
Corporate action:
This is an event initiated by the issuing company that can change the number of shares held by an investor. A dividend is one example of a corporate action. Another example is a share split, where a company splits existing shares into multiple shares at a specified ratio e.g. 2:1. Following a split, an investor will hold 2 shares for every 1 they held prior to the split. The dollar value of the shares held by an investor will remain the same, but they quantity they hold increases.
Off market transactions don’t show automatically in your Online Share Trading portfolio.
Interested in online share trading? Find out more at
ASB Securities
.

question: Will you close the bank/store card once the balance transfer is completed?
answer: No, if you wish to close your bank/store card you will need to contact them yourself.

question: How long will it take for my Australian super to be transferred to my KiwiSaver account?
answer: The time it takes from the start of your application through processing varies between Australian superannuation fund providers. Your Australian provider has 30 days to assess your application and if approved it may take another 30 days to transfer the funds, so it can take up to 60 days.

question: How do I make a transfer using the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Transferring money to and from your ASB accounts using the
ASB Mobile Banking app
is easy. Here’s how:
Log in to the ASB Mobile Banking app
Select ‘Transfers’ from the bottom bar
Scroll to choose the accounts you wish to transfer to and from
Enter the transfer amount, add a description (optional) then tap 'Transfer' to complete
Note:
For applicable accounts you can start the transfer process by selecting the ‘Transfer’ button located on the ‘Summary’ screen.
Learn how to
make payments via the Mobile Banking app

question: How long does it take to complete a balance transfer?
answer: After applying via FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app it should take 1-2 business days for the balance transfer process to be completed.

question: How are payments made to my credit card when I have a balance transfer or Smart Rate purchase?
answer:
When a payment is made to an ASB credit card account, the payment is allocated to transaction types according to a particular order. If you have completed a balance transfer or
Smart Rate purchase
, payments made to your account will be applied to interest, charges, cash advances and purchases (other than Smart Rate purchases) before balance transfers and Smart Rate purchases.
If you have many balance transfers and/or Smart Rate purchases, they are paid in order of highest to lowest interest rate. If they are the same interest rate, balance transfers are paid before Smart Rate purchases.
Please refer to the 'Allocation of payments' sections of the
Credit Card Conditions of Use
for full details.

question: What information do I need to complete a balance transfer?
answer: You will need to know the card number details, the amount to transfer and the financial provider you wish to transfer a balance from.

question: What is the maximum amount and how many balance transfers can I complete per year?
answer: You can transfer a maximum of $20,000 per balance transfer. You can complete a maximum of two balance transfers per credit card account in a calendar year.

question: Will my superannuation savings transferred from an Australian complying superannuation fund be taxed?
answer:
The transfer of Australian superannuation savings to New Zealand is exempt from entry or exit taxes. Different tax rates and rules apply in each country; once your savings are transferred they will be subject to the taxation rules that apply in New Zealand.
Before you decide whether or not to transfer your Australian savings, you should consider any tax implications and seek independent tax advice.
For more information about how your investment is taxed see section 6 ‘What taxes will you pay?’ of the
Other Material Information
document.

question: How can I find out if my Australian superannuation fund has been approved to accept KiwiSaver savings?
answer:
Use
Super Fund Lookup
to check if your Australian provider is regulated by the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA). If listed, you’ll need to confirm if it will accept KiwiSaver savings as providers aren’t required to do this. If you complete a
transfer form
, we can contact your Australian superannuation provider for you.

question: Can I transfer my KiwiSaver savings (including any Australian superannuation) to another KiwiSaver provider in the future?
answer: You can change KiwiSaver schemes however the new provider must be able to accept any Australian savings transferred into your KiwiSaver account. Providers can choose not to do this.

question: What payment types can I authorise on the ASB Mobile Business App?
answer:
IRD Payments
Transfers
Direct Credit (Bulk)
Payroll (Bulk)
Creditors (Bulk)
Credit Cards (Bulk)
International Money Transfers
Same Day Cleared Payment

question: I'm using FastNet Business, how do I transfer money between my accounts?
answer:
You can transfer money from any bank account to which you have transact access rights, into any of your other ASB bank accounts or to your ASB credit card accounts.
Transfers to suffixes within the same account (intra-account requires only one person to authorise. Transfers from an account to a different account number (inter-account) that you can view on FastNet Business, may need authorisation by more than one person. You can save a transfer and email the authoriser(s) so that they can complete the authorisation process from any location they have access to FastNet Business or via the ASB Mobile Business app.
Before you begin, display the 'Create Transfer Page':
On the FastNet Business tab, click 'Transactional Banking'.
Below the tab bar, click 'Payments'.
On the left menue, click 'Transfer'.
To make a transfer now, or in the future:
On the 'Create Transfer' page, use the drop lists to select the relevant From account and To account.
In the Amount field, type the amount to be transferred - no dollar sign is needed.
The Date of transfer field automatically shows today's date. If you want to process the transfer on a future date, click the calendar icon and select any date up to 365 days in the future. The transfer will be processed automatically on the specified date (if it has been authorised and sent).
(Optional) In the Reference fields, type useful notes to appear on your statements.
To authorise the transfer now, see below. Alternatively, if you want to save the transfer for future editing or authorisation, click Save transfer.
The Create Transfer page is re-displayed with a message confirming that the transfer has been created and saved. The transfer has been automatically moved to the Unsent Payments page.
To make a transfer now, or in the future:
At the bottom of the Create Transfer page, click Authorise transfer. The transfer will display on the Authorise Payments page.
Type your User ID (if necessary) and Password then click Authorise payments. If the transfer is fully authorised and you have Send permission, a message informs you that the transfer is ready to send. Otherwise, the Unsent Payments page is displayed with the transfer listed on it.
Click Send payment if you want to send the transfer for processing now. Alternatively, if you want to send the transfer later, click Don't send payments yet.
If sent, the authorised transfer is automatically moved to the Sent Payments page and the Unsent Payments page is re-displayed.
To view and confirm that the sent transfer is being processed, go to the Sent Payments page.

question: ASB's position on sanctions and how our customers may be affected.
answer:
What are sanctions?
Sanctions are foreign policy tools used by governments and international organisations to support national security interests and/or advance foreign policy objectives. The requirements of different sanctions regimes will vary and may be imposed or changed without or at short notice. Those requirements depend on the government or entity administering them, their intended aims, and the individuals, groups, entities, vessels, and activities they target. Measures commonly imposed include the following:
Financial restrictions
Asset freezes
Travel bans
Targeted arms embargos, and/or
Import/export bans on certain commodities.
ASB has a Sanctions Policy in place to ensure ASB's compliance with sanctions requirements. This Policy includes prohibitions on carrying out transactions or providing services that may violate sanctions. ASB complies with a number of national and international sanctions authorities including but not limited to:
The United Nations Security Council
New Zealand (Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade)
Australia (Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade)
United States (Office of Foreign Assets Control)
The European Union
United Kingdom (Office of Financial Sanctions Implementation).
How could you be affected?
ASB is committed to ensuring it complies with applicable New Zealand and international sanctions obligations. ASB checks transactions facilitated by it for sanctions compliance. To ensure that ASB and its customers meet sanctions requirements, ASB undertakes due diligence on a wide variety of things, including:
ASB's customers
Trade transactions, including use of controlled/dual use goods
International payments.
When ASB is completing the required sanctions due diligence, it may result in extended processing times for some customers' transactions. As part of ASB's due diligence process, customers may need to provide additional information including, but not limited to, purpose of payment, supporting documents, and additionally for businesses, specific information on ownership relating to counterparties or underlying parties.
ASB strives to make all transactions including international money transfers as requested. However, depending on applicable domestic or international sanctions and ASB’s risk appetite, ASB may:
require that you provide more information for due diligence purposes
not process your transaction(s)
be required to block or freeze your transaction(s) or asset(s) for a determinate or indeterminate length of time
reject your transaction(s)
not extend credit or provide services
or take other action as it considers necessary to meet its obligations.
ASB will let you know if it cannot process your transaction or if it requires further information from you. ASB is unable to guarantee that your transaction will be processed.
ASB will not process certain transactions
ASB will not process any transactions prohibited by sanctions laws or its policies. ASB will also not process transactions where ASB is unable to obtain sufficient information confirming that the transaction does not contravene applicable sanctions and/or its policies. Certain countries/territories are subject to comprehensive sanctions and ASB will not facilitate most transactions involving the following countries/territories:
Cuba
Iran
North Korea
Syria
Ukraine disputed/occupied regions (Crimea, Sevastopol, Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia and Kherson).
In addition, ASB will not facilitate commercial and trade finance transactions involving Afghanistan, Myanmar, certain regions of Ukraine, Venezuela, Russia or Belarus. Personal remittances involving these jurisdictions will only be considered following due diligence and approval processes. However, please note that ASB will not facilitate personal remittances with a connection to Russia. Exceptions may only be considered in very limited circumstances and subject to enhanced due diligence. Even where transactions are allowed, delays to payment processing should be expected.
International sanctions are often implemented or changed at short notice in reaction to significant world events, for example, acts of terrorism. Please note that the above list of countries to which sanctions apply may be subject to change without notice.
The information found on this webpage is solely for information purposes. ASB is unable to advise you on how your transactions can be processed in line with applicable domestic and international sanctions. You are encouraged to obtain independent legal advice and discuss your needs with ASB to minimise the risks of interruptions or delays.
To find out more, e-mail
ASBOperations@asb.co.nz
.

question: Can I make a payment to an international account using ASB Mobile?
answer:
Not currently. To send money overseas, please use the International Money Transfer functions (IMT) in FastNet Classic.
For more information see
'How do I make an International Money Transfer?'

question: Why should I provide beneficiary address details on an international money transfer (IMT)?
answer:
Providing the full and valid physical address of the beneficiary of an IMT can sometimes assist in the funds being correctly transferred if the account number provided is incomplete or invalid. Many overseas banks now request this or they may reject the payment.
ASB is unable to complete validation on any of the beneficiary account details provided by you. It’s your responsibility to ensure these details are correct before making a payment.
An IMT is treated like an electronic payment; they’re usually sent to an account number only with no manual intervention.
Name and address information provided is usually only used by the beneficiary bank if the payment needs to be applied manually due to incomplete or invalid account number information.
Find out more information on
how to make and International Money Transfer
.

question: What are the International Money Transfer cut off times for FastNet Business?
answer:
For same day processing, the cut-off time for FastNet Business international money transfers is 3.00 pm New Zealand time.
Transactions received by the bank after 3.00 pm will be processed on the following business day.

question: Can I automatically pay off my credit card each month from my transaction account?
answer:
Yes. You can pay off your credit card in full each month from any transactional account you wish to use. If you always pay the full amount of your statement closing balance on or before the payment due date every month, you won't pay any interest, unless you take a cash advance.
Check our guide for more information on how to
manage your credit card payments
.

question: Why can't I add, alter or delete any automatic payments, bill payments or one-off payments?
answer:
Our system updates overnight from 10pm to approximately 3am the next morning.
During this time, you can:
View upcoming bill and automatic payments you have previously scheduled.
Create a new scheduled bill payment (
note: this newly scheduled payment may not appear in your list of upcoming payments until the next calendar day, on either FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile app
).
Make a one-off payment
(note: whilst your balance will update immediately, this payment may not appear in your transaction history
until the next calendar day, on either FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile app
).
Make a transfer
(note: whilst your balances (to and from accounts) will update immediately, the transfer may not appear in your transaction history
until the next calendar day, on either FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile app
).
During this time, you will be unable to:
Add, edit or delete automatic payments.
Edit or delete scheduled bill payments.
Save a new payee.
Note
: When a regular Automatic Payment is set up, only the next (one) upcoming payment in the series will display in the View Upcoming Payments pages.

question: What are the processing times for automatic payments?
answer: Automatic Payments can be processed at any time during each calendar day. This includes payments to non ASB accounts, and payments that fall on a weekend or public holiday. If there’s not enough money in the account, we’ll try the payment again in the evening.

question: What information do I need to put in the Particulars, Code and Reference fields when I am making an Automatic Payment, Bill Payment or One-Off Payment?
answer:
The Particulars, Code and Reference fields allow you to enter information that you want to appear on your statement and/or your payee's statement, so you (and they) know what the transaction is.
The information you enter in these fields is your choice, just note the Particulars, Code and Reference in each field limits you to 12 characters. Some companies may ask you to enter specific information in these fields for their statement; such as an invoice or customer number.

question: How do I remote authorise an Automatic Payment via FastNet Business?
answer:
You can now remotely authorise Automatic Payments in FastNet Business web.
You can do this via the Unsent Payments page.
Navigate to the Unsent Payment page via the left hand menu. Automatic payments will display within the list of payments with a status of ‘In progress’.
To authorise automatic payments select one, or more, of the check boxes displayed. Complete all relevant User ID, password and Netcode fields. Click Authorise payments. A message will display informing you that the automatic payment was successfully created.
Modified and deleted Automatic Payment requests must be fully authorised before 8pm the day prior to payment. If this does not happen, the request will expire and must be requested again via the main Automatic Payment page.
Note:
Remote authorisation using FastNet Business mobile is not yet available. However, you are able to view outstanding authorisations in the Unsent Payments page in FastNet Business mobile.
Note:
Newly created automatic payments, that are not fully authorised, cannot be edited. They must be deleted from Unsent Payments and recreated from the main Automatic Payments page.

question: How do I edit an automatic payment in FastNet Business?
answer:
To edit an automatic payment:
Log in to FastNet Business
At the top of the page, click on ‘Transactional Banking’
Choose ‘Payments’
On the left-menu click ‘Automatic Payment’
On the Automatic Payments page, select the relevant automatic payment you want edit.
Note:
Only automatic payments that use a withdrawal account that you have access to will be displayed.
Click the Edit button
Modify the relevant details
Note:
If you want the automatic payment to recur indefinitely, leave the Final Amount and Final Date fields empty. If you want the payments to stop on a particular day, complete the Final Date field. If you want the final amount to differ from the regular amount, complete the Final Amount field.
Note:
Not all fields and drop-lists can be modified. If you wish to modify one of these fields, you will need to delete the automatic payment and add a new automatic payment.
Click Save & Authorise
Once saved a confirmation message will display directing you to the Unsent Payment page for final authorisation.
Navigate to the Unsent Payment page via the left hand menu. Automatic payments will display within the list of payments with a status of ‘In progress’.
To authorise automatic payments select one, or more, of the check boxes displayed. Complete all relevant User ID, password and Netcode fields. Click Authorise payments. A message will display confirming the automatic payment was successfully created.
Note:
Modified and deleted automatic payment requests must be fully authorised before 8pm the day prior to payment. If this does not happen, the request will expire and must be deleted and recreated from the main Automatic Payment page.
Note:
Newly created automatic payments, that are not fully authorised, cannot be edited. They must be deleted from Unsent Payments and recreated from the main Automatic Payments page.

question: I'm using FastNet Business, and I’ve exceeded the daily limit for payments – what do I do?
answer:
When you cannot send a payment because you have exceeded a daily limit, FastNet Business will display a helpful message:
An information message (shown in blue) if you may - or have already - exceeded a daily limit
An error message (shown in red) when a payment cannot be sent because you have exceeded a daily limit
If possible, delay sending the payment until the next day when the daily limits are reset.
If you need to send the payment today, read the error message carefully to see which type of daily limit you are exceeding:
User Limit
Account or Division Limit
International Limit (for international payments)
When you know which type of daily limit you are exceeding, use the following options to try and send the payment successfully.
If you're exceeding a User Limit, you can
increase the User Limit.
Alternatively, you may be able to:
Request a payment release. Email a copy of your batch printout, with instructions to release. This must be signed by your account mandate (i.e. if a two to sign account, two signatories must sign). Please email to asb.FastNetBusinessHelpdesk@asb.co.nz. Payment releases will be actioned within 1 hour and you will receive confirmation once done
Ask another User to authorise the payment
If you're exceeding an Account or Division Limit you can ask the FastNet Business Helpdesk to
increase the limit.
Alternatively, you may be able to:
Request a payment release. Email a copy of your batch printout, with instructions to release. This must be signed by your account mandate (i.e. if a two to sign account, two signatories must sign). Please email to
asb.FastNetBusinessHelpdesk@asb.co.nz
. Payment releases will be actioned within 1 hour and you will receive confirmation once done.
Pay from a different account.
Exclude non-urgent payees.
Change the payment date.
If you're exceeding an International Limit on an International Payment
If you don't have the FastNet Business International module, your overseas payments made with basic International Money Transfers (IMTs) cannot exceed the daily limit of your approved/account limit or a maximum of $250,000.
If you need to send the payment today, please contact your ASB Branch or Relationship Manager.
If you regularly exceed this limit, we recommend that you contact the FastNet Business Help Desk on 0800 22 55 27 between 8.00am-6.30pm, Monday to Friday, and ask about the International module.

question: Can I use my mobile number to shop online?
answer:
Yes, with Online EFTPOS you can use your mobile number to shop securely online at participating stores, using cash in your ASB (transaction or savings) account.
You can receive and approve payment requests, confirm which account the payment is coming from and approve your transactions.
To use Online EFTPOS, you’ll need to download the latest version of the
ASB Mobile Banking app
, then:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap 'Settings' and under ‘Devices’ select 'Online EFTPOS'.
Tap the selector to activate Online EFTPOS payments.
If Online EFTPOS is available on the website that you are shopping on, there will be a ‘Pay by Online EFTPOS’ button at the checkout/payments page, next to the credit card or internet banking button.
You’ll then need to do the following things:
Click the ‘Pay by Online EFTPOS’ button.
Enter your mobile number (make sure it’s the same one you used to register with the ASB Mobile app).
Go to your ASB Mobile app, confirm the default account you want to pay from (or select another).
Authorise the payment.

question: I'm in FastNet Business, can I make same day cleared payments?
answer:
A Same Day Cleared Payment is a single payment to another New Zealand bank account and commonly used for major purchases eg: for property purchases, where the person receiving the payment requires a payment confirmation on the same business day.
Useful if you need to make an urgent payment to a non-ASB bank account.
The money would be credited to the account you're transferring within an hour.
Same day cleared payment cannot be reversed and a fee is charged.
You can send a Same Day Cleared Payment to any participating New Zealand bank between 9.00am and 4.30pm on a business day online via
FastNet Business
or through a branch where the service will be available only till 2.30pm given processing requires manual activities.
Fee applied for electronic transactions is available on
FastNet Business internet banking fees & costs | ASB
where for manual transaction via the Branch is $30.
If you want to urgently pay funds from your ASB account to another ASB Account, we recommend that you use the one-off payment via internet banking instead, as these transfers happen instantly and incur no fees.
NOTE: below is further detailed on
7-day payment processing - Better payments every day with ASB
Property settlements mostly still use
Same-Day Cleared Payments
which process on business days. 7-day payment processing gives you more flexibility and can be used for payments any day of the week where Same Day Cleared Payments are not available on weekends. The payment method to use would be your choice and the choice of other parties involved in the settlement.
Same Day Cleared Payments provide a confirmation to all parties that the transaction has been processed whereas payment confirmation is not available when transferring funds via 7-day payment processing.
What are the participating banks for Same Day Cleared Payments? - ASB Help

question: How is interest paid on my Savings Plus Monthly account?
answer:
We pay you a base rate of interest regardless of the activity on your account. We calculate interest on a daily basis and pay it monthly by crediting it to your account (
see the current rates
).
You may earn additional interest as a reward at the end of each month, depending on the number of withdrawals you make and when it was made during that month. A "month" means the period from the first day of the calendar month until the last day of that month, inclusive. Reward interest is calculated on the daily closing balance of your account.
You can ask to withdraw all or part of your account balance at any time. The first withdrawal during the first day of the month will not affect your ability to earn reward interest. However, any subsequent withdrawals on the first day of the month or withdrawals outside of this period will mean you do not earn reward interest for that month.

question: What is Visa contactless and why should I use it?
answer: Visa contactless is a faster way to pay with your credit or debit card for purchases under $200 at participating stores. Instead of swiping or inserting your card at the checkout, simply wave or tap your card on the stores contactless terminal.

question: How will I know if my card has contactless capability?
answer: If your Visa credit card has the contactless logo on it then it has contactless functionality. Check for this logo:

question: How do I know what shops I can use my contactless card at?
answer:
If an EFTPOS terminal has the contactless logo, you will be able to pay through contactless.
Just wave or tap your card to the contactless terminal and once there are green lights flashing, the transaction will be complete.

question: Can I top up or extend my existing ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
Yes you can, subject to our lending criteria. In fact many of our customers do this for a new purchase or travel or even home renovations.  At the moment the application form for topping up your personal loan is the same as taking out a new loan, but we’re working on improving that process for our existing personal loan customers.
If you have been making the loan repayments and managing your accounts within their limits, we’d welcome your top-up application.
Please log in to FastNet Classic and
apply here
to top up your existing loan; our usual lending criteria will apply.
ASB’s lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: How do I pay a police infringement in FastNet Classic?
answer:
You can pay a police infringement notice by setting up a Bill Payment.
If you incur another police infringement, you will need to edit the Police Infringement Bureau payee details and change the infringement number (reference details) before making the payment.
To set up or edit Bill Payment Payee details for the Police Infringement Bureau:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Click the 'Make a payment' button on the balances page, then 'Bill payment'.
From the drop down box, select the account that you want to load the Bill Payment Payee against.
Click on 'New Bill Payment Payee'.
Search for Police Infringement Bureau in the Registered payee list.
Select the name 'Police Infringement Bureau' from the search results.
Enter the details to appear on their statement – you will need the notice number, your vehicle registration and your driver licence number.
Enter the details to appear on your statement.
Select ‘Submit’ then "yes" to confirm set up of the Bill Payment Payee.
Once you have set up or edited the Bill Payment Payee details for the Police Infringement Bureau, you can make the payment:
Click the 'Make a payment' button on the balances page, then 'Bill payment'.
From the drop down box, chose the account you will make the payment from.
Click the ‘Make a Payment’ button.
Chose ‘Police Infringement Bureau’ from the list of payees.
Select the account you want to make the payment from.
Select the date and enter the amount.
Click ‘Add Bill Payment’.
Check the date and amount before clicking ‘Yes’ to confirm the payment.
There is no set up fee when the payment is set up via FastNet Classic online banking.
Alternatively, this can also be set up via our contact centre by phoning
0800 803 804
(or +64 9 306 3000) or at any
ASB branch
.
Please note
: A $5 fee will apply when setting up via the contact centre or ASB branch.

question: How and when do I receive the proceeds for shares I have sold?
answer: We can pay proceeds from sale directly to your ASB Securities Cash Management Account (CMA) or Foreign Currency Account (FCA). We will deposit these on the third business day after your order has traded.

question: Can I delete a pending Visa payment?
answer:
Unfortunately we are unable to reverse a pending transaction, as the merchant has already received authorisation to process it. If the transaction is not authorised by yourself, we recommend contacting the company directly and asking them to reverse the payment. If you are unable to make contact or do not know who they are, you can
cancel and replace your card
in FastNet Classic internet banking or via the ASB Mobile app.
Once the transaction has been processed, we can then dispute the transaction/s.

question: What is Klarna?
answer: Klarna is a global leader in shopping and payment services that’s recently launched in New Zealand. Shop now and pay in 4 instalments from online retailers with the Klarna app. There are no upfront fees or interest when the payment is made on time (standard ASB account fees and interest will apply where applicable).

question: How do I disable/turn off the contactless option on my card?
answer:
If you have a Visa credit or debit card and the ASB Mobile Banking app, you can turn off contactless transactions using
ASB Card Control.

question: Why can't I set up a new bill payee?
answer:
Our apologies if you are currently experiencing a 'Service Unavailable' error when setting up a new bill payee in FastNet Classic internet banking. Customers are experiencing this error when their account name contains special characters such as "+", "?" and "&".
Our team are currently working on a resolution to this issue.
In the meantime, you can still set up a new bill payee using one of the following workarounds:
Use the ASB Mobile Banking app to add new payee
Choose the add/save payee option when using the Make a Payment (to new payee) function
Note:
This issue does not impact existing bill payees. Once a bill payee is setup, payments can be made as normal.

question: What are the participating banks for Same Day Cleared Payments?
answer:
You can make same day cleared payments to participating New Zealand banks between 9.00am and 2.30pm on any business day.
Use the following bank codes in the Bank field of the To Account:
Bank
Bank Code
ANZ
01, 06, 11
ASB*
12
BNZ
02
Citibank
31
HSBC
30
ICBC
10
Kiwibank
38
TSB
15
Westpac
03, 13-14, 16-24, 27
Bank of China
88
* If the same day cleared payment is to another ASB account, we recommend that you use another payment method such as a one-off payment or a bulk payment. These payment methods, when authorised before 8.00pm on any business day, ensure that a successfully processed payment is deposited on the same business day.

question: Can I apply for a mortgage repayment deferral?
answer:
A mortgage repayment deferral means that the regular repayments to your loan are stopped for a period of time. You can take up to a 3 month deferral from making payments but there must be a minimum of 12 months between the expiry of a mortgage repayment deferral and the beginning of another. Provision of a mortgage repayment deferral is subject to meeting specific lending criteria and terms and conditions.
Interest is still charged and is added to the loan. This means your loan balance will increase and you'll likely pay more interest overall. After the mortgage repayment deferral ends, the repayments will likely be higher unless the loan term is extended.
We recognise that life is always changing, but ASB is here to help you through those times of financial pressure.
Find out more about
ASB's commitment to customers in financial difficulty
.

question: What is Online EFTPOS?
answer:
Online EFTPOS is a secure, convenient way to pay online from your ASB transaction or savings account controlled from within the ASB Mobile Banking app. You don’t even need a payment card; all you need is a mobile phone number.
It’s only available on websites who are registered and approved by Worldline and payments can only be authorised by you from within the ASB Mobile app which is protected by your password, five digit PIN, fingerprint or facial recognition.
Online EFTPOS purchases are subject to a daily (calendar day) limit of $30,000 and transactions appear instantly on your ASB account statement.
There’s usually no charge for using Online EFTPOS, this depends on the website you purchase from and any transaction fees should be displayed when you’re entering your payment details.
Online EFTPOS only works when using the
ASB Mobile app
.

question: What's the difference between using Online EFTPOS, PayPal or a credit or debit card for online payments?
answer:
Online EFTPOS uses the registered mobile number for your ASB Mobile Banking app which;
Can be easier to remember than card details or username and password.
Is secure within the ASB Mobile app.
Is safer to share because payment requests need to be confirmed using the ASB Mobile app.

question: Can I set up a new bill payee using the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Yes, to set up a new bill payee using the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
From the menu, tap 'Payments'.
Tap 'Make a Payment.'
You may be asked to enter your PIN to continue.
Tap 'Choose who to pay', then 'Pay someone new'.
Enter the payee details, then select 'Check payee details'.
We'll perform a
Confirmation of Payee
check, show a response and then you can choose whether to go ahead with the payment or to edit the payee details.
Select 'Save this payee' so you have the account details if you need to pay them again in the future.
Enter the amount, the frequency of the payments and any reference details if required then tap 'Pay'.

question: Can I use my Garmin Pay Wallet if I report my ASB Visa card has been lost or misplaced?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using your Garmin Pay Wallet from the next day.
If you currently use your Garmin Pay Wallet, when you call ASB to report your physical card as lost, your physical card will be deactivated and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated in your Garmin Pay Wallet overnight and you will be able to use your Garmin Pay Wallet on your device from around 5 am the next day.
You can
lock and replace your ASB Visa card
using ASB Card Control in the ASB Mobile Banking app or
replace your card
using FastNet Classic internet banking.

question: I'm in FastNet Business, how do I get a payment release?
answer:
You can ask the FastNet Business Help Desk to release Bulk payments, Same Day Cleared Payments, Bulk IMTs and Transfers on your behalf:
This is the best option if you do not have other authorisers with sufficient User Limits and if you can get the required signatories' approval today.
To request a payment release:
