Check that the payment is fully authorised and that you have attempted to send it for processing (you have clicked the Send payments button).
Print a summary of the payment (from within FastNet Business).
Write your instructions on the printout (e.g. Please release this payment as we have exceeded our limit).
Write a contact name and phone number and/or email address on the printout so that the FastNet Business Help Desk knows who to contact regarding this payment.
Ask the account signatories for the account from which the payment is being deducted to sign the printout (e.g. a two-to-sign account requires two signatories to authorise the payment release).
Send the completed form to the FastNet Business Help Desk by fax 09 337 2102, or scan and email it to
FNBHelpDesk@asb.co.nz
between 8:00am and 6:30pm, Monday to Friday.
Please allow around an hour for your payment release. The FastNet Business Help Desk will contact you by phone or email when it’s done.

question: I'm using FastNet Business, how do I increase an Account or Division Limit?
answer:
You can request a permanent change to an Account Limit or to an International Division Limit. This is the best option if you regularly exceed either of these limits.
To request a change:
Complete a
Limit Change Request
form.  This form must be signed by the account signatories (e.g. a two-to-sign account requires two signatories to authorise the new limit).
Send the completed form to the FastNet Business Help Desk by email to
FNBHelpDesk@asb.co.nz
between 8.00am - 6.30pm, Monday to Friday.
Please allow around an hour for your payment release. The FastNet Business Help Desk will contact you by phone or email when it’s done.

question: How do I import a Direct Credit bulk payment file from Xero to FastNet Business?
answer:
On 16 November 2023, Xero removed their direct payment link feature.
If you are a Xero customer who previously set up payments this way, you can follow these steps to create and export (download) a payment file in Xero, then import (upload) directly into FastNet Business for approval.
This change does not impact ASB account transaction feeds to Xero.
To create a payment file in Xero:
Visit the
Pay multiple bills article on Xero Central
for the steps to create a payment file.
From the Xero Batch Payment page, you can choose to export (download) the batch file.
Ensure that the file's due date is today or up to 60 days in the future. To import an ASB formatted file, the file's withdrawal account must be an account that you have access to.
For support creating a payment file in Xero, please check out
Xero Central
or raise a case to Xero Support.
Next, display the Import Files page in FastNet Business:
Once you've downloaded the payment file from Xero, you don't need to open it. Simply upload it into FastNet Business.
Log in to FastNet Business and display the Import Files page. To do this:
1. On the FastNet Business tab bar, click Transactional Banking
2. Below the tab bar, click Import/Export Files
3. On the left-menu, click Import Files
To Import a Direct Credit File in FastNet Business:
1. On the Import Files page, select the Direct Credit option, then click OK.
• Result: The 'Import Direct Credit File' page is displayed.
2.  Click Choose File.
3. Locate the relevant file, then click Open.
• Result: The Choose File window is closed and the file path of the file is displayed in the Path and Filename field.
4. Click Import.
• Result: The file is imported successfully and its details are displayed.
5. Click Save & Exit.
• Result: The Import Files page is displayed; ready to import another file.
• Next step: From the Unsent Payment page, review and approve the payment.

question: What are the processing times for outgoing bill payments?
answer: Outgoing bill payments in FastNet Classic and the mobile app are processed up until ASB goes offline for end of night processing. Once the bank is offline your bill payment will be processed the following calendar day.

question: What is a Direct Debit and how do I set one up?
answer:
A Direct Debit is a type of payment authority that gives permission for another person, business or company (which is then called the 'Direct Debit Initiator') to deduct money from your ASB bank account. You can have more than one Direct Debit set up.
To set up a Direct Debit, you must provide authorisation for the Direct Debit Initiator to debit your bank account. You can do this in one of two ways:
You can provide authorisation directly to the Direct Debit Initiator -
the Initiator should let you know in advance how much your bill is and when your payment is due. They will then automatically deduct that amount from your ASB account on the due date.
You can provide ASB with a completed Direct Debit Authority Form -
you can get this form from the Direct Debit Initiator. Complete the form and drop it off at any
ASB branch
to be processed.
For ASB Credit Cards:
You can set up a Direct Debit to make regular payments to your ASB Credit Card. You can select whether you want to pay the minimum amount due, a percentage of the account balance, a fixed amount or the total credit card account balance.
To set up a Direct Debit for your ASB Credit Card, you will need to do the following:
ASB Bank Account
– if the deduction account is with ASB and you are a signatory, you can send a secure message via FastNet Classic internet banking with the required information. Alternatively, you can call the ASB Contact Centre on 0800 803 804 or complete the
ASB Credit Card Direct Debit Authority Form
and hand this into any
ASB branch
.
Non-ASB Bank Account
– if the deduction account is with another bank within New Zealand, please complete the
ASB Credit Card Direct Debit Authority Form
and hand this into any
ASB branch
.
A Direct Debit Authority will remain loaded on your ASB bank account or Credit Card until either you or the Direct Debit Initiator cancel it.
See
How do I stop, cancel or delete a Direct Debit Authority?
for more information.

question: How can I donate to a charity?
answer:
It's simple to donate to a charity.
Set up an automatic payment to a charity from your bank account.
Find out how to
set up automatic payments on FastNet Classic internet banking
.
Find out how to
set up automatic payments through the ASB Mobile Banking app
.
You’ll need to have the charity’s bank account details handy, which you should be able to find on their website.
Add the charity as a bill payee, then make a payment. Their details will be saved for any future donations you want to make.
Find out how to
add a bill payee in FastNet Classic internet banking
.
Find out how to
add a bill payee through the ASB Mobile Banking app
.
Or get in touch with your charity directly to set up a regular donation or a one-off payment. You can do so through their website or give them a call.
They can set up a direct debit for you – which means the money will be taken out of your bank account on a date you’ve agreed on. You could also make donations using your ASB Visa Debit or credit card. The charity will be able to set this up for you.

question: Can I do a contactless transaction when the amount is over $200?
answer:
Yes, in New Zealand you will be prompted to enter your PIN for any transactions over $200. Other countries may have different contactless rules; please contact either
MasterCard
or
Visa
for more information.

question: If I need a PIN for Visa credit card transactions, what about contactless transactions?
answer:
For contactless transactions under $200, you can still tap your card without the need of entering your PIN.
For transactions over $200 you can still tap your card, but you will be prompted to enter a PIN to complete the transactions. Contactless transactions are protected by multiple layers of security and meets all the same security standards as traditional chip cards.
Find out more information about making
contactless payments
.

question: Do I need to use my PIN when I use Garmin Pay?
answer:
Garmin Pay works like all contactless Visa Debit and Visa credit cards, so there's no need to enter a PIN when paying for things under NZ$200. Payments over NZ$200 will require you to enter your card PIN at the contactless terminal.
For added security, you will need to enter your Garmin Pay passcode every 24 hours or after every time you put your watch back on after taking it off.

question: Can I increase the payments on my ASB Personal Loan?
answer:
Yes. The more you pay with each instalment, the faster you'll pay off your loan and the less interest you'll pay. So, if you can comfortably increase your repayments permanently without putting yourself under financial pressure, it's a smart option and it's free to do.
You can do this by sending a secure email request through My Messages in FastNet Classic internet banking, visiting any ASB branch or by calling 0800 272 662. You may be required to sign a document in branch as this may need to be witnessed.
You also have the option to
make additional payments
that are one-off on your ASB Personal Loan too.

question: What happens when using dual access for contactless payments?
answer: Any contactless transactions processed on a credit card will debit the credit card account. If you want to pay for a purchase from your transaction or savings account you will need to select chq or savings on the terminal.

question: How and when do I pay for shares I have bought through ASB's share trading service?
answer:
You can pay either with funds from your ASB Securities Cash Management Account (CMA) or Foreign Currency Account (FCA). Please make sure you have sufficient funds in either of these accounts before you place any orders.
For buy orders, we’ll withdraw the funds from the account you’ve selected approximately two business days after your order has traded.

question: Can I delete a bill payment that has been set up for a future date in FastNet Classic internet banking or the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Yes, to delete a bill payment via FastNet Classic:
Log into Fastnet Classic.
Select 'Make a payment'.
Select 'Bill Payments'.
Select the nominated bank account that the payment is to be deducted from.
Select 'View'.
Select 'Delete Payment'.
Choose the payment you wish to delete and select 'Delete Payment'.
To delete a bill payment via the ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
From the menu, tap 'Payments'.
Tap on the 'Upcoming payments' button.
You may be asked to enter your PIN to continue.
Tap the payment you wish to delete. The details of the current payment will be shown.
Tap 'Delete payment' at the bottom of the screen.

question: What happens to my Klarna payments when I return an item?
answer: You can report returns right in the app and Klarna will pause your purchases, so you only pay for what you keep.

question: Why am I having a problem adding my card to Klarna?
answer:
Check to make sure your card is active. If it's not you can activate it via the ASB Mobile Banking app or FastNet Classic internet banking.
Find out
how to activate an your credit card or Visa debit card
.

question: How secure is Klarna?
answer: Payment information is processed securely by Klarna. All transactions take place via secure connections, protected with the latest industry standard security protocols, including computer safeguards and secured files. They also maintain other physical, electronic, and procedural safeguards to protect your information.

question: Which ASB cards can I use with Klarna?
answer: You can add an ASB Visa credit card or an ASB Visa Debit card to Klarna with the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: How can I make a payment to my ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account on the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
You can simply transfer money from your everyday ASB account to your
ASB KiwiSaver Scheme
account.
If you’ve come from your Balances page in the ASB Mobile Banking app to view the details of your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account, click on the “contribute more” button. The “contribute more” link opens the Transfers page. Your everyday ASB account prepopulates the “From” selection. Your ASB KiwiSaver Scheme account prepopulates the “To” selection.
Click on the [Continue] button to check the transfer details. Don’t forget to indicate that you understand the transfer cannot be reversed, at the bottom of this page.
The Confirmation page displays the successful transfer of funds.

question: How can I return a payment credited to my ASB account by mistake?
answer:
To return a payment to the sender's bank account, call our contact centre on
0800 803 804
or +64-9 306 3000.
Alternatively, you can visit your nearest
ASB branch.

question: How do I make a payment using the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Making a payment using the
ASB Mobile Banking app
is easy. Here's how:
1. Log in to the ASB Mobile Banking app
2. Select 'Payments' from the bottom bar
3. Choose one of the following options:
Make a payment (pay once or multiple times)
Pay to mobile
/
email
Pay a Trade Me seller
For applicable accounts you can start the payment process on the ‘Summary’ screen. Simply select 'More options', then 'Make a payment'.
Other mobile payment tips
Learn how to
set up automatic payments via the app
.
Learn how to
make transfers via the app
.

question: Can I pay someone by email through ASB Mobile Banking?
answer:
Yes. Here’s how:
Log In to
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your smartphone.
Select 'Payments' from the bottom bar.
Tap Pay to mobile/email.
Enter the email address of the person you are paying, or open contacts and select your payer’s email details.
Enter the payment amount and description details.
Confirm payment by hitting the Pay button.
You'll see a confirmation that your payment has been sent and you will receive a notification once the payment has been collected or refunded.

question: Can I pay someone by mobile or email through the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Yes you can, here’s how:
Log In to
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your smartphone.
Select Payments from the bottom bar.
Tap Pay to mobile/email.
Either manually enter the mobile number or email address you are sending the payment to, or select from your contacts list on your smartphone.
Enter the payment amount and description details.
Confirm payment by hitting the Pay button.
You’ll see a confirmation your payment has been sent and the recipient will receive a notification that the payment has been received if they're an ASB mobile user. If the recipient is not an ASB Mobile user, the payer will receive a notification once the payment has been collected or refunded.
Watch our video on paying to email or a mobile here.
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: How do I increase my home loan payments permanently?
answer:
To increase your repayments, please call us on
0800 100 600
(Mon-Fri 8am - 8pm, Sat 9am-5pm).
You can also have a look at our
Top tips to pay off your home loan faster
for general information about increasing your repayments and other helpful guides.

question: What are the daily payment limits in the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Daily payment limits are a security precaution. You can make payments of up to $100,000 per day (or a lower amount as chosen by you) if you have Netcode-verified the device that is using the
ASB Mobile Banking app
.
If you do not
Netcode
verify the app, your daily limit is $1,000 (or a lower amount as chosen by you).
The daily limit for the ‘Pay to mobile/email’ function in the app is $5000.
If you need to make a payment that exceeds the daily limit, please contact us on
0800 803 804
, or if you're calling from overseas +64 9 306 3000.
How do I register for Netcode?
How can I change the daily Netcode limit for payments?

question: Can I use ASB Mobile to pay Trade Me Sellers?
answer:
Yes.  You can use ASB Mobile to pay for items you have won on Trade Me.  Here’s how to pay your Trade Me Seller.
Log In to
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your smartphone.
Select the Pay Trade Me Seller button.
The first time you select this option, you’ll be asked to log into Trade Me, please do so.
We’ll ask your permission for ASB Mobile to access your Trade Me account, so items you have won will display within the app.  (You won’t have to grant this permission again). Select the Allow button.
The items you have won will be displayed. Select the item you want to pay for.  It’s sensible to review the item, just to make sure the details are right, before hitting Continue.
On the Pay Item screen, select the account from which you wish to pay. The payment amount, reference details and seller’s account number will be displayed (from the won item details).  If the seller hasn’t provided an account number in the payment instructions, you can manually enter this.
Check the payment details and select the Pay button to continue.
Enter your password to confirm payment. We’ll then make a one off payment to the seller’s account number.
You’ll receive a payment confirmation.  You can send a confirmation email to the Trade Me seller if you wish to.
Does a Trade Me Seller need to collect an ASB Mobile payment?
No, the payment will be made directly to their account.  If they are an ASB account holder, the payment will be immediate, if not, it may take up to two working days for the payment to appear.

question: Can I view, print and export the principal and interest payments I have made to my loan?
answer:
Yes, to view your loan repayments for a selected time period:
Log into FastNet Classic internet banking.
On the 'Balances' page, select the deduction account from which the payments are made.
In the search area above the transactions, enter the desired date range (e.g. 01/04/2017 to 31/03/2018).
Then enter the loan account number in the search field (e.g.1234567 from loan number: 12-1234-1234567-91 001).
This will return a list of all payments (principal and interest) made to the loan(s) under the deduction account which you can view and print. You can also export this information to various file types, however these files will contain all transactions for the chosen time period, and may require further filtering on the file itself.
Note:
if you only wish to see payments to a specific loan, try entering your full loan number in the search instead (e.g.12-1234-1234567-91 001).

question: How do I view, edit or delete my payees in the ASB Mobile App?
answer:
To view or change the Particulars, Code or Reference details via the ASB Mobile App:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app
From the menu, tap ‘Payments’
Tap ‘Manage payees’
If you have more than one account, select the bank account you wish to view the payees for
Tap the Payee you wish to view or edit
In the Edit payee screen edit the details of your payee
Select ‘Save’ in the top right corner
Return to Manage payees screen
To delete an existing payee via the ASB Mobile App
Log in to the ASB Mobile app
From the menu, tap ‘Payments’
Tap ‘Manage payees’
If you have more than one account, select the bank account you wish to view the payees for
Find the payee you wish to delete
a) On iOS, swipe left on the payee and select the trash can icon
b) On Android, long press the payee
Select ‘Delete’ from the Confirm screen
Return to Manage payees screen
OR
Log in to the ASB Mobile app
From the menu, tap ‘Payments’
Tap ‘Manage payees’
If you have more than one account, select the bank account you wish to view the payees for
Tap the Payee you wish to delete
Scroll to the end of the Edit payee screen and select the Delete payee button
Select ‘Delete’ from the Confirm screen
Return to Manage payees screen

question: Can I make automatic payments in FastNet Business?
answer:
Yes.
FastNet Business allows you to view and modify existing automatic payments and create new ones.
You can use this feature to set up recurring payments (weekly, fortnightly etc.) to any standard bank account in New Zealand.
Please ensure you have sufficient funds in your account, as fees may be charged on dishonoured payments.
To set up an automatic payment:
Before you begin, display the Automatic Payments page. To do this:
On the FastNet Business tab bar, click Transactional Banking.
Below the tab bar, click Payments.
On the left-menu click Automatic Payment.
On the Automatic Payments page, click Add.
Select the relevant account from which funds will be deducted.
Complete the remaining fields and select the relevant frequency.
If you want the automatic payment to recur indefinitely, leave the Final Amount and Final Date fields empty. If you want the payments to stop on a particular day, complete the Final Date field. If you want the final amount to differ from the regular amount, complete the Final Amount field.
Click OK. The Automatic Payment Confirmation screen displays the payment's details to save and authorise.
A) Click Save and Authorise
1. This takes you automatically to the Authorisation screen, within Unsent Payments, where you are able to authorise this automatic payment.
B) Click Save
1. Navigate to the Unsent Payment page on the left hand menu
2. All automatic payments waiting for authorisation will be displayed here along with all the other payment types. The status will be ‘In progress’
To authorise automatic payments select one, or more, of the check boxes displayed. Complete all relevant User ID, password and Netcode fields. Click Authorise payments. A message will display confirming the automatic payment was successfully created.
Once an automatic payment is authorised it will display as Sent in the queue until the end of the day. It will also be visible in Sent Payment queue, as well as back on the Automatic Payment page.
Note:
New automatic payment requests must be fully authorised before 8pm on the day of payment. If this does not happen, the request will expire and must be deleted and recreated from the main Automatic Payment page.

question: What is an electronic transaction?
answer: An electronic transaction is any transaction that you complete yourself, without the help of ASB staff. These include EFTPOS, ASB ATM transactions, automatic payments, bill payments, One-Off Payment, direct debits, direct credits, FastNet Classic or FastPhone transfers and Mobile Top-ups.

question: How do I reset the passcode for my Garmin Pay?
answer:
Three incorrect attempts at entering your passcode will lock your wallet on the watch. You'll have to go to the Garmin Connect Mobile app on your smartphone or tablet to set a new passcode.
You will need to re-enter your old passcode at this point. If you don't remember it, Garmin will delete your entire wallet to protect you. If this occurs, repeat the
set up process
.

question: How do I suspend the ASB Visa card I use for Garmin Pay?
answer:
To suspend an ASB Visa card from being used with Garmin Pay:
On the Garmin Connect Mobile app dashboard, tap or click the 'Settings' icon.
Tap or click 'Garmin Pay'.
Tap or click 'Manage your Wallet'.
Select the card you want to suspend.
Tap or click 'Suspend Card'.
To reactivate the card on your watch, complete these steps and tap or click 'Unsuspend Card'.

question: Where can I use Garmin Pay?
answer:
You can use Garmin Pay almost anywhere you can make contactless payments. Just look for the contactless symbol on the payment terminal at the store check-out.
For overseas transactions the limits and ability to use contactless will vary depending on which country you are travelling in. It is always a good idea to take more than one form of payment when travelling.
Additional fees
will apply to overseas transactions made using your ASB Visa card.

question: How do I remove the ASB Visa card I use for Garmin Pay?
answer:
To remove an ASB Visa card from Garmin Pay:
On the Garmin Connect Mobile app dashboard, tap or click the 'Settings' icon.
Tap or click 'Garmin Pay'.
Tap or click 'Manage your Wallet'.
Select the card you want to remove.
Tap or click 'Delete Card'. The card will disappear from the wallet.
If the card is stored on your watch, sync your watch to remove it.

question: What do I do if I've lost or misplaced my Garmin watch?
answer:
The Garmin watch has its own PIN code as a layer of protection. For added security, you can suspend (and reactivate) the card on your watch in the 'Wallet' section of the Garmin Connect Mobile app (even if your watch isn't nearby).
When you suspend a card, it remains stored in your wallet, but you can’t use it to make payments with your watch.
You can also suspend or resume using Garmin Pay through the ASB Contact Centre by calling 0800 803 804.

question: How do I change the card I use for Garmin Pay?
answer: Your last-used card will automatically appear on your watch screen. To switch to a different card simply swipe down on your screen.

question: Which ASB cards can I use with Garmin Pay?
answer: You can use all ASB Visa Debit and ASB Visa credit cards to make payments using your Garmin watch.

question: Can I export the principal and interest payments I have made to my home loan?
answer:
Yes, to export your loan repayments for a selected time period:
Log into FastNet Classic internet banking.
On the 'Balances' page, select the deduction account from which the payments are made.
In the search area above the transactions, enter the desired date range (e.g. 01/04/2017 to 31/03/2018).
Then enter the loan account number in the search field (e.g.1234567 from loan number: 12-1234-1234567-91 001).
This will return a list of all payments (principal and interest) made to the loan(s) under the deduction account which you can now export.
Note: if you only wish to see payments to a specific loan, try using the payment number in the search (e.g. 001 from loan number: 12-1234-1234567-91 001).

question: I didn't receive a notification and my Online EFTPOS payment didn't go through, what went wrong?
answer:
Online EFTPOS notifications only appear within the ASB Mobile Banking app. Log into the app within four minutes of creating the Online EFTPOS payment request on the website.
There are a couple of reasons why your payment might not go through:
Mobile data or Wi-Fi signal may have been lost before the notification could be delivered.
The mobile number you entered on the website is not registered to your ASB Mobile Banking app.
Simply try the payment again and select Online EFTPOS or try an alternative payment method.

question: Can I cancel an Online EFTPOS payment I've made and get a refund?
answer: Once you have approved the transaction, the Online EFTPOS payment is processed in real-time. For all refund enquiries please contact the company or website where you made the purchase.

question: Why was my Online EFTPOS purchase declined?
answer:
There are a few reasons why your purchase may have been declined:
You have 'Receive payment requests' turned off in the ASB Mobile Banking app.
You did not approve the transaction in your ASB Mobile Banking app within four minutes.
You declined the payment notification within the ASB Mobile Banking app.
There are not enough available funds in the account you selected.
The mobile number you entered is not registered for your ASB Mobile Banking app.
Identify the reason specific to your payment and then try the payment again.

question: Can I use Online EFTPOS with my ASB credit card?
answer: Online EFTPOS uses available funds from your ASB transaction or savings account that appears in your ASB Mobile Banking app. It does not work for credit cards.

question: I’m being asked to install an update to enable access to Near Field Communication (NFC) on my Android device, is this safe?
answer:
Yes it is safe, by accepting the permissions there will be no changes to your phone.
Once you agree to install the update you will see this screen. Select ACCEPT to begin the install the update.
After the app has updated if you select the app permissions link at the bottom of the Google Play Store page, you will see this screen confirming the update.

question: What happens if my Direct Debit fails and can't be processed?
answer:
A Direct Debit can fail for a number of reasons. They will automatically dishonour if the account you're attempting to pay from cannot accept the Direct Debit. Reasons a Direct Debit may not be accepted include:
You have a hold on your account.
ASB hasn’t received an authority allowing the Direct Debit.
The Direct Debit is to the wrong account suffix.
ASB cancelled the Direct Debit authority on your request.
The Direct Debit was attempted on a savings or investment account.
If a Direct Debit transaction is attempted while there are insufficient funds to make the payment, one of two actions will happen.
Depending on your account history, we may choose to process the Direct Debit, taking your account into unarranged overdraft.
The Direct Debit transaction may be attempted again after midnight. If there are still insufficient funds available then the Direct Debit will not be processed.
Dishonoured payments are sent back to the initiator (the person you pay with the Direct Debit) and it is their responsibility to arrange an alternative payment method with you.

question: How can another payment go through if my ASB Visa Debit card has been cancelled?
answer:
​The transaction may have occurred prior to your card being cancelled. Depending on the merchant, it can take a bit of time for that transaction to be taken off your account.
Please review the transaction, and if you wish to dispute it you must notify us in writing within 60 days of the date the transaction was charged to your account, with full details. Please refer to the
Visa Debit Card Conditions of Use
document for more details.

question: Can I stop future dated bulk payments in FastNet Business?
answer:
Yes, to stop a future dated bulk payment in FastNet Business:
1. Log in to FastNet Business
2. Select 'Payments'
3. In the 'Payments' menu, select 'Sent Payments'
4. Click the payment name you wish to update
5. Select 'Stop Payment'
5. Once confirmed you will see a success message and future dated bulk payments for the selected payment will now be stopped

question: What is the quickest way to pay money into my ASB account?
answer:
​The quickest way to deposit money into an account is by transferring from another ASB account or by depositing cash directly at any
ASB branch
. This will appear in your account immediately.
You can also use the ASB Mobile app to make quick and easy payments using a mobile phone number or email address.
Find out more information about paying safely and easily using the
ASB Mobile app
.

question: What happens if I make a payment to an incorrect or invalid account number?
answer:
If you pay to an invalid account number, typically what will happen is the funds will be returned to you within 1-3 working days.
If the account number is incorrect but active and belonging to someone else then the funds will remain in the incorrect account unless an agreement can be made to reverse the funds back. If this happens, contact us so we can arrange with the bank the account is held with to have the funds returned.
Please be aware that there is a $40.00 charge to recover funds and it is not guaranteed that they will be able to be returned.

question: How can I reduce my personal loan payments?
answer:
If you have made additional payments and reduced the term of your loan you can extend the term of your loan​ back out to the original term which in turn reduces the payment fortnightly or monthly.
You can do this by sending a secure email request through My Messages in FastNet Classic internet banking, visiting any
ASB branch
or by calling
0800 272 662
. You may be required to sign a document in branch as this may need to be witnessed.

question: What types of payments can I authorise and send on ASB Mobile Business?
answer:
At the moment, you can authorise and send:
IRD Payments
Transfers
Direct Credit (Bulk)
Payroll (Bulk)
Creditors (Bulk)
Credit Cards (Bulk)
International Money Transfers
Same Day Cleared Payment

question: Why doesn’t the Minimum Payment Due change when I’ve made a payment to my ASB credit card?
answer:
The ‘Minimum Payment Due’ and ‘Closing balance’ amounts are there for your reference on your statements based on a closing date and will remain constant, even if you make a payment before the due date.
If you would like to confirm how much you have paid, you can view this in the transaction summary in FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app.

question: How do I create or edit a Direct Debit receipt in FastNet Business?
answer:
Before you begin, display the Create Direct Debit Receipt page by:
Selecting Transactional Banking on the FastNet Business tab bar
Below the tab bar, select Receipts
On the left-menu, select Direct Debit
When performing the steps below, remember to save your work frequently. This will ensure that all the payers that you add to the direct debit receipt and all the changes you make are saved.
To Create a Direct Debit Receipt:
On the Create Direct Debit Receipt page, in the Receipt Name field, type a name that will identify this direct debit receipt.
Note: Receipt names must be unique and no more than 20 characters
If relevant, from the Registration ID drop-list, select the correct registration ID.
If the Particulars, Code and Reference fields (in the Payee Details section) are active, type the relevant details into these fields.
Note: Use only letters and numbers. These are the details that will appear on your statement.
Note: If these fields are inactive (greyed out), the details to appear on your statement will be entered against each payer transaction, later.
(Optional) Click the calendar icon and select a different due date.
Note: This is the date the receipts are to be processed.
Note: You can also overtype the displayed date.
(Optional) At the bottom of the page, in the empty Payer Details fields, enter a payers name, account number, amount to be deducted from the payer, and (optional) the particulars, code and reference details to be printed on the payer's, and if relevant, your statement.
Note: At some stage, you will need to add payers to your direct debit receipt; either manually as described here, or from the Direct Debit Library, as described later in this procedure.
Note: This number can be entered with or without hyphens. For bank account numbers, the format is 11-2222-3333333-44 or 11-2222-3333333-444.
Note: When entering particulars, code and reference details, use only letters and numbers; do not use special characters.
(Optional) At the bottom of the page, click 'Add Payers from library', select the relevant group of payers, or selection of payers from a group, then click 'Add selection to current receipt'.
Result: The payer(s) are added to the list of payer transactions.
Note: Remember to complete the transaction details for each payer you have added.
Hint:
For payers that are to have the same amount, particulars, code and reference details specified, select the relevant payers, then click 'Fill Multiple Fields' . You can then enter and apply the same amounts and details to all selected payers.
When you have finished adding payers to the direct debit receipt, click Save & Exit.
Result: The Current Receipts page is displayed. The newly created receipt is listed on the Current Payments page.
To Edit a Direct Debit Receipt:
On the Current Receipts page, using the checkboxes, click the relevant receipt's name.
Result: The Modify Direct Debits Receipt page displays the receipt's details.
(Optional) Edit the Payer Details Due Date.
If relevant, edit the Payee Details Particulars, Code, Reference fields.
Note: Anything that you type in these fields will appear on your bank statement. If you are unable to type anything into these fields, you can type them into the fields under the 'Show on Your Statement' section of the payers' details.
If relevant, select another registration ID.
(Optional) Add payers from the payer library.
Hint: Click 'Add Payers from Library'.
(Optional) Edit existing payers details.
Hint: To change a single payer transaction, overtype the relevant fields, then on your keyboard press Tab.
Hint: To apply the same changes to more than one payer transaction, select the relevant payer transactions then click 'Fill Multiple Fields'. Make the changes you want to apply across selected payer transactions, then click OK.
(Optional) Delete unwanted payer transactions from the receipt.
Hint: Select the relevant payer transaction(s) then click Delete, then click Yes.
(Optional) Add new payer transactions.
Hint: Click New then complete the new payers fields.
Click Save & Exit, then click OK.
Result: The changes are saved and the Current Receipts page is displayed.

question: I'm in FastNet Business, can I make IRD payments?
answer:
Yes. With FastNet Business, you can set up tax and other payments to pay Inland Revenue, either immediately, or automatically on a future date. Once processed, payments to Inland Revenue cannot be reversed.
Payments are processed Monday to Friday with the exception of public holidays. You can make same day payments during ASB's normal processing hours. Outside these hours, you won’t be able to select today’s date.
Please ensure that your IRD payment is fully authorised, scheduled for payment on or before its due date and that the withdrawal account has sufficient funds on the scheduled date to cover the payment. For information on due dates, see
www.ird.govt.nz
.
To create a new IRD Payment:
On the FastNet Business tab bar, click Transactional Banking, then below this, click Payments and on the left-menu, click IRD Payment.
Complete all mandatory fields.
Once you click on 'Save and Authorise’ you will be transferred to the ‘Authorise Payments’ page.
Make sure you complete the User ID and password fields, once entered select the ‘Authorise Payments’ button. If you need more than one authoriser or remote authoriser(s),
follow these instructions.
Once you have authorized the payment, send the payment from the screen prompt or Unsent payments list.
Confirmation and status of your payment is displayed in the Sent Payments page.
I can’t find the payment I’m looking for. What’s happened?
All IRD Payments can be viewed once Authorised and Sent in the Sent payments screen
Check the Unsent Payments screen if not found or
It may have been deleted by someone
Not be visible because you do not have permission and/or the relevant level of account access. In this instance, see your FastNet Business Administrator.

question: What if the contactless functionality on my card doesn’t work?
answer: Your card needs to be activated before the contactless functionality will work. If you use your card first on a transaction by inserting your card into a terminal and entering your PIN, then contactless should work after this transaction.

question: Is there a daily limit on purchases I can make with my contactless card?
answer: No there is no daily limit. You can make as many contactless transactions as you want.

question: Can I still use the chip or swipe my contactless card to process a transaction?
answer: Yes. If the shop doesn’t have a contactless terminal installed, you can continue to insert or swipe your contactless credit or debit card just as you always have. You can also choose to use this option even if there is a contactless terminal available.

question: Can I be charged twice if my contactless card has been waved or tapped more than once?
answer: No, it doesn't matter how many times a card is read by the contactless terminal, the charge will only occur once.

question: How do I use my contactless card?
answer:
Look for the contactless symbol in participating stores.
Look for the amount displayed on the terminal and/or a flashing green light. Wave or tap your card.
Listen for the ‘beep’ or look for the green lights. Remove your card. Your transaction is complete.

question: I'm using FastNet Business, how do I create, authorise and send FastCheque payments?
answer:
With FastNet Business you can make a one-off payment to any New Zealand bank account. Use FastCheque to pay people or organisations that you are not expecting to pay again.
You can create a FastCheque payment then authorise it.  An authorised FastCheque payment is sent immediately.
You can also create and save a FastCheque payment so that you can view, edit, print, authorise and send it later, or delete it.
If you need more than one authorisation, all the authorisers must enter their details so that the payment can be sent. You cannot partially authorise, then save, a FastCheque.
To create and authorise a FastCheque, you must have both Create and Authorise permission for the FastCheque function, and Transact access to the withdrawal account.
Before You begin display the FastCheque Payments page.
On the FastNet Business tab bar, click Transactional Banking.
Below the tab bar, click Payments.
On the left-menu, click FastCheque.
Then:
Check the FastCheque Payments Details page is displayed directly, it should be if there are no unsent FastCheques. Otherwise, click Add to display.
Complete all relevant fields. (Mandatory fields are marked with an asterisk
*.)
Click Save & Authorise. The Authorise FastCheque Payments page displays a summary of the payment.
Complete all relevant User ID, password and Netcode fields.
Click Authorise. Confirmation of your FastCheque payment is displayed.Click OK. The FastCheque Payments page is displayed.

question: I'm in FastNet Business, can I create a new bulk payment from scratch?
answer:
Yes.
Follow these steps to create a Direct Credit, Payroll, Creditors, or Credit Cards bulk payment from scratch.
Before you begin, display the relevant Create a Payment page. To do this:
On the FastNet Business tab bar, click Transactional Banking.
Below the tab bar, click Payments.
On the left-menu click the relevant bulk payment type.
Then:
On the relevant Create…. Payment page, type the name of this bulk payment in the Payment Name field.
Click the Account drop-box if you want to change the withdrawal account then select the correct account from the list. This is the account from which the payment will be deducted. You can choose your preferred withdrawal account for bulk payments in User Preferences.
The Due Date is shown with today's date. If you want to change it, click the calendar icon and select the date when you want your payees to be paid, or type the relevant date directly into the field.
(Optional) Tick the checkbox to show all payee lines on your statement. Otherwise, the bulk payment will be shown as one transaction on your statement. If individual payee lines are shown on your statement, a fee per payee line (transaction) may apply.
(Optional) Type the relevant details into the Particulars, Code and Reference (PCR) fields.
Add each payee to the Payee Details section of the bulk payment. You can click Add Payees from Library to quickly add the details of any existing payees.
The Account numbers can be entered with or without hyphens. If you are using hyphens, use the format 12-1234-1234567-12 or 12-1234-1234567-012 for bank account numbers, and 1111-2222-3333-4444 for credit card numbers.
All payments will be directed to the account number that you specify. ThePayee Description is for your reference only.
When making a bulk Credit Cards payment, only credit cards belonging to ASB customers will be accepted.
(Optional) For each payee line, enter any relevant details in the PCR and Internal Reference fields. If you want a number of payees to receive the same amount and/or show the same PCR details, tick the checkbox next to each of those payees then click Fill Multiple Fields.  See Editing an Unsent Bulk Payment.
To display another empty payee line, click New or press Tab when the cursor is in the final Internal Ref. field.
If you are entering a large number of payee lines, we recommend that you frequently click Save to keep ASB's systems up-to-date with the information you have entered.
When you have finished, click Save & Exit. The Unsent Payments page is displayed with a message confirming that the bulk payment has been successfully saved. The new bulk payment is shown on the Unsent Payments page.
You can add, modify, or delete payee lines, or change the payment name, due date, and withdrawal account. For more information, see Editing a Bulk Payment.
A bulk payment may need authorisation by more than one person. You can save a bulk payment and email the authoriser(s) so that they can complete the authorisation process from any location.
To obtain permission for creating a particular type of bulk payment, see your FastNet Business Administrator.
If you want to create a bulk International Money Transfer (IMT), please refer to
Bulk International Money Transfers.

question: What are the operating times for Same Day Cleared Payments?
answer:
You can send same day cleared payments to participating New Zealand banks between 9.00am and 4.30pm on a business day.
Important:
A same day cleared payment that has been saved, but not authorised and sent, will expire at midnight.
Time (NZST)
Permitted Tasks
9.00am to 4.30pm on a business day
Save, authorise, and send a same day cleared payment.
Result: The payment is processed and made the same day.
4.30pm to midnight on a business day
Save a same day cleared payment.
Result: The payment expires at midnight.
Midnight to 9.00am on a business day
Save, authorise, and send a same day cleared payment.
Result: The payment is processed after 9.00am and made the same day.
Anytime
Select an expired same day cleared payment.
Result: The payment date changes to today's date.
Anytime on a non-business day
Save a same day cleared payment.
Result: The payment expires at midnight.

question: How do I delete a payee line in FastNet Business?
answer:
To delete any unwanted payee lines from your batch:
Click the checkbox next to the payee(s) you wish to delete
Click ‘Delete’
Click ‘OK’ at the ‘Are you sure you want to delete the selected rows?’ pop up message

question: Why does my payment says expired, even though I authorised it in FastNet Business?
answer:
Even though your payment may have been authorised, it was not ‘sent’ for processing and would have expired at midnight on the due date.
Upon authorisation you should have received a pop up to confirm your payment is authorised and ready to send.
To send your expired payment:
- Log into FastNet Business
- Go to ‘Unsent Payments’
- Click on the name of the payment to open it up
- Change the due date
- Click authorise & send (enter in your user details)
- Click send at the confirmation pop up message

question: How do I store my IRD number in FastNet Business?
answer:
With FastNet Business, you can create a list of commonly used IRD / GST numbers, and for each number, add a description.
To set up your IRD number(s):
- Click on Administration
- Click on Client
- Click on IRD/GST Maintenance
- Click Add
- Type in your  IRD / GST Number
- Type in your description
- Save
- Once saved, the new IRD / GST number will save on your main IRD/GST page

question: Will I have to pay charges or fees if I pay off my ASB Debt Consolidation Loan early?
answer:
No you won’t. You can pay off your ASB Debt Consolidation loan at any time, with no fees and no charges. In fact, the faster you pay it off, the more you will save in interest payments.
ASB’s lending criteria, terms and fees apply.

question: Why is there a hold on my account?
answer: There are a number of reasons why a hold may be applied to your account.  Please contact us on 0800 803 804 or visit your nearest branch to discuss the hold.

question: Can I change the date by which I need to make a credit card payment?
answer:
Yes. For personal ASB credit card accounts, payment is due 25 days after your statement is produced. You can arrange to pay your account before the due date if you wish.
You can nominate an alternative date which suits you better, so long as the statement date is between the 1 and 28
of the month. Please note that this may require us to close your current credit card account and transfer your balance to a new credit card account. This will mean you have a new credit card number and any existing automatic payments or direct debits will need to be amended.
Please call us on 0800 255 382 to discuss your options.

question: Can I create payments on ASB Mobile Business?
answer: Yes, you can now create a Direct Credit payment using the ASB Mobile Business app. It is limited to having one Payee, so you are not able to initiate Bulk Payments. The valid date range is up to 60 days in advance. Depending on your permissions you will either be able to save the payment for authorisation by another user or authorise and send it yourself. Your same daily limits also apply.

question: How can I change the order my accounts are displayed in?
answer:
You can change the order of your accounts in the ASB Mobile Banking app and FastNet Classic internet banking.
Here’s how:
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings'.
Under 'Personalise', tap 'Reorder accounts'.
Drag and drop to reorder your accounts.
Tap 'Save'.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Personalise' on the top of the accounts.
Select 'Reorder accounts'.
Use the arrows to reorder your accounts.
Click 'Save'.

question: How do I set up an appointment with the bank?
answer:
Setting up an appointment is easy in the ASB Mobile Banking app, FastNet Classic internet banking, or by phoning our Contact Centre.
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the question mark icon in the top right of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Book an appointment’, and under ‘Other ways to contact us.
Follow the instructions.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on the message bubble with the three dots inside in the bottom right.
Tap ‘Book an appointment’, and under ‘Other ways to contact us.
Follow the instructions.
Phone our Contact Centre on 0800 803 804.
The helpful staff will be delighted to arrange a time for you to meet with a consultant either by phone, or in person at your nearest branch.  The details of your nearest branch can be found using our
branch locator
.

question: My ASB Visa Credit card or ASB Visa Debit card has been stolen, damaged, or I've lost it, what should I do?
answer:
Lock and replace using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
You can lock your card using
ASB Card Control
in the ASB Mobile app. To do this:
Tap 'More' at the bottom right of the accounts screen.
Then tap 'Cards & devices'. and choose your card.
Tap 'Lock card' or if your card is lost for good, scroll down and tap 'Cancel and replace card'.
If you find your card, just go back into Card Control to unlock it and start using it again.
If you've cancelled or locked your card, you can still use any of our contactless payment options (Google Pay, Apple Pay, Garmin Pay or Fitbit Pay) to pay.
Note:
If you have replaced your card as it was lost or stolen, your mobile or watch will update with the new card details and be available to use around 5 am the morning after your card has been replaced.
See our detailed step-by-step instructions on
cancelling and replacing your card
, where you can download and print the guide.
Replace using FastNet Classic internet banking.
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Select 'Cards' from the menu on the accounts page.
Select 'Replace card' from the Cards section.
You should receive your replacement card within 5 to 7 working days. Overseas delivery times will be subject to local service providers.
Don't have FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app?
If you need help to replace your card call 0800 803 804 or visit your nearest
ASB branch
. While doing this, It's simple to set up
Fastnet Classic
or the
ASB Mobile app
, after cancelling your card, just ask the ASB staff member to guide you through the process.
If you are overseas, call us collect through the local operator on +64 9 306 3000. Alternatively, notify the nearest office of Visa.
My Card was stolen and there have been unauthorised transactions.
If any loss arises from the unauthorised use of your card, please call us immediately on 0800 803 804 or if you are overseas, +64 9 306 3000, or visit an ASB branch for assistance.
Visit our
Disputing a transaction
page for more details on how to file a claim, you’ll find step-by-step guidance and answers to common questions.

question: What do I need if the deceased did not have a will and has total assets at date of death under $40,000?
answer:
• The death certificate – original or a certified true copy.
• A birth or marriage certificate (as applicable) of the applicant – original or a true certified copy. This is to identify the relationship between the applicant and the deceased.
• If the deceased had no will and no more than $40,000 of funds deposited with ASB at the time of death. In this scenario we may pay the funds in accordance with instructions received from next of kin upon application.
• If the application is in a single name and there are other parties eligible for funds, a
Consent  for payment form
must be completed by each eligible party. Please note that this is a sworn declaration and as such will need to be witnessed by a Justice of the Peace (JP), Solicitor or Notary Public.
For further information, view our online
Deceased Estates brochure
.

question: My EFTPOS card has been stolen, damaged, or I've lost it, what should I do?
answer:
If your EFTPOS card has been lost, stolen or damaged, call us on
0800 803 804
or visit your nearest
ASB branch
and we'll cancel the card and issue a replacement for you. If you're overseas, call us on
+64 9 306 3000
.
You should receive your replacement card within 5 to 7 working days (customers who visit a branch will get their EFTPOS card immediately). Overseas delivery times will be subject to local service providers.

question: What is my True Rewards card PIN and how can I set or change my PIN?
answer:
Your True Rewards card has a PIN number which you create. It is unique to your card and is a security measure designed to prevent anyone unauthorised from using your card as one True Rewards Dollar is equal to one New Zealand dollar.
To activate, select or change the PIN on your True Rewards card, you can do this by:
ASB Mobile Banking app> Cards> your True Rewards card> Update PIN.
FastNet Classic internet banking> select your Profile> Card PIN.
Visiting any
ASB branch
.

question: What is the difference between current balance, available credit and credit card limit?
answer:
Your current balance is the amount currently owing on your card account.
The available credit is the amount that you have available to spend. This is based on the credit limit less the current balance less any pending transactions.
The credit limit is the amount of credit available on your card account. You can spend up to this limit.
If your account has a credit current balance or the pending transaction is a payment, the available credit will be your credit limit plus the credit current balance plus the credit pending transaction.

question: What is reward interest on my Savings Plus Monthly account?
answer: Reward interest encourages you to stay on track with your savings goal. If you make no withdrawal or 1 withdrawal during the first day of the calendar month you will receive reward interest. Any subsequent withdrawals on the first day of the month or withdrawals during the remaining days of the month will mean you do not earn reward interest for that month.

question: What do I need if the deceased did not have a will and has total assets at date of death over $40,000?
answer:
Letters of Administration – original or a certified true copy. This will require an application to the High Court.
Written closing instructions signed by all administrators.
The forms that ASB requires for this scenario can be found in Branch or by clicking
here
.
For further information, view our online
Deceased Estates brochure
.

question: What do I need if the deceased had a will and has total assets at date of death over $40,000?
answer:
A probate document –original or a certified true copy. This is granted through an application to the High Court. In this scenario ASB will, pay funds in accordance with instructions received from the executor upon application. This can only occur after probate has been granted.
Written closing instructions signed by all executors.
The forms that ASB requires for this scenario can be found in Branch or by clicking
here
.
For further information, view our online
Deceased Estates brochure
.

question: How do I request a new deposit book?
answer:
You can order a new deposit book by:
Visiting your nearest
ASB branch.
Calling FastPhone
0800 272 272
; follow the menu prompts for Other Financial Services, Order Items, Deposit book.
Calling our contact centre on 0800 803 804.

question: How do I share my account owner name and number from the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
To share your account details:
Log into the ASB Mobile app.
Select the account you want to receive money into.
Select the share button located to the right of the account number.
You can then copy and paste your account owner name and number into an email or message, or send directly via the app of your choice.
The account name that appears might be different to the name that shows on your account page (e.g., J S Smith). The share or copy details are the correct ones for your account to share in order to get a match response in the
Confirmation of Payee
check.

question: My ASB Visa credit card or ASB Visa Debit card has been damaged or doesn't work, what should I do?
answer:
If your ASB Visa credit card or ASB Visa Debit card is not working or has been damaged, you can replace your damaged card in the ASB Mobile Banking app under ASB Card Control or in FastNet Classic internet banking:
Replace using the ASB Mobile app.
Log into your ASB Mobile App.
Tap 'More' at the bottom right of the accounts screen.
Then tap 'Cards & devices'. and choose your card.
Scroll down and tap 'Cancel and replace card’.
Replace using FastNet Classic internet banking.
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Select the 'Open and apply' button on the accounts page.
When the Open & apply page open, select 'Replace card' from the Cards section.
Once you replace your card, you will be issued with a replacement card with the same number, but a new expiry date. This will not affect any bills you pay via direct debit. If you have previously set up Apple Pay or Google Pay, you can continue using your mobile or watch for contactless payments without any interruptions.
You should receive your replacement card within 5 to 7 working days. Overseas delivery times will be subject to local service providers.
Don't have FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app?
If you need help to replace your card call 0800 803 804 or visit your nearest
ASB branch
. While doing this, It's simple to set up FastNet Classic or the ASB Mobile app, after replacing your card, just ask the ASB staff member to guide you through the process.

question: Can I order a statement on an account I have closed?
answer:
Yes. We keep account statements for seven years. To request a statement, call us on 0800 803 804 or visit any ASB branch.
If you request a statement over the phone you will receive it via post.

question: How do I close my account?
answer:
Before you close your account:
Download any statements or documents you need on
FastNet Classic internet banking
.
Move any
Automatic Payments
or
Direct Debits
.
Pay any overdrawn amounts, loan balances or credit cards.
If you wish to close an account where only one signatory is required, do one of the following:
Open the ASB Mobile Banking app. Select the account you wish to close, select more options or open the menu on the top right of your screen and select 'Close this account'. Follow the prompts shown onscreen from here.
Send us a secure message. The best way to get in touch with us is by
sending a message in FastNet Classic
and one of our team will try to reply within the same business day. In your message, please include the account number of the ASB account you would like to close, the account will need to have a balance of zero before it can it be closed.
If you’ve forgotten your ASB login password, you can
reset your password
using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Visit any
ASB branch
and bring 2 forms of ID with you (including photo ID).
However, if you wish to close an account requiring two or more signatories, all signatories will need to come into an
ASB branch
with ID.
For more ways to get help please
see our contact us page
.

question: How can I add someone to my ASB bank account?
answer:
​You can do this at your local
ASB Branch
.
To make an appointment call our contact centre on
0800 803 804
during the branch opening hours.
Please note:
You will both need to
provide identification
to do this.

question: How do I order a replacement True Rewards card?
answer:
You can order a replacement True Rewards card using the ASB Mobile app under ASB Card Control and then clicking on Cancel and replace. If you're using FastNet Classic internet banking:
Log in to FastNet Classic.
Under the main menu select 'Cards' then 'Replace card'.
Follow the instructions to order a card replacement.
You should receive your replacement True Rewards card within 5 to 7 working days. Overseas delivery times will be subject to local service providers.
If you don’t have ASB FastNet Classic Internet banking or the ASB Mobile app you can visit any
ASB branch
or call
0800 878 373
, and we’ll organise a replacement card for you.

question: My Visa credit card or Visa Debit card is due to expire, when will I get a new card?
answer:
You’ll receive your new card the month before it expires and your expiring card will be valid until the last day of the month.
As your new card is sent to your mailing address in your profile please make sure your
address is kept up to date
.
If you need your card early (i.e. if you are going overseas) call 0800 803 804 or visit any
ASB branch
but please allow at least 5 to 7 working days before you need your new card.
The PIN for your new card is the same as your previous card. To activate your PIN, use your new card at any ATM or for any purchase that requires your PIN.

question: What’s the best strategy when I’m choosing a home loan?
answer:
The best home loan strategy is one that takes into account your individual circumstances, like your cash flow. Does your income and expenditure regular or does it vary? Could you afford higher repayments if interest rates increase? These and other factors might affect your ability to deal with future changes in home loan payments. Often there’s more to it than picking the lowest interest rate, so take a little time to work out what is best for you. It’s worth the effort.
We’re happy to help you with this decision. You can speak to one of our dedicated lending specialists on
0800 100 160
, get in touch with a
Home Ownership Manager
directly, or drop into any
ASB branch
. Together, we can work out a home loan that’s ideal for you.

question: What is a self-service transaction?
answer: A self-service transaction is any transaction that you complete yourself, without the help of ASB staff. These include EFTPOS, ASB ATM transactions, automatic payments, bill payments, One-Off payments, direct debits, direct credits, Fastnet Classic or FastPhone transfers and Mobile Top-ups.

question: What is a staff-assisted transaction?
answer: A staff-assisted transaction is any transaction that’s completed with the help of our staff – including staff assisted transactions in branch, by phone or at the Business Banking Centre.

question: Can I update my savings goal once it's achieved?
answer:
Yes, you can. You can either delete the achieved goal and start a new one or edit the existing savings goal and give yourself a new target.
Find out
how to I create, edit, or delete a savings goal.

question: How do I create, edit, or delete a savings goal?
answer:
If you have a savings account with us, you can create a savings goal using the ASB Mobile Banking app. Here's how:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap on the account that you want to set up the savings goal in.
Tap the three dots in the top right of the screen, then tap 'Create a goal' from the menu.
To edit or delete an existing savings goal:
Go to the account you set the savings goal in and just tap the savings tile.
Tap ‘Edit’ in the top right of the screen, then ‘Update goal’ or ‘Delete goal’.

question: Will I be able to access the ASB account I’ve opened from overseas when I arrive or how long will it take?
answer: After you apply and have provided us with your certified passport and address verification documents to open a fully activated account from overseas, we will contact you to set up internet banking to access your new account prior to your arrival.

question: What identification do I need to open an ASB account from overseas?
answer:
You'll need to provide a photocopy of your passport – photo and details, plus your visa type (if applicable). This will also need to be certified by a Trusted Referee to activate the account from overseas. We'll also need proof of your overseas residential address before we can open your account – these could be a bank statement, utility bills dated within the last twelve months that have your name and residential address (not a P.O. Box).
We will provide further guidance on the requirements once you have applied.

question: Unclaimed money at ASB.
answer:
If it's been five or more years since you last transacted on your accounts, and we haven’t had any other contact with you during that time, the money in your account(s)
*
may need to be treated as unclaimed – in accordance with the Unclaimed Money Act. In such situations, we're required by law to transfer your money to Inland Revenue (IRD) as 'unclaimed money'.
How does this affect me?
We'll try and contact you about any money that may become unclaimed. In order for us to do that, it's important that your personal details with us, such as email address, phone number and postal address, are up to date -
here's how to update them
.
If there's no new activity or response from you by a specific date (we'll let you know what date this is), your account(s) will be closed – in line with our banking terms
^
– and your money will be transferred to IRD.
Once unclaimed money is transferred to IRD, you will need to contact them directly to
claim the funds
.
Please note, if you have $100 or less in your account(s) and your money remains unclaimed after the specified date, it will be donated to charity.
What do I need to do?
If you've received a message from us about unclaimed money, email
unclaimedmoney@asb.co.nz
and let us know if you want to keep your account(s) open. Please include your reference number in the email.
The easiest way to avoid your account(s) falling into unclaimed money scope in the future is to regularly log in to ASB internet or mobile banking, or to transact on one of your accounts.
When is money treated as unclaimed?
Under the terms of the Unclaimed Money Act, we're required to transfer the money in a customer’s bank account(s) to Inland Revenue (IRD) when:
None of the customer’s bank accounts have been transacted on for 5 years or more, and;
The customer has not interacted with us on any other matter over the same 5-year period, and;
We are unsuccessful in making contact with the customer.
For Term deposits, there is a further applicable condition – in addition to those above:
The Term deposit has been auto-reinvesting for 5 years or more since the end of the first term, and the maturity date of the term that took it over 5 years has been reached.
*
Accounts include any Term deposits that have been auto-reinvesting for 5 years or more since the end of the first term. KiwiSaver accounts are excluded.
^
As per our
Personal Banking terms and conditions
and
Business, Rural and Corporate Banking terms and conditions
, we reserve the right to close your account(s) by giving you 14 days’ notice in writing.
Term deposits:
Unclaimed Term deposits may need to be broken before the end of their current term.
Foreign Currency Accounts:
Unclaimed money in Foreign Currency Accounts will be converted to the NZD equivalent (at the prevailing exchange rate) before being transferred to Inland Revenue, or donated to charity if the balance of the account(s) is $100 or less.

question: How do I stop, cancel or delete a Direct Debit?
answer:
You'll need to contact us to cancel a Direct Debit. The best way to get in touch with us is by
sending a message in FastNet Classic internet banking
and one of our team will try to reply within the same business day. If you've forgotten your ASB login password, you can
reset your password
using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Alternatively, you can visit any
ASB branch
.
You won't need to complete any forms, simply advise of the account number from which the Direct Debit is paid, and the name of the company charging you. If you're visiting us in branch, make sure you have photo ID with you, as we have to establish your identity in order to do this.
We can cancel the Direct Debit authority from our end however, Direct Debits are controlled by the company you are paying. Deleting a Direct Debit Authority will stop any further deductions from your account under that authority. If you have other payments going to this same company, cancelling the direct debit authority will also affect these. Once the Authority has been deleted from your account, the company will not be notified until they next try to charge your account, at which point the charge will decline.
For credit cards:
You'll also need to contact us to cancel a Direct Debit that is set to pay your ASB Credit card. As above, the best way to get in touch with us is by
sending a message in FastNet Classic
and one of our team will try get back to you within the same business day.
To cancel recurring transactions to your credit card account, you'll need to get in touch with the organisation that holds your recurring payment authority.
For more ways to get help please
see our contact us page
.

question: Why can't I see account grouping in the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer: Group accounts is a feature that's only available in the ‘Personalise’ menu in FastNet Classic internet banking. You'll be able to see the order of the accounts in the ASB Mobile app, but won’t see any groups you created in FastNet Classic.

question: How does ASB confirm my address online?
answer:
We use a trusted third party to check your name, date of birth and address. They then match your information to their database.
If they find a match, that’s all we need to do.
Otherwise, you’ll see an option to upload an appropriate
proof of address document
showing your name and current home address if you have completed the Join ASB form or in the ASB ID app. You can return to this step if you are a new customer by logging in to ASB using the Login button on the top right of the ASB website, or for all customers, in the ASB ID app after you have submitted your ID.
Before you upload your proof of address, check that it is an original PDF which you received in the last 12 months, shows your name and home address, is one of these
approved PDF document types
, and is smaller than 700kB.
You can also take an
address verification document
to an ASB branch.
We go to great lengths to protect your privacy. You can find out more about the way we collect, hold and use your information in our Personal Banking Terms and Conditions.
We go to great lengths to protect your privacy. You can find out more about the way we collect, hold and use your information in our
Personal Banking Terms and Conditions
.

question: I have an ASB Visa Debit Card; do I have to use a PIN?
answer: Yes, however a PIN is not required for internet, mail or telephone order transactions, or for contactless transactions under $200.

question: I am a joint investor and we have different Prescribed Investor Rates, which rate should I use?
answer:
Each account holder should provide us with their individual Prescribed Investor Rate (PIR). The Portfolio Investment Entity (PIE) income attributable to jointly held investments will be taxed using the highest PIR of the account holders.
Find out more information about PIRs
.
To update your PIR in FastNet Classic internet banking:
Log in to FastNet Classic and select your Profile in the top right hand corner.
Select Update Tax Details.
You’ll see your tax details we have for you.
Make any changes you need to.
Select Save.
Or by
contacting us
.

question: Which account is linked to my ASB Visa Debit card and can I change it?
answer:
The account(s) will be the one(s) you selected when you applied for your ASB Visa Debit card. Commonly this will be your transaction or savings account.
All accounts you access using your EFTPOS card may also be accessed using your ASB Visa Debit card. However, ASB Cash Fund, term deposit and Passbook accounts can't be used for an ASB Visa Debit.
To change the accounts linked to your ASB Visa Debit card, please visit any
ASB branch
, or contact us on
0800 803 804
.

question: Can joint account holders make changes to a joint account without both agreeing to it?
answer:
It depends on how you have specified the account should be operated.
1. You do not require more than one account holder to operate your joint account:
In this case ASB will make payments, allow withdrawals, close an account, give information or do anything else we’ve been asked to do by either of the account holders.
2. You have specified that two or more joint account holders are required to operate the account:
In this case you must give ASB instructions on your joint account together. However there are some basic maintenance functions that you’ll be able to do individually. These include requesting information on transactions, saving payees and ordering a statement.

question: Can I track my savings and donations through Save the Change?
answer:
Yes, you can easily view the contributions you have made to your savings account and/or an ASB charity partner via the ASB Mobile Banking app. Here's how:
Log in and select the transaction account you are Saving and/or Donating the change from.
On your Account summary, select 'Save the Change setup' under 'More options'.
At the top of the screen, you can view the total amount of your contributions so far, including a monthly breakdown.

question: Is there a limit to the amount of cash I can get out from an ATM machine?
answer: Yes, for your security there is. You can withdraw up to NZ$2,000 per card, per day, at ATM machines.

question: Can I Save and Donate my Change at the same time?
answer:
Yes – you can choose to enable Save and Donate the Change at the same time.  The change saved will be distributed evenly between your nominated savings account/s and your nominated charity at the end of each day.
Alternatively, you can choose to Save the Change only or Donate the Change only.

question: Where can I view my Foreign Currency Account in FastNet Business?
answer:
Below is the list of various Foreign Currency Accounts you can access on FastNet Business and where you can view them.
To access the International for Financial Markets modules, you must complete the
FastNet Business Change Request form
to apply.
Account type
FastNet Business International Module
FastNet Business Financial Markets Module
Foreign Currency Accounts Call
Transactional Banking > Transaction > History >
Money Market & Foreign Currency
N/A
Foreign Currency Term Deposit
Transactional Banking > International Trade > International Reports
Select 'Division' then 'Term Deposit Report'
N/A
Trade Advance Loan
Transactional Banking > International Trade > International Reports
Select 'Division' then 'Trade Advance Report'
Financial Markets > Financial Markets Reports > Facility Information Report
Foreign Currency Loan
Transactional Banking > International Trade > International Reports
Financial Markets > Financial Markets Reports > Facility Information Report

question: Can ASB charge me fees and interest after allowing my account to be overdrawn?
answer:
Yes, attempting to withdraw more money than you have in your account is treated as a request for an unarranged overdraft. So it’s important to make sure you have enough money in your account for payments or automatic bills or arrange an overdraft ahead of time.
We may not agree to an unarranged overdraft, depending on your personal circumstances.
If you have received an unarranged overdraft, the
unarranged overdraft interest rates and fees
will apply. The interest on your overdraft is calculated daily and charged on a monthly basis. This, as well as the fee, is debited to your account on the second day of each month (or the last day if you have an ASB Unlimited account) and when your account is closed.

question: Can I see all my accounts in FastNet Business?
answer:
You can add any ASB account to your FastNet Business site with the correct authority. This includes NZ domestic accounts, loans, Term Deposits, Credit Cards and Foreign Currency accounts.
To add additional accounts to your FastNet Business site, please complete the
FastNet Business Change Request form
.
The form needs to be signed by the account signatories of the account(s) being loaded. Once complete, please scan and help to
fnbhelpdesk@asb.co.nz
or fax to 09 337 2102.
There is normally a 2-3 day turn around to adding additional accounts to a FastNet Business site.

question: How do I load a new account onto my FastNet Business site?
answer:
You can add multiple ASB accounts to your FastNet Business site as long as the request has been signed by the appropriate account signatories.
To add a new account to your site
- Print off the
FastNet Business Change Request Form
.
- Once completed, please scan and email your completed form to FNBHelpDesk@asb.co.nz.
Alternatively you can fax us on 09 337 2102
Please note that the signatories on the account loaded to your FastNet Business site must sign these forms.
Once your account(s) have been loaded the FastNet Business Helpdesk will email you confirmation with instructions on how to grant yourself and your user’s access.

question: What is FATCA and how does it affect me?
answer:
The Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA) requires New Zealand Financial Institutions (like ASB) to collect and share the account information of US persons, US entities and New Zealand entities controlled by US persons with the New Zealand Inland Revenue Department (IRD). The IRD will then pass this information on to the United States’ Internal Revenue Service (IRS).
ASB has a regulatory requirement to comply with the Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA).
FATCA is United States legislation that aims to reduce tax evasion of US citizens, tax residents and entities that have bank accounts outside of the US.
New Zealand’s involvement with FATCA
FATCA is enabled in New Zealand through the Double Tax Agreements Order 2014 and the Intergovernmental Agreement and Memorandum of Understanding between New Zealand and the United States.
This could affect you
If you receive a letter or email from us relating to FATCA please contact us – even if you think FATCA doesn’t apply to you. We may be required to collect additional information from customers in order to determine their correct FATCA status.
The questions ASB (and other financial institutions) might ask you
To make this as easy for you as possible we can collect this information over the phone. In order to ensure the information we have for your account is correct and meets legal and regulatory requirements, we might ask you:
For Individual Customers:
For your Country(s) of Citizenship
For your Country(s) of Foreign Tax Residence (Countries outside of New Zealand you have an obligation to complete a tax return)
