We require the permission to enable users to select cell phone numbers and email addresses from their contact list for the Pay to Mobile/Email function.
Get accounts:
This is used for sending notifications.
Storage permission:
This is to allow you to share screenshots of payment confirmation pages if you wish.

question: How do I set up Clever Kash?
answer:
Setting up Clever Kash is easy, just follow these steps:
Go to the settings menu in your smartphone and turn on Bluetooth®.
Then log in to your ASB Mobile Banking app.
Select your child’s ASB savings account that you want to link Clever Kash to.
Tap on the three dots in the top right-hand corner and select ‘Clever Kash’ in the dropdown menu.
Tap ‘Set up Clever Kash’.
Give Clever Kash a gentle shake to wake him and his tummy will light up. Try shaking Clever Kash up and down. If this doesn’t work, you may need to charge the battery.
Once Clever Kash is awake, select Add Device in the top right-hand corner of the ASB Mobile Banking app. When you tap Add Device, Clever Kash automatically links to the app when your Bluetooth® is turned on. You don't need to pair the device.
Follow the prompts to link your Clever Kash to your child’s ASB savings account.
Give Clever Kash a name, and it will appear on his tummy.
Confirm your child’s ASB savings account to link to Clever Kash. Once you’re finished, tap Continue in the top right-hand corner.
If you have more than one Clever Kash to set up, follow the same steps.

question: Why can’t I see my Clever Kash on Bluetooth®?
answer:
If you’re having trouble seeing Clever Kash with Bluetooth® turned on, here are a few tips you can try to resolve this:
Try connecting to Clever Kash using another mobile device.
If you are using an Android device, then ensure your location services are turned on before attempting to connect.
If none of these tips help, then please email
cleverkashsupport@asb.co.nz
for assistance.

question: I can’t update my Clever Kash anymore, what should I do?
answer:
Make sure that there are new transactions to update. Check the last date that you updated Clever Kash (this is on Clever Kash’s screen) and check your child’s ASB savings account on the ASB Mobile Banking app to see if there have been any new transactions since this date.
If the balance in the account does not match what is showing on Clever Kash’s tummy, try manually updating Clever Kash. This can be done by:
Selecting the Clever Kash tab in the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Selecting the account you wish to update.
Then tap on ‘Update Clever Kash’.
If Clever Kash is still not updating, please email
cleverkashsupport@asb.co.nz
for assistance.

question: How do I unlink Clever Kash from an account?
answer:
Before you complete the actions below, ensure Bluetooth® is turned on, that Clever Kash is awake and within 3 metres of your smartphone.
Log in to the ASB Mobile Banking app and tap on the 'More' menu in the bottom bar and select 'Clever Kash'.
Tap on the account that you would like to unlink Clever Kash from.
Then select 'Unlink Clever Kash'. Your app will then connect to Clever Kash and unlink from him.
You can then choose to set up Clever Kash again using a different account or smartphone when you are ready.

question: I can’t connect my ASB Mobile Banking app to Clever Kash, how do I do this?
answer:
If you can’t see the Clever Kash device in the ASB Mobile Banking app, here are a few tips you can try to resolve this.
Make sure Clever Kash is awake by giving him a gentle shake.
If he doesn't wake up, your Clever Kash may be low on battery and needs to be charged.
Ensure
Bluetooth®
is turned on. If you are using an Android device, you will need to turn on location services as well.
Ensure your Clever Kash is within 3-5 metres of your smartphone so that he can connect using
Bluetooth.
Check the bank account that Clever Kash is linked to. Clever Kash can only be linked to one child’s savings account at a time, so you won’t be able to connect if he is linked with a different bank account.

question: How do I change the name of Clever Kash?
answer: If you want to change the name of the Clever Kash that appears in your ASB Mobile Banking app and on Clever Kash’s tummy, you can do this by unlinking Clever Kash, then setting him up again using the different name that you want to appear.

question: How do I change the account Clever Kash is set up with?
answer: If you want to change the account that Clever Kash is linked to, you will need to unlink Clever Kash then follow the set up process again.

question: Who can update Clever Kash?
answer:
Clever Kash can be updated by any person that has authority over the child’s ASB savings account (usually a parent or guardian), and has set up Clever Kash using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
However, anyone can transfer money to the child’s bank account. The parent or guardian, who is linked to Clever Kash, can then sit with the child and swipe the money to their Clever Kash using the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: How do I transfer money to Clever Kash?
answer:
If you want to transfer money to display on Clever Kash, like when you want to pay your child pocket money, simply make a transfer to that account just like you normally would in your ASB Mobile Banking app, then:
Open your ASB Mobile Banking app, and go to your child’s savings account summary page.
Click Update Clever Kash.
Anyone can transfer money to the child’s bank account and then the parent who has authority over the child’s account can sit with the child and swipe the money to their Clever Kash using the ASB Mobile Banking app.
As with any savings account, any interest and/or fees that apply will be shown in the total account balance. When you update Clever Kash, the latest balance will display on his tummy.

question: Will Clever Kash change how the child’s bank account displays, i.e. on statements?
answer: No, the child’s bank account is not affected or changed in any way through using Clever Kash. Transactions happen on the child’s bank account as they usually would. The difference is that you can make the child’s savings more tangible to them, by enabling them to view their bank balance on Clever Kash and swiping their savings or pocket money to Clever Kash using the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: Both my partner and I want to use Clever Kash with our child, can we do so?
answer: If you both have authority over the child’s account you can both be linked to the Clever Kash device – but note you can’t both update Clever Kash at the same time.

question: My child uses ASB’s school banking, how does this work with Clever Kash?
answer: School banking works as it currently does, where your child takes their money into school and it is then collected and deposited into their account. But, now with Clever Kash, you can show your child how the money they deposited into their account at school is accumulating. Deposits made via school banking can be swiped to update Clever Kash.

question: Is Clever Kash safe for children?
answer:
Yes he is. Clever Kash has passed all recommended safety standards.
Note, for health and safety reasons, Clever Kash should only be used by children over the age of 3.

question: I have more than one child, do I need one Clever Kash for each child?
answer: Yes, each child should have their own Clever Kash as long as they are between the ages of 3-12. If you wanted to swap the same Clever Kash from one child to another, you would need to go through a full set up process each time.

question: How do I update the balance on Clever Kash?
answer:
Before you can update your balance, you need to wake Clever Kash up. Gently shake Clever Kash and the screen will light up. Remember, you need to have your phone’s Bluetooth® and GPS (for Android only)  turned on to use Clever Kash. You can turn this on in your phone’s settings.
The first thing you need to do is transfer money into your child’s ASB savings account.
Log in to the ASB Mobile app, tap on the main menu and select Transfers.
Transfer money to your child’s ASB savings account that is linked to Clever Kash.
Once the transfer is complete, tap the Update Clever Kash button on the success screen. Note, there is also an Update Clever Kash button on your child’s account summary page if you want to update Clever Kash later.
To update Clever Kash’s balance on his tummy, you and your child need to swipe the virtual notes and coins towards Clever Kash.
Once you’re finished, Clever Kash will display your child’s updated account balance on his tummy and show your child’s progress towards their savings goals.

question: Can someone other than the parents (like aunts and uncles) transfer money to Clever Kash?
answer: Anyone can transfer money to the child’s bank account and then the parent who has authority over the child’s account can sit with the child and swipe the money to their Clever Kash using the ASB Mobile banking app.

question: Why is the balance on Clever Kash different to the balance in my child’s account?
answer:
Clever Kash does not automatically update when a deposit has been made into your child’s ASB savings account. This is why the balance on Clever Kash’s tummy can be different to the balance of your child’s ASB savings account that is linked to Clever Kash.
The reason why it doesn’t automatically update is because we want you (the parent/guardian) to sit with your child each time you update Clever Kash – so that each update is an opportunity to teach your children about money and savings. To make sure the balance does not get out of date, it is good to update Clever Kash regularly.

question: Who can I contact if I need help with Clever Kash?
answer:
If you can’t find the answer to your question in our
Clever Kash FAQs
, you can call
0800 803 804
for assistance.

question: There is an option to name Clever Kash in the settings, what do I call him?
answer: You can call Clever Kash whatever you like. You could use the name of your child, or you might like to let your child make up a name for their Clever Kash. Giving Clever Kash a name will help you to distinguish between more than one Clever Kash.

question: If someone gains access to Clever Kash, can they then access my account directly?
answer: No, as all data is sent from the ASB Mobile Banking app to Clever Kash and controlled entirely by the parent/guardian. If someone else gained access to your Clever Kash they can only see your last updated balance but they cannot see any transactions, access funds or make any changes to your accounts.

question: I have more than one child, can I use my smartphone to update all their Clever Kash’s?
answer: Yes, that’s easy. A smartphone can be linked to more than one Clever Kash. You can set up and link each of your children’s Clever Kash to your smartphone, but you will need to update them all separately. You can manage and update all your children’s Clever Kash by going to the ‘Clever Kash’ tab in the main menu on ASB Mobile.

question: How is Clever Kash powered, does he need charging and how long does this last?
answer: Clever Kash is battery powered and if used for a few minutes a day he will last about 7 days before requiring a recharge via micro USB. The battery is rechargeable and is not able to be changed.

question: Does Clever Kash connect to the internet?
answer:
No. Clever Kash uses
Bluetooth®
low energy technology to connect to the ASB Mobile Banking app which connects to the internet.

question: Can I use Clever Kash while he is charging?
answer: To set up or use Clever Kash, you will need to unplug him from the charger.

question: Is communication between Clever Kash and the ASB Mobile Banking app secure?
answer: We've taken precautions to ensure a high level of security and data encryption. All communication between the ASB Mobile Banking app and Clever Kash are encrypted with no account information stored on the device.

question: What is GetWise?
answer:
GetWise is a financial literacy programme designed to teach Kiwi kids to become cash clever. Over one million kids have participated in ASB GetWise, New Zealand's largest in-school financial literacy programme, and we’re pleased to be offering more kiwi kids taking out a Headstart account, the chance to learn money-smart skills with Mission Control.
Find out more about
GetWise
.

question: How to set up and manage recurring payments with ongoing payment consents.
answer:
Ongoing payment consents are a secure and convenient way for you to manage recurring payments. They're an alternative to direct debits and give you more control over the payments you make.
There are a couple of ways to set one up:
On a desktop or laptop
When you are on a merchant or a third parties' website, and you're ready to make a payment, you’ll be redirected to an ASB webpage that shows a QR code, or you’ll receive a notification.
Use the ASB Mobile Banking app
to scan the QR code.
Follow the instructions to authenticate yourself, then select the account that you want payments to be made from and authorise your consent.
On a mobile device
When you are on a merchant or a third parties' website, and you're ready to make a payment, you’ll be redirected directly to the ASB Mobile app.
Follow the instructions to authenticate yourself, then select the account that you want payments to be made from and authorise your consent.
If you need to view, or stop an ongoing payment consent:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings'.
Under 'Open banking', tap 'Ongoing payment consents'.
Select the payment consent to view or stop it.

question: How can I switch between light and dark mode in the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
You can personalise the colour of the ASB Mobile app by switching between dark and light mode.
Here's how:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, and under ‘Personalise’ select ‘Appearance’.
Choose between light or dark mode.

question: How do I turn push notifications on or off for the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Firstly, enable push notifications on your mobile device:
For iPhone / iPad:
Open the ‘Settings’ app.
Scroll to ‘ASB Mobile’.
Select ‘Notifications’.
Ensure ‘Allow Notifications’ is enabled.
For Android devices (e.g. Samsung):
Open the ‘Settings’ app.
Select ‘Apps’.
Select ‘ASB Mobile’.
Select ‘Notifications’.
Swipe right on the toggle bar to enable notifications.
Note: These steps may differ depending on the version of your device, please refer to the official guides on the Apple and Google websites for more information:
Apple Support
.
Android Help
.
Secondly, enable push notifications within your ASB Mobile app settings:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, and under ‘Notifications’ and then select ‘Push notifications’.
Tap the selector to activate push notifications.
If you are still not receiving push notifications from ASB:
Please try restarting your phone (turning it off and back on again).

question: I have a new phone. How do I set up the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Make sure you know your
FastNet Classic internet banking
password. If you don’t know your password, you may be able to
reset it on your old device
.
Follow these steps to set up the
ASB Mobile Banking app
on your new phone:
De-register the app on your old phone
:
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, and under ‘Devices’ select ‘Manage devices’.
Select the device you wish to deregister and tap 'Remove device'.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Manage devices’.
Select the device you wish to deregister and click 'Remove device'.
Click 'Remove' to confirm.
Download and register
the ASB Mobile Banking app on your new device
.
If you’re having trouble registering the app on your new device, uninstall the ASB Mobile Banking app, then install it again and continue with the registration process.

question: Why am I receiving a ‘Could not load the ASB Mobile Banking app’ error for Open Banking authorisations when I already have the app installed?
answer:
There are a few reasons you could be experiencing this error. You’ll need to investigate your own phone browser settings (e.g. Chrome, Safari, Samsung) or phone settings, but some tips could include:
Ensure your browser on your phone is set up to open links/other apps.
Your Samsung browser may not accept the opening of http/https links. Please try another browser.
On your Samsung device check the following settings by following these steps:
Apps
Choose default apps
Opening links
ASB Mobile
Check that ‘Open supported links’ is ‘on’

question: How do I use a QR code to set up an ongoing payment consent?
answer:
To set up an ongoing payment consent, you'll need to use the QR code scanner in the ASB Mobile Banking app.
To find and use the QR code scanner:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap 'Payments' at the bottom of the screen.
Select 'Scan QR code to pay'.
Scan the QR code on your desktop screen and follow the instructions to set up the payment.
The scanner will only work for ASB QR codes and ​as these codes are encrypted with specific data for the consent you are authorising, you won't be able to use your regular camera, only the ASB Mobile app to scan it.​
​
If you run into any difficulties scanning the QR code, wait for a moment, then try again. If the QR code expires, refresh it and then try again.​

question: Can I see my last credit card statement?
answer:
Yes. You will receive a notification in FastNet Classic and the ASB Mobile banking app to view, download or share a PDF file of your credit card statement.
The ASB Mobile credit card statement page also shows you a summary of your last statement so you can see your transactions at a glance.
You can also use the ASB Mobile app to
view and download your credit card statements from the last 12 months
.

question: Are there terms & conditions for using ASB Mobile Banking?
answer:
Yes. You can see The Terms and Conditions for using the ASB Mobile Banking app in the Apple App Store and in Google Play. By downloading the App you accept these conditions. Terms and Conditions are viewable in the ‘About’ section of the app once you have downloaded it.
Watch our video to find out tips on how you can manage your money on the go:
Refresh page for YouTube video to render.

question: How do I set up Google Pay?
answer:
You can set up and add cards to Google Pay from within the
ASB Mobile Banking app
or alternatively in the Google Pay app.
Before you add your cards, you will need to ensure the NFC functionality is switched on and set up a screen lock on your phone. If you turn off the screen lock, there are Google Pay features that will remove any cards you have added to Google Pay for your security.
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap ‘More’ at the bottom of the screen.
Tap ‘Cards and devices’ and choose the card you want to add.
Tap 'Add to G Pay', follow the prompts, and read and accept the terms and conditions.
That’s it. You can now start using Google Pay.
Google Pay app:
Launch the Google Pay app. You may be prompted to download Google Pay from the Google Play Store.
Select ‘Add a card’.
Follow the prompts, and read and accept the terms and conditions.
You must also remember to make sure your tap and pay settings are correct on your Android device. This can be done through your device Settings menu by searching, ‘Tap and Pay’ to ensure your default mobile payment service is set to Google Pay.

question: How do I update the OS version on my Apple (iOS) device?
answer:
As steps may vary between devices, we strongly recommend you follow the instructions set out by your device’s manufacturer.
Visit the
Apple support guide
.
Please note: Some older devices may not be able to apply the latest update, in this scenario, it’s best to visit the manufacturer’s website for more information.
Why should I keep my devices up to date?
Software updates aren’t just about adding new features or fixing bugs, they’re also about fixing weaknesses, or vulnerabilities. Vulnerabilities are parts of your device’s software that can be used for purposes other than those intended. It’s through these vulnerabilities that attackers can gain access to your device and your information.
The New Zealand Government has established a national Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT), to help inform, monitor and report cyber security issues across New Zealand. The
CERT website
has a wealth of information on Cyber security.
Find out more information on
why you should keep your devices and software up to date
.

question: Why is there a limit to the number of devices that I can register the ASB Mobile Banking app on at any one time?
answer:
It's a security consideration. ASB Mobile requires Netcode at registration, not for subsequent payments, so we encourage customers to actively manage the number of registered mobile devices via the ASB Mobile app or FastNet Classic internet banking.
ASB Mobile app:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app.
Tap the profile icon in the top left corner of the ‘Accounts’ screen.
Tap ‘Settings’, and under ‘Devices’ select ‘Manage devices’.
Select the device you wish to deregister and tap 'Remove device'.
FastNet Classic:
Log in to your FastNet Classic account.
Click on 'Settings' in the top right of the page.
Click on ‘Manage devices’.
Select the device you wish to deregister and click 'Remove device'.
Click 'Remove' to confirm.
This will allow you to register a new device to your account.

question: Can I see my statements in the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Yes, to see your recent statements in the ASB Mobile app:
Log into the ASB Mobile app.
On the accounts screen, tap the account or credit card you want to view statements for.
When the summary screen opens, tap ‘More options’.
Then select ‘Statements’.
You’ll see the last twelve months of statements (if available).
To share or download the statement, just tap on the share icon in the top right of the screen for Android, or the bottom of the screen for iOS.
Find out how to
view older statements
by logging into FastNet Classic internet banking.

question: How do I update the OS version on my Android device?
answer:
As steps may vary between devices, we strongly recommend you follow the instructions set out by your device’s manufacturer.
Find out how to
check & update your Android version
.
Please note: Some older devices may not be able to apply the latest update, in this scenario, it’s best to visit the manufacturer’s website for more information.
Why should I keep my devices up to date?
Software updates aren’t just about adding new features or fixing bugs, they’re also about fixing weaknesses, or vulnerabilities. Vulnerabilities are parts of your device’s software that can be used for purposes other than those intended. It’s through these vulnerabilities that attackers can gain access to your device and your information.
The New Zealand Government has established a national Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT), to help inform, monitor and report cyber security issues across New Zealand. The
CERT website
has a wealth of information on Cyber security.
Find out more information on
why you should keep your devices and software up to date
.

question: How do I update the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
If you haven’t set your device to update apps automatically, here’s how to manually update the ASB Mobile Banking app for iOS and Android.
To manually update the ASB Mobile Banking app with Apple iOS:
Open the App Store
Tap your profile icon at the top of the screen
Scroll to see pending updates and release notes. Tap 'Update' next to an app to update only that app, or tap 'Update All'
Please note:
You may be asked to enter your Apple ID in order to complete the update.
For more information on how to enable automatic app updates, visit
Apple’s support guide
.
To manually update the ASB Mobile Banking app with Android OS:
Open the Google Play Store app
Tap Menu, My apps & games
Apps with an update available are labelled ‘Update’
Find the ASB Mobile app and tap ‘Update’
For more information on how to enable automatic app updates, visit
Android’s support guide
.
Why should I keep my devices up to date?
Software updates aren’t just about adding new features or fixing bugs, they’re also about fixing weaknesses, or vulnerabilities. Vulnerabilities are parts of your device’s software that can be used for purposes other than those intended. It’s through these vulnerabilities that attackers can gain access to your device and your information.
The New Zealand Government has established a national Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT), to help inform, monitor and report cyber security issues across New Zealand. The
CERT website
has a wealth of information on Cyber security.
Find out more information on
why you should keep your devices and software up to date
.

question: Can I use Apple Pay if I report my ASB Visa card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using Apple Pay from the next day.
If you currently use Apple Pay, when you call ASB to report your card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions on it, your physical card will be deactivated, the card will be removed from Apple Pay, and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated on your ASB Mobile Banking app overnight and you will first need to add the card into Apple Pay again via the ASB Mobile app to use Apple Pay on your device from around 5 am the next day.

question: What’s the ASB Mobile Banking app?
answer:
Take the bank with you in your pocket.
Download the ASB Mobile Banking app
to your smartphone, and you can check on your loan, make an extra loan payment and much more.
Find out
what you need to use the ASB Mobile Banking app
.

question: Which ASB cards can be used with Apple Pay?
answer: You can use all ASB Visa Debit and ASB Visa credit cards to make payments using Apple Pay.

question: What happens to Apple Pay if my phone is lost or stolen?
answer:
Go to Apple ID account page or use
Find My iPhone
to suspend or permanently remove the ability to pay from that device with Apple Pay.
On the Apple ID account page, sign in and click your device. In the information that appears, go to the Apple Pay section and click either Remove or Remove all.
Your card or cards will be suspended or removed from Apple Pay even if your device is offline and not connected to a cellular or Wi-Fi network.
Alternatively, customers can suspend or resume using Apple Pay through the ASB Contact Centre by calling 0800 803 804.
We also have this useful guide to help you.
What do I do if my device has been lost or stolen?

question: What do I do when the ASB Mobile Banking app is telling me I have too many devices registered?
answer:
If you exceed the device limit, you will be prompted to remove devices when you log in on the ASB Mobile app.
Follow the on-screen instructions to review and remove devices in order to proceed.
You can also manage/remove devices through FastNet Classic by selecting your username in the top-right hand corner and clicking 'Manage Devices'.
Please note:
If you experience technical issues when managing your devices, you will still be able to proceed with basic banking activities until services resume.

question: I'm a merchant, will customers need to enter a PIN when paying with Apple Pay?
answer: No, your customer should not need to enter a PIN when using Apple Pay. In some cases, for transactions over $200 customers may need to enter their card PIN on the payment terminal when using Apple Pay in store.

question: I forgot the PIN for my ASB Mobile app, can you tell me what it is?
answer:
For security reasons if you’ve forgotten your PIN, you’ll need to reset it.
Open your ASB Mobile Banking app.
Tap on 'Login options' in the bottom left corner of the PIN screen and then tap on 'Forgot PIN'.
Enter your FastNet Classic password to log in.
Tap on the person icon in the top right corner of the Accounts screen.
Tap on 'Password & security'.
Tap on 'Change PIN'.

question: Which ASB cards can I use for Google Pay?
answer:
You can add your ASB Visa Debit and/or ASB Visa credit card to Google Pay via the
ASB Mobile app
or within the Google Pay app on your Android device. You can add multiple cards to Google Pay, however only one card can be set as the default payment card.

question: Where can Google Pay transactions be viewed?
answer:
For a full list of transactions made you can log on to
ASB FastNet Classic
or download or open the
ASB Mobile Banking app
.
You can also view details on payments you've made using Google Pay using the Google Pay app.
Open the Google Pay app.
Touch the card you used to pay.
Find and tap your transaction.

question: How do I make my ASB card the default card for Google Pay?
answer:
Your default card is the first card you add to Google Pay and is the card that is used when paying with your phone.  If you have multiple cards in your wallet and your ASB card is not set as the default, you can change this from within the
ASB Mobile app
:
Log in to the ASB Mobile app from your Android phone, select ‘Cards and Devices’ from the menu and choose the card you want to make default.
Tap on the G Pay tile.
In the options available, tap on “Set as default card“.

question: What is Google Pay?
answer:
Google Pay
(G Pay) is a simple and secure way to make purchases instore, online and in-app at online checkouts – all from your Android Device. Plus, the Google Pay app keeps your ASB Visa Debit or ASB Visa credit cards, loyalty or gift vouchers, and much more together in one place.

question: Why is there a $2.00 charge on my account when I add Google Pay?
answer:
When you add Google Pay to an ASB Visa card via the Google Pay app, Google will process a $2.00 authorisation to check that the card is valid. This $2.00 transaction is automatically reversed back into the card account that was authorised once Google Pay has been added to that card. This may take a few minutes to clear.
When you are adding Google Pay via the Google Pay app, you should remove any Card Controls that you have in place prior to commencing to allow the authorisation to go through and check the card is valid.
If you add Google Pay via the
ASB Mobile Banking app
the $2.00 authorisation is not triggered from Google.

question: How do I change the card that I pay with?
answer:
The first card that is added to Google Pay is the default card. Customers can choose to add multiple cards.
If you have multiple cards loaded from ASB, you can change your default card from within the
ASB Mobile app
from the Device Details screen. If you have multiple cards loaded which include other issuers, you can set your desired default card from within the Google Pay app from the Payment tab.

question: Do I need to do anything with Google Pay if I have received a reissued card?
answer:
There is no requirement to update card information with Google Pay. Once you have activated your ASB reissued card, your Google Pay card will automatically be updated with your new card details.
If you have lost or misplaced your ASB Visa card, you are still able to use Google Pay for all contactless transactions. You can lock and replace your ASB Visa plastic card using ASB Card Control in the
ASB Mobile app
or replace your card using
FastNetClassic internet banking
.

question: What does pending verification, suspended and default status mean on my Google Pay tile In the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
ASB provides a status icon and text for customers on the Google Pay tile within the
ASB Mobile app
to show customers what their Google Pay status is.
Status: "Pending verification"
An ASB card was added to Google Pay but further verification is needed. Either a onetime passcode from your ASB Netcode registered mobile phone is required to be entered or contacting the ASB Contact Centre for I.D. verification.
To complete this process from the ASB Mobile app, simply tap on the Google Pay device tile to reach the Device details screen. If the status is “Pending verification”, you will see there is a “Verify” link on this screen. Tap this link and follow the prompts to complete the verification process.
Status: “Suspended”
An ASB card is suspended by ASB and cannot be currently used as payment method for Google Pay. Please call us on
0800 803 804
, or from overseas call us during New Zealand business hours (international toll charges apply) on +64 9 306 3000.
Status: Default
An ASB Visa card is set as the default payment card for any Google Pay transactions. Only one card or account can be set as the default card at any given time.
If you have Google Pay loaded on more than one ASB card, you can change the default card in the ASB app by clicking on the Google Pay tile in the cards and devices screen on the card that is not currently set as default, then tapping on the “set as default” option.
If you wish to use a different ASB card to make a one off purchase, but do not wish to set it as default, you can do this by:
Opening the Google Pay app.
At the top of the screen, on your card, swipe left until you find the card you want to use.
Pay as normal, by holding the back of your phone against the contactless payment terminal.
When the payment is made, your phone will beep or vibrate
If prompted, choose ‘Credit’ regardless of your type of card.
For transactions over NZ$80 in New Zealand, you will be asked to enter your card PIN on the terminal. The terminal will indicate if the transaction has been successful.

question: How do I remove a card from Google Pay?
answer:
There are two ways to remove a card from Google Pay.
Through the ASB Mobile app:
Open the ASB Mobile app.
Select 'Cards and Devices' from the menu.
Select the Google Pay tile linked to the card you want to remove and follow the prompts.
Through the Google Pay app:
Open the Google Pay app.
Select the card you want to remove.
Scroll down and select 'Remove card'.

question: How do I make an online or in-app payment with Google Pay?
answer:
To use Google Pay for in-app purchases or when you shop online, look out for the Google Pay icon at the checkout of the online store or the app.
At checkout, simply select the Google Pay purchase button.
Before confirming your order, you might be asked to pick a payment method and enter your shipping address.

question: Can I delete a card I have added to Apple Pay from within the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
No, cards added to Apple Wallet can only be deleted from the Wallet app. This option is accessible when you have tapped on the card you want to remove. Once on the card screen select the three dotted menu icon, scroll down and select “Remove This Card.”
Further information on managing the cards that you use with Apple Pay can be found here:
https://support.apple.com/en-nz/HT205583

question: Am I able to see or set a default Apple Pay Card from within the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
No, within the ASB Mobile app you will be able to see all the cards you have added to Apple Pay, but you won’t be able to know or set the default payment card. This can be done by:
Go to Settings > Wallet & Apple Pay on your iPhone or iPad
Scroll down to 'Transaction Defaults'.
Tap 'Default Card', then select the card you would like to use as your default.
On iPhone, you can also open the Apple Wallet App, touch and hold a card, then drag it to the front of your cards, to make it your default.
Further information on managing the cards that you use with Apple Pay can be found here:
https://support.apple.com/en-nz/HT205583

question: What does pending verification and suspended mean on my Apple Pay tile In the ASB Mobile app?
answer:
ASB provides a status icon and text for customers on the Apple Pay tile within the ASB Mobile app to show customers what their Apple Pay status is.
Status: "Pending verification"
An ASB card was added to Apple Wallet but further verification is needed. Either a onetime passcode from your ASB Netcode registered mobile phone is required to be entered or contacting the ASB contact centre for ID verification.
To complete this process from the ASB Mobile app, simply tap on the Apple Pay device tile to reach the Device details screen. If the status is “Pending verification” you will see there is a “Verify” link on this screen. Tap this link and follow the prompts to complete the verification process.
Status: “Suspended”
An ASB card is suspended by ASB and cannot be currently used as payment method for Apple Pay. Please call us on 0800 803 804, or from overseas call us during New Zealand business hours (international toll charges apply) on +64 9 306 3000.

question: Why do I see the error “There was a problem completing your request. Please check your internet connection and try again” when I want to view a document in the ASB Mobile app?
answer: There is a newer version of the ASB Mobile app available. Please update your ASB Mobile app to the latest version to resolve this error.

question: Do I need to do anything with Apple Pay if I have received a reissued card?
answer:
There is no requirement to update card information with Apple Pay. Once you have activated your ASB reissued card, your Apple Pay card will automatically be updated with your new card details.
If you have lost or misplaced your ASB Visa card, you are still able to use Apple Pay for all contactless transactions. You can lock and replace your ASB Visa plastic card using ASB Card Control in the ASB Mobile app or replace your card using FastNetClassic internet banking.

question: How and where can I make payments using Apple Pay?
answer:
Hold your iPhone over the terminal and you’ll be prompted to authenticate using either Face ID, Touch ID or the device passcode, to authorise the payment. There is no requirement to unlock the phone to Pay.
If you’re paying within apps or on the web with Safari look for the 'Apple Pay' icon during checkout. Simply tap the icon to review your payment information and confirm your contact details are correct; then use either Face ID, Touch ID or passcode or double click the side button on your Apple Watch to complete your purchase. The word 'Done' and a tick will appear on your screen when your payment is successful.
Using Bluetooth, you can select your iPhone or Apple Watch to authorise a web payment (Safari only) on your Mac. You will need to ensure all your devices have handoff enabled to ensure your Mac is able to discover them to complete a purchase.
Using Apple Pay to make purchases in store does not require an internet connection. Your device uses Near Field Communications (NFC) to communicate with the terminal.
Apple Pay works like all contactless Visa Debit and Visa credit cards, however all Apple Pay transactions are authenticated using either Face ID, Touch ID or the device passcode, there's no need to enter a PIN. Payments over $80 may require you to enter your account PIN at the contactless terminal (depending on the terminal).

question: What is Apple Pay and where can I use it?
answer:
Apple Pay is a payment method that lets you use your compatible iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch and Mac to securely pay with your eligible ASB cards in store, within apps and on the web with Safari.
When using Apple Pay in store, look out for the contactless symbol or where contactless payments are accepted. For ASB customers, Apple Pay will work in all countries wherever contactless payments are accepted. We recommend you take your plastic card as a backup when travelling overseas.
You can read more about Apple Pay on the
Apple Pay website
. You can also provide your
feedback.

question: I'm a merchant, what is a device account number and will it show on the receipt when a customer uses Apple Pay?
answer:
Instead of using a card number, Apple Pay assigns a unique Device Account Number (Token). When a customer makes a purchase, this number, along with a transaction-specific code, is used to process the payment – meaning you will never see card details.
The last four digits of the Device Account Number will appear on customer receipts instead of the last four digits of the card number.

question: I'm a merchant, how do I process refunds with Apple Pay?
answer:
Refunds for an Apple Pay purchase are processed in a similar way to traditional refund.
Please note that it is important that refunds are processed on the same card as the original transaction.
You will need to obtain the Device Account Number to find the purchase and process the return.
To see the last four or five digits of the Device Account Number, ask the customer to go to Wallet, tap the card, and tap info on the lower-right corner of the display. You can also have the customer hold their iPhone or Apple Watch near the reader, select the card they used to make the original payment, and authorise the return with Touch ID or passcode.

question: I'm a merchant, how will the customer authenticate the transaction for an Apple Pay payment within an app or a website (Safari only)?
answer: Customers will need to use either Face ID, Touch ID or a device passcode to verify all Apple Pay transactions. Using Bluetooth customers can authorise web payments (Safari only) on their Mac through their iPhone or Apple Watch. Customers will need to ensure their devices have handoff enabled to complete a purchase.

question: I'm a merchant, what are Apple Pay in app or web payments (Safari only)?
answer: Apple Pay provides an easy and secure way for customers with eligible ASB credit and debit cards to pay for goods and services in your iOS app or website (Safari only) with a single touch without having to enter payment, billing and contact details.

question: I'm a merchant, how do customers authorise a payment when using Apple Pay?
answer: When a customer makes a payment, they will authorise the payment via Face ID, Touch ID, or their device passcode. The terminal will indicate if the transaction has been successful.

question: I'm a merchant, how can I let my customers know they can pay with Apple Pay?
answer:
The Apple Pay payment mark can be used in websites, emails, online display ads, terminals, digital displays, and in-store to let your customers know that you accept Apple Pay.
The Apple Pay digital payment mark artwork and guidelines for usage can be downloaded on the Apple Pay website.

question: I'm a merchant, how do I start accepting Apple Pay payments in my store?
answer:
If you accept contactless payments today, you're likely to be able to accept Apple Pay payments.
If you're not already accepting contactless or would like to accept in app payments, contact the ASB Merchant Sales team on 0800 272 555 to find out more.

question: I'm a merchant, are Apple Pay Payments charges as card present or card not present?
answer: Payments made using Apple Pay in stores are card present transactions. Payments made using Apple Pay within apps are card not present transactions.

question: I'm a merchant, are there additional fees to accept Apple Pay payments?
answer: There are no additional fees to accept Apple Pay.

question: What happens if I did not receive the text message containing my OTP (One Time Pin) when setting up Apple Pay?
answer:
The One Time Pin (OTP) will be sent to your Netcode registered mobile number. If you did not receive your OTP it is likely that this mobile number is out of date. We can help you, just
give our contact centre a call
or pop into your
local branch
to change your Netcode registered mobile number.

question: How do I remove an ASB card that I use for Apple Pay?
answer:
If you need to, you can remove a card from your device:
iPhone or iPad
To remove a card that you use on your iPhone or iPad, go to Settings > Wallet & Apple Pay, tap the card that you want to remove, then tap Remove Card.
Or open Wallet, tap a card, tap symbol with the 3 dots, scroll down, then tap Remove Card.
Apple Watch
To remove a card that you use on your Apple Watch, open the Watch app on your iPhone, go to the My Watch tab, scroll down, tap Wallet & Apple Pay, tap the card, then tap Remove Card.
Or tap Wallet on the Home screen of your Apple Watch, tap a card, press it firmly, and tap Remove Card.
Mac
To remove a card that you use on your Mac, go to System Preferences > Wallet & Apple Pay, click the card that you want to remove, then click the minus (–) sign to remove the card.
For more information on how to remove cards visit
Apple support
.

question: Why am I receiving a "call your bank" error message when loading Apple Pay?
answer: It sounds like you may not have activated your card. Please activate your new card, and add a pin to it, before adding your card into your Wallet.

question: Is Apple Pay secure?
answer:
Yes, using your ASB cards with Apple Pay is secure:
When you make a purchase, Apple Pay uses a device-specific number and a unique transaction code. Your full card number is never stored on your device or on Apple servers, and it can’t be shared with merchants. Apple Pay doesn’t keep transaction information that can be tied back to you, ensuring all purchases are private.
Every transaction on an iPhone, iPad or Mac requires you to authenticate with either Face ID, Touch ID or the device passcode. Apple Watch is protected by a passcode, and is required every time you put the Watch on.
Do not share your passcode or let anyone else, including family members, have their fingerprint registered to your device while your card is registered in Apple Pay.
Learn more about Apple Pay and its security
.

question: How do I change my default card for Apple Pay transactions?
answer:
The first card that you add to Wallet is your default card. If you add more cards and want to change your default card, use these steps.
iPhone or iPad
Go to Settings > Wallet & Apple Pay on your iPhone or iPad, and scroll down to Transaction Defaults. Tap Default Card, and then choose a new card.
On your iPhone, you can also open Wallet, touch and hold a card, then drag it to the front of your cards.
Apple Watch
Open the Apple Watch app on your iPhone. Tap the My Watch tab, tap Wallet & Apple Pay > Default Card, then choose a new card.
Mac models with Touch ID
Go to System Preferences > Wallet & Apple Pay. Choose a new card from the Default Card pop-up menu.
For more detailed information on how to manage cards visit
Apple support
.

question: Can I view previous Apple Pay transactions?
answer:
Yes, you can view your last transaction made using Apple Pay when tapping on your card in the Wallet app. The last 10 transactions made with your device can be viewed by tapping the transactions button.
For a full list of transactions made with your card log on to ASB FastNet Classic internet banking or download or open the ASB Mobile banking app.

question: How do I return an item purchased with Apple Pay in store?
answer:
If you need to return a purchase made with Apple Pay, you may be asked to provide the last four digits of your Device Account number. To find them, select
Settings > Wallet & Apple Pay > Card
> scroll down and view
'Device Account Number'
. You can also find it in the Wallet app by selecting the card and tapping on the symbol with the 3 dots on the lower right hand corner and then scrolling down and view 'Device Account Number'.

question: Do I still earn True Rewards when I use Apple Pay?
answer: Yes, you will continue to receive all of the same benefits, rewards, and security your eligible ASB card offers when you pay with Apple Pay.

question: Can I add more than one card to Apple Pay?
answer: Yes, you can add up to eight cards to Apple Pay on each device.

question: How do I set up Apple Pay?
answer:
Adding your card to Apple Pay is simple.* Here's how to get started.
iPhone
Open the Wallet app.
Tap the “+” sign in the upper-right corner.
To add the credit or debit card affiliated with your iTunes account, enter its security code.  Or tap "Add a different card" and use your iPhone camera to scan the card information.
Apple Watch
Open the Watch app on your iPhone.
Tap “Wallet & Apple Pay”, and then tap "Add Credit or Debit Card".
To add the credit or debit card affiliated with your iTunes account, enter its security code. Or tap "Add a different card" and use your iPhone camera to scan the card information.
iPad
Go to Settings.
Tap "Wallet & Apple Pay", and then tap "Add Credit or Debit Card".
To add the credit or debit card affiliated with your iTunes account, enter its security code.  Or select "Add a different card" and use your iPad camera to scan the card information.
MacBook Pro with Touch ID
Go to System Preferences.
Select "Wallet & Apple Pay", and then select "Add Card".
To add the credit or debit card affiliated with your iTunes account, enter its security code.  Or select "Add a different card" and use your MacBook Pro camera to scan the card information.
If you are having trouble adding your card, please send us a private message or call us on 0800 803 804 and we will check this out for you.
*For a list of compatible Apple Pay devices, see
Apple Support
.
Note:
Do not add your card to someone else's device as they can then use your card to pay. Learn more about adding a card to your device.

question: Why does ASB make updates to its Mobile Apps?
answer:
From time to time technology manufacturers make updates to their software, which means we need to update our apps in order to provide the best experience for customers. The main reasons we make these changes are:
To make sure we maintain security and data integrity
To continually improve the mobile experience
To respond to customer feedback for improvements
To take advantage of the latest technologies such as Touch ID, Smart Watches and Contactless payments
To make sure our apps continue to function properly on the latest mobile operating systems

question: I can't log in to ASB Mobile – what am I doing wrong?
answer:
If this is the first time you’ve tried to Log in to ASB Mobile, the first thing to check is your Username and Password.  This is the same Username and Password you use for FastNet Classic internet banking.
Check that the zeros, O's, ones or I's in the Username you selected and the Password we supplied to you are correct. It’s easy to miss-key these when entering them. If your password was changed after 23 May 2015, it will be case sensitive.
If you’ve selected your own Password, check it’s correct.  If it is correct and you still can’t log in, call the Mobile Help Desk on 0800 MOB BANK (
0800 662 226
).  If you are overseas, call us on + 64 9 306 3185.

question: If I get a replacement ASB Visa card, will my ASB Card Control settings continue to apply?
answer:
Any settings will continue to apply to a replacement card if the card number is identical.
Any settings will not continue to apply to a replacement card, if the card number is different.
If you get a new card, we encourage you to check your ASB Card Control settings.

question: How can I use Card Control?
answer: Log into the ASB Mobile Banking app > Select 'Cards' from the menu > Choose a card to command > Pick a feature to control. If you cannot see the 'Cards' menu, check that you have downloaded the latest update for the ASB Mobile Banking app.

question: What if I am not satisfied with the resolution of my complaint?
answer:
If we have not been able to resolve your complaint, you can:
Request that your complaint is passed onto one of our Customer Care managers
Contact one of our Customer Care managers directly on 0800 272 005 or +64 9 377 8930 (if calling from overseas)
Refer your complaint to the
Banking Ombudsman Scheme
for further consideration. The Banking Ombudsman provides a free and independent service that helps people with unresolved problems they have with their banking service providers
Report information about ASB or any of our advisors to the Financial Markets Authority on the contact details below:
fma.govt.nz
PO Box 1179
Wellington 6140

question: What happens when I make a complaint?
answer:
We want you to have a good banking experience. If you’re not satisfied with anything we’ve done,
please let us know
. We’ll try to put it right, quickly and fairly. If you do make a complaint, we’ll:
Let you know that we’ve received it within 48 hours
Keep you up-to-date with progress if it’s something we can’t resolve for you immediately
Consider your complaint fairly – letting you know the result within a reasonable time

question: How do I make a complaint?
answer:
Your feedback is important to us. If you are not completely satisfied with our products and services, we want to know as soon as possible so that we can resolve it quickly and fairly.
Ways you can make a complaint:
Send us your feedback using the
online complaint form
Log in to FastNet Classic, go to ‘My Messages’ and send us a secure message
Talk to the team at your nearest ASB branch
Call us on 0800 803 804 or +64 9 306 3000 (if calling from overseas) any day between 6:30 am and 11:00 pm NZT

question: Can an enduring power of attorney manage ongoing payment consents on my behalf?
answer: Yes, an enduring power of attorney will have the authority to set up an ongoing payment consent on behalf of a customer and will have the authority to revoke that consent in the consent management feature in the ASB Mobile Banking App.​

question: Can I use Google Wallet on my Fitbit device if I report my ASB Visa card has been lost or misplaced?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using Google Wallet on your Fitbit device from the next day.
If you currently use Google Wallet on your Fitbit device, when you call ASB to report your physical card as lost, your physical card will be deactivated and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated on your Google Wallet overnight and you will be able to use Google Wallet on your Fitbit device from around 5 am the next day.
You can
lock and replace
your ASB Visa card using ASB Card Control in the ASB Mobile Banking app or
replace your card
using FastNet Classic internet banking.

question: Can I use Garmin Pay Wallet if I report my ASB Visa card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions?
answer:
No, when you call ASB to report your card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions on it, your physical card will be deactivated, the card will be removed from your Garmin Pay Wallet, and a replacement card will be posted.
Once you receive the replacement card in the mail, you need to add the card details into your Garmin Pay Wallet via the Garmin Connect Mobile App before you can start using your Garmin Pay Wallet again.

question: Can I setup Apple Pay or Google Pay while I wait for my physical card to arrive?
answer:
Yes, you can, here's how:
Open the ASB Mobile Banking app and tap 'More' at the bottom right of the screen.
Next select 'Cards & devices'.
Choose a card that you wish to add.
Tap 'Add to Apple wallet’ / ‘Add to Google Pay’
Follow the prompts to complete the process.
Please note this process is only for ASB Mobile app. To load your card directly into Apple or Google wallet you will need to wait for your physical card.
Note:
Transactions over $200 may require a PIN. If so, you'll need to wait for the physical card.

question: How do I make a payment with Google Pay in a store?
answer:
You can pay with Google Pay in any store that accepts contactless payments. It works just like a contactless ASB Visa card, and you will still be prompted to enter your card PIN for payments over $200.
Simply wake and unlock your phone and hold it up to the contactless terminal.
Your phone will beep or vibrate, and the terminal screen will show your payment has been successful.
Just look for the Google Pay or contactless icons when you go to pay.

question: Can I use Google Pay if I report my ASB Visa card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using Google Pay from the next day.
If you currently use Google Pay, when you call ASB to report your card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions on it, your physical card will be deactivated, the card will be removed from Google Wallet, and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated on your ASB Mobile Banking app overnight and you will first need to add the card into Google Pay again via the ASB Mobile Banking app to use Google Pay on your device from around 5 am the next day.

question: Can I use Google Pay if I report my ASB Visa card as lost?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using Google Pay from the next day.
If you currently use Google Pay, when you call ASB to report your physical card as lost, your physical card will be deactivated, and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated on your Google Wallet overnight and you will be able to use Google Pay on your device from around 5 am the next day. Your Device or Virtual Account Number will remain the same.
You can also
lock and replace your ASB Visa card
using ASB Card Control in the ASB Mobile Banking app or
replace your card
using FastNet Classic internet banking.

question: My card has been replaced, can I still pay with my mobile or watch?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using your mobile or watch for contactless payments even if your ASB Visa card is replaced.
Card replaced as it was damaged:
You can seamlessly continue using your mobile or watch without any interruptions.
Card replaced as it was Lost:
You can continue using your mobile or watch from the next day. Your mobile or watch will update with the new card details overnight, and you can resume contactless payments from around 5 am the next morning.
Card replaced as it was stolen or due to fraud:
You can continue using your mobile or watch from the next day. The ASB Mobile Banking app will update overnight, and you need to add the card to Apple Pay or Google Pay via the ASB Mobile Banking app. It will be ready for use around 5 am the following morning.
Note:
Transactions over $200 may require a PIN. If so, you'll need to wait for the physical card.

question: Can I use Apple Pay if I report my ASB Visa card as lost?
answer:
Yes, you can continue using Apple Pay from the next day.
If you currently use Apple Pay, when you call ASB to report your physical card as lost, your physical card will be deactivated, and a replacement card will be posted.
Your replacement card details will be updated on your Apple Wallet overnight and you will be able to use Apple Pay on your device from around 5 am the next day. Your device account number will remain the same.
You can also
lock and replace your ASB Visa card
using ASB Card Control in the ASB Mobile Banking app or
replace your card
using FastNet Classic internet banking.

question: Can I use Google Wallet on my Fitbit device if I report my ASB Visa card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions?
answer:
No, when you call ASB to report your card as stolen or has had fraudulent transactions on it, your physical card will be deactivated, the card will be removed from Google Wallet, and a replacement card will be posted.
Once you receive the replacement card in the mail, you need to add the card details into Google Wallet via the Fitbit Mobile App before you can start using Google Wallet on the Fitbit device again.

question: Why do I need Google Wallet for my FitBit?
answer:
From 29 July 2024
*
, Fitbit Pay will no longer be available. Google Wallet is now available on Fitbit devices that support contactless payments. Set up Google Wallet in the Fitbit app (version 4.14.1 or higher) to continue making contactless payments using your Fitbit device.
Getting started with Google Wallet on your Fitbit device is simple:
Open the Google Wallet app on your watch and tap 'Get started".
Then, open the Fitbit app on your phone and follow the instructions.
Access your card in the Google Wallet app on your wrist for a speedy and secure checkout.
For more information on this change, please visit the
Fitbit website
.
*Note:
If you're located in Saudi Arabia, Japan, or Taiwan, your device will continue to use Fitbit Pay past July 29.

question: What happens to Google Pay if my phone is lost or stolen?
answer:
Your Google Pay information can also be removed remotely so it can’t be accessed by anyone. Google provides a service called
Android Device Manager
which helps find, locate, and erase a phone if it’s permanently lost or stolen.
Alternatively, you can remove or suspend Google Pay by calling the ASB Contact Centre on 0800 803 804 or call collect from overseas on +64 9 306 3000. Your physical card will continue to work as normal.
If you find your device, you can easily re-add your card to Google Pay through the ASB Mobile Banking app or by calling the ASB Contact Centre.
We also have this useful guide to help you.
What do I do if my device has been lost or stolen?

question: I’m a merchant, will customers need to enter a PIN when paying with Google Pay?
answer: Yes for transactions over $200, customers will need to enter their card PIN on the payment terminal when using Google Pay in store.

question: Do I need my PIN when I use Fitbit Pay?
answer:
Fitbit Pay works like all contactless Visa Debit and Visa credit cards, so there's no need to enter a PIN when paying for things under $200. Payments over $200 will require you to enter your card PIN at the terminal.
There may be instances where you won’t be prompted to enter your card PIN for payments over $200, depending on the terminal being used. This is because Fitbit has added security which requires you to enter a security code every 24 hours or when the Fitbit has been removed from your wrist.

question: What if my ASB PayTag is lost or stolen?
answer:
ASB has removed support for ASB PayTag on 30 June 2020, however if your ASB PayTag has been lost or stolen, lock your card using
ASB Card Control
. To do this:
Log into the ASB Mobile Banking app.
Tap 'More' at the bottom right of the accounts screen.
Tap 'Cards & devices'.
Choose your PayTag, then scroll down and tap 'Temporarily lock card'.
If you don’t have ASB FastNet Classic Internet banking you will need to call
0800 255 382
or notify your nearest
ASB branch
and we will cancel your ASB PayTag straight away. If you are overseas, call us collect through the local operator to +64 9 306 3000.

question: Why do I sometimes need to enter my PIN and other times I don’t when using Google Pay?
answer: Depending on the type of ETFPOS terminal used, when making purchases over $200, some will require you to enter your four digit PIN whilst others won’t as they can verify your identify via biometrics held in the phone (e.g. thumb or face recognition). This is completely normal and is dependent on the varying technology found within different ETFPOS terminals at merchants. Rest assured Google Pay remains the secure way to pay using biometrics in order to enable transactions.

question: Where can I use Google Pay?
answer:
You can use Google Pay in thousands of places – in-store, online and in-app, simply look for the Google Pay or contactless icons when you go to pay.
You can find out more about where to use Google Pay on the
Google Pay website
.

question: I’m a merchant, how do merchants process refunds with Google Pay?
answer:
Refunds for a Google Pay purchase are processed in a similar way to traditional refund. It is important that refunds are processed on the same card used as the original transaction.
Merchants will need to obtain the Device or Virtual Account Number to find the purchase and process the return.
To see the last four or five digits of the Device or Virtual Account Number, ask the customer to go to Google Pay Wallet, on the payments tab, tap the card.
Scroll down and you will see the Device or Virtual Account Number listed.
Merchants can also have the customer hold their phone​ near the reader, select the card they used to make the original payment, and authorise the return with their passcode.

question: I’m a merchant, what is a Device or Virtual Account Number and will it show on the receipt?
answer:
Instead of using a card number, Google Pay assigns a unique Device or Virtual Account Number (Token). When a customer makes a purchase, this number, along with a transaction-specific code, is used to process the payment – meaning the merchant will never see card details.
The last four digits of the Device or Virtual Account Number will appear on customer receipts instead of the last four digits of the card number.

question: I’m a merchant, how do customers authorise a payment?
answer: When a customer makes a payment, they will authorise the payment via waking and unlocking their phone and in some instances using their device passcode. The terminal will indicate if the transaction has been successful.

question: I’m a merchant, how can merchants let their customers know they can pay with Google Pay?
answer:
The Google Pay payment mark can be used in websites, emails, online display ads, terminals, digital displays, and in-store to let your customers know that the merchant accepts Google Pay.
The Google Pay digital payment mark artwork and guidelines for usage can be downloaded from the
Google Pay website
.

question: I’m a merchant, are Google Pay Payments charged as card present or card not present?
answer: Payments made using Google Pay in stores are charged as card present transactions. Payments made using Google Pay within apps are not categorised as card present transactions.

question: Do I still earn True Rewards dollars when I use Google Pay?
answer: Yes, you will continue to receive all of the same benefits, rewards, and security your eligible ASB card offers when you pay with Google Pay.

question: What happens if I have multiple Google accounts?
answer: If you have more than one Google account, you will be prompted to select one when you start the process of adding your card to Google Pay. Depending on which account you select, you may experience a delay whilst Google is setting up the Google Pay wallet on your device. This is normal and will proceed to the next stage shortly.

question: What should I do if I can't set up Google Pay?
answer:
There are a few reasons why you might not be able to set up Google Pay. You need to ensure your device is NFC compatible and that this feature is switched on. If you were previously using ASB Virtual you will need to ensure your default payment method has been switched to Google Pay. This can be done through your device Settings menu by searching ‘Tap and Pay’ to ensure your default payment services is set to Google Pay.
For detailed information, take a look at the
Google Pay help website
.

question: How does Google Pay keep my payment information safe?
answer:
Your card details are never stored on the phone, and payment information is encrypted and stored on secure Google servers. For more information, you can visit the
Google Pay help website
​.

question: Which ASB cards can I use with Fitbit Pay?
answer: You can use all ASB Visa Debit and ASB Visa credit cards to make payments using your Fitbit Ionic or Fitbit Versa. Check the specs of your Fitbit to see if it supports Fitbit Pay.

question: How do I remove an ASB Visa card from my Fitbit Pay?
answer:
To remove an ASB Visa card from Fitbit Pay:
On Fitbit app dashboard, tap or click the Account icon.
Tap or click your watch tile.
Tap or click the Wallet tile.
Swipe to find the card you want to remove.
Scroll down and find the option to remove it. The card will disappear from the Wallet.
If the card is stored on your watch, sync your watch to remove it.

question: How do I suspend my ASB Visa card from Fitbit Pay?
answer:
To suspend an ASB Visa card from Fitbit Pay:
On Fitbit app dashboard, tap or click the Account icon.
Tap or click your watch tile.
Tap or click your Wallet.
Swipe to find the card you want to suspend.
Tap or click Suspend Card.
To reactivate the card on your watch, complete these steps and tap or click 'Unsuspend Card'.

question: Where can I use Fitbit Pay?
answer:
You can use Fitbit Pay at any store that accepts contactless payments. Look for this symbol on the payment terminal at the store check-out.
For overseas transactions the limits and ability to use contactless will vary depending on which country you are travelling in. It is always a good idea to take more than one form of payment when travelling.
Additional fees
will apply to overseas transactions made using your ASB Visa card.

question: How do I activate Fitbit Pay?
answer:
When you load your ASB Visa cards into Fitbit Pay we will send you a one-time code to verify the set-up. Enter this code into the Fitbit app, then you'll be ready to go.
You'll need to be
Netcode
registered to receive the one-time code. If you're not registered for Netcode, you can contact ASB Contact Centre on 0800 803 804 and we'll be able to activate for you.

question: What do I do if I've lost or misplaced my Fitbit watch?
answer:
The Fitbit watch has its own PIN code as a layer of protection. But for added security, you can suspend the card on your watch in the Wallet section of the Fitbit app (even if your watch isn't nearby).
When you suspend a card, it remains stored in your Wallet, but you can’t use it to make payments with your watch. You can also suspend or resume using Fitbit Pay through the ASB Contact Centre by calling 0800 803 804.

question: Do I need a special Access Code and Password to access my Online Share Trading account?
answer:
Yes. In order to access to your financial information and transact online using ASB Securities Online Share Trading service, you will need to enter a unique Access Code and Password of your choosing. This Access Code and Password should be known only by you.
What can I do to protect my Online Share Trading account security?
Choose a Password that you will remember but cannot be easily guessed, and keep it to yourself.  When you use your Password to Log In, take care others cannot see your Password being entered and change it regularly, we suggest every 3 months. If you believe that your Password has been compromised, change it immediately.
What if I forget my Password?
If you forget your Password simply call us on
0800 327 863
. A customer services representative will ask you some security questions and then provide you with a temporary Password to give you access to your accounts.

question: What is FMCA?
answer:
The Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 (FMCA) is a piece of law that governs how financial products are created, promoted and sold. The Financial Markets Conduct Bill was introduced into Parliament in October 2011 and passed into law in September 2013. It replaces the Securities Act 1978, Securities Markets Act 1988, and incorporates and amends a range of other related investment legislation.
The Financial Services Legislation Amendment Act (FSLAA) is in effect from 15 March 2021. FSLAA will bring the regulation of financial advice into FMCA.
New disclosure regulations change the information ASB is required to disclose to our customers.
See our
Advice Disclosure Statement
for more information.
Find out more about the Act at
www.fma.govt.nz
.

question: What happens if I don’t re-fix my home loan rate before my current fixed rate rollover (FRR) expires?
answer:
Your home loan rate will automatically switch to ASB's variable rate as advertised on
asb.co.nz
. The personalised rates offered will be available for 3 months after your current fixed rate expires. Rates are subject to change.
Please note that Early Repayment Adjustments (ERAs) may apply if you break or amend your loan after selecting a fixed interest rate. Please contact your lending specialist to discuss, or call us on 0800 10 600.

question: How much equity do I need to take out a home loan top up?
answer:
If you're an owner-occupier, you'll usually need more than 20% equity in your property to be eligible for a home loan top up. If you have less than 20% equity in your property, you may be referred to our credit team. They will assess your application to see if you could qualify at a higher interest rate.
You can check how much you may be able to afford to top up your home loan using our
home loan borrowing calculator
.

question: How do I know that my home loan re-fix request has been received?
answer:
A confirmation letter will be added to your document centre in FastNet Classic internet banking 24 hours after you’ve re-fixed your home loan.
To view your documents:
Log in to FastNet Classic internet banking.
Open the menu in the top left of the screen.
On the right-hand side, select 'Documents'.

question: What does the home loan rate promise mean?
What is a home loan pre-approval?
answer:
A home loan pre-approval is a conditional approval that we can lend you a certain amount of money, provided the property you purchase meets ASB's home loan criteria. It's a good idea to ask us for a home loan pre-approval before you start house hunting. It will help you determine how much you can borrow and to give you some bargaining power when negotiating a purchase price.
ASB’s home loan pre-approvals are valid for up to 90 days from the date of issue.

question: How do I change my expiring fixed rate home loan to become a variable rate loan?
answer: Upon expiry date of your fixed rate home loan, it will automatically move to a variable rate home loan. There’s nothing more you’ll need to do.

question: What if home loan rates go down after I choose to re-fix?
answer: If you choose to re-fix your home loan online at personalised rates before the expiry date of your current fixed interest rate term and rates for the same term decrease before the new rate is fixed, we will apply the lower of the two rates.

answer: Yes. You will be able to re-fix your home loan through Fastnet Classic internet banking. Select the loan that is expiring and follow the on screen prompts.

question: Can I change my home loan term before it expires?
answer:
Before accepting a new home loan term you will have the ability to make changes to your loan. Once you’ve agreed to a new term and rate and you want to change your loan term or rate, an Early Repayment Adjustment may apply.
Find out more information on
managing your home loan

question: Can I fix my interest rate on a progress payment loan?
answer:
Yes it is possible to fix and interest rate on a progress payment loan but this can only be done once the entire balance of the loan has been drawn down in full. Progress payments loans are often used to fund construction as they enable you to draw down your loan in instalments over the first 12 months of the loan term.
They are structured on a variable (floating) interest rate, during the draw down period, and repayments of ‘interest only’ are made on the loan balance until the approved loan amount is fully drawn down.
Only once the loan amount has been fully drawn down can you then change to a fixed interest rate.
See our guide
Building your new home
for more information.

question: How do I know which Early Repayment Adjustment (ERA) calculation method applies to my loan?
answer:
Generally loans documented before 1 April 2015 use the Credit Contracts and Consumer Finance Act (CCCFA) Method and loans documented from 1 April 2015 use the Wholesale Rate Method. If you are unsure, please refer to your loan Terms & Conditions or speak with a lending specialist on
0800 100 600
.

question: My fixed rate loan is about to expire, what will happen and do I need to do anything?
answer:
You have a number of options when the fixed rate period ends:
You can do nothing, your loan will automatically revert to our standard variable interest rate and we'll write to tell you the amount of your minimum monthly or fortnightly repayment; or
You can choose another fixed interest rate period to begin immediately after your current term expires; or
You can review your loan structure. For example, if interest rates increase, you may be able to extend your loan term (total term of loan cannot exceed 30 years) to keep your repayments the same. If interest rates reduce, you may be able to pay off your loan faster by keeping your repayments the same; and
You can split your loan across a variable and one or more fixed interest rate periods.
We will contact you well before the expiry of your current fixed term. If you would like to talk to a Lending Specialist about these options, simply call 0800 100 600, or visit your nearest
ASB branch
. If you're an ASB business customer, you can also contact your Relationship Manager to find out more.

question: Can I break or switch my loan’s fixed interest rate at any time?
answer:
Yes. You can choose to break or switch your loan’s fixed interest rate at any time however you may be charged an early repayment adjustment to do so.
Subject to when your loan was documented, an early repayment adjustment (ERA) is charged when:
the fixed interest rate (on the loan being repaid or switched to a different interest rate) is higher than the current interest rate ASB can re-lend the money for the remainder of the fixed interest rate period
OR
there is a difference between the wholesale interest rate at the start of your fixed interest rate period and the wholesale interest rate at the date of your early repayment (on the loan being repaid or switched to a different interest rate) which incurs a loss for the bank.
ERAs are charged because when you choose to fix your loan’s interest rate, ASB also enters into loans from other sources (i.e. depositors, wholesale money markets, other banks and through the issuing of bonds) for the same fixed rate period to fund your loan. This is how we manage the risk of interest rate fluctuations. When you decide to switch your interest rate or repay your loan early, either partially or in full, ASB is still required to honour its commitment to the funding it has borrowed for the remainder of its fixed interest rate period and may incur a loss. ASB will charge you an ERA to help recover this loss.
For more details follow
this guide
.
As with any important financial decisions, we encourage you to talk to us about your options. To get an ERA quote or find out more about your options, call us on 0800 100 600.
